revision.comment: Auto generating page. date placed far in the past so it is attributed to Immanuelle and FireflyBot sends them messages if it is ever imported to Wikipedia.

revision.comment: [[WP:AES|←]]新しいページ: '{{記事名の制約|title=洲崎濱宮神明神社}} {{神社 |名称 = 洲崎濱宮神明神社 |画像 = [[ファイル:Image-request.png|100px]]</br>画像募...'
revision.text:
{{記事名の制約|title=洲崎濱宮神明神社}} {{神社 |名称 = 洲崎濱宮神明神社 |画像 = [[ファイル:Image-request.png|100px]]</br>画像募集中 |所在地 = 三重県四日市市海山道町1-62 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|56|34|N|136|37|18|E|scale:5000}} |祭神 = 天照大神 |社格 = |創建 = 876年 |本殿 = |別名 = 海山道神社 |例祭 = 10月3日～5日 |神事 = 狐の嫁入り神事 |}} '''洲崎濱宮神明神社'''（すざきはまみやしんめいじんじゃ）は[[三重県]][[四日市市]]海山道（みやまど）にある[[神社]]である。
境内社である'''海山道稲荷神社'''（みやまどいなりじんじゃ）の方が大きく、「[[狐の嫁入り]]神事」で知られる。 境内には多くの稲荷社や天神社などもあり、総称して「'''海山道神社'''」（みやまどじんじゃ）とも呼ばれている。
==祭神== *神明神社：[[天照大神]]、他 *稲荷神社：[[ウカノミタマ|倉稲魂神]]、他
==概要== [[876年]]創建と伝えられる。[[平安時代]]、当地は[[海浜]]で洲崎を成していたことから、古来「浜の宮」と呼ばれていた。 稲荷神社の勧請時期は不詳。
==社殿・境内== 境内社に海山道稲荷神社があり、その規模は神明社を凌ぐ。 境内末社は稲荷社だけでも20社以上のほか、日本一の大きさの[[菅原道真]]像を祀る天神菅原社や、筆塚、眼鏡塚、狐塚など多くある。
==祭事・年中行事== *例大祭：10月3～5日 *稲荷大祭：4月8日～9日 *狐の嫁入り神事：[[節分]]の日に行われる稲荷神社の[[厄祓い]]行事。 *:境内の福徳稲荷社の娘狐が、同じ境内の助四郎稲荷社へ嫁入りするという設定で行われる。その年の[[厄年]]の男女が狐の新郎新婦に扮し、[[福引]]券入りの[[節分#豆撒き|福豆まき]]も行われる。
==所在地・交通== 三重県四日市市海山道町1-62 *[[近畿日本鉄道]][[近鉄名古屋線|名古屋線]][[海山道駅]]下車すぐ。
==関連項目== *[[神明神社]] *[[稲荷神]]
{{shinto-stub}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:すさきはまみやしんめいしんしや}} [[Category:三重県の神社]] [[Category:四日市市]] [[Category:神明神社]]

{{記事名の制約|title=洲崎濱宮神明神社}} {{神社 |名称 = 洲崎濱宮神明神社 |画像 = [[ファイル:Gthumb.svg|100px]]
画像募集中 |所在地 = 三重県四日市市海山道町1-62 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|56|34|N|136|37|18|E|scale:5000}} |祭神 = 天照大神 |社格 = |創建 = 876年 |本殿 = |別名 = 海山道神社 |例祭 = 10月3日～5日 |神事 = 狐の嫁入り神事 |}} '''洲崎濱宮神明神社'''（すざきはまみやしんめいじんじゃ）は[[三重県]][[四日市市]]海山道（みやまど）にある[[神社]]である。 境内社である'''海山道稲荷神社'''（みやまどいなりじんじゃ）の方が大きく、「[[狐の嫁入り]]神事」で知られる。 境内には多くの稲荷社や天神社などもあり、総称して「'''海山道神社'''」（みやまどじんじゃ）とも呼ばれている。 == 祭神 == * 神明神社：[[天照大神]]、他 * 稲荷神社：[[ウカノミタマ|倉稲魂神]]、他 == 概要 == [[876年]]創建と伝えられる。[[平安時代]]、当地は[[海浜]]で洲崎を成していたことから、古来「浜の宮」と呼ばれていた。 稲荷神社の勧請時期は不詳。 == 社殿・境内 == 境内社に海山道稲荷神社があり、その規模は神明社を凌ぐ。 境内末社は稲荷社だけでも20社以上のほか、日本一の大きさの[[菅原道真]]像を祀る天神菅原社や、筆塚、眼鏡塚、狐塚など多くある。 == 祭事・年中行事 == * 例大祭：10月3～5日 * 稲荷大祭：4月8日～9日 * 狐の嫁入り神事：[[節分]]の日に行われる稲荷神社の[[厄祓い]]行事。 *: 境内の福徳稲荷社の娘狐が、同じ境内の助四郎稲荷社へ嫁入りするという設定で行われる。その年の[[厄年]]の男女が狐の新郎新婦に扮し、[[福引]]券入りの[[節分#豆撒き|福豆まき]]も行われる。 == 所在地・交通 == 三重県四日市市海山道町1-62 * [[近畿日本鉄道]][[近鉄名古屋線|名古屋線]][[海山道駅]]下車すぐ。 == 関連項目 == * [[神明神社]] * [[稲荷神]] {{shinto-stub}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:すさきはまみやしんめいしんしや}} [[Category:三重県の神社]] [[Category:四日市市]] [[Category:神明神社]]

{{記事名の制約|title=洲崎濱宮神明神社}} {{神社 |名称 = 洲崎濱宮神明神社 |画像 = [[File:Suzakihamamiya(Mie).JPG|260px]] |所在地 = 三重県四日市市海山道町1-62 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|56|34|N|136|37|18|E|type:landmark_region:JP-24|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = 天照大神 |社格 = |創建 = 876年 |本殿 = |別名 = 海山道神社 |例祭 = 10月3日 - 10月5日 |神事 = 狐の嫁入り神事 }} '''洲崎濱宮神明神社'''（すざきはまみやしんめいじんじゃ）は[[三重県]][[四日市市]]海山道（みやまど）にある[[神社]]である。
境内社である'''海山道稲荷神社'''（みやまどいなりじんじゃ）の方が大きく、「[[狐の嫁入り]]神事」で知られる。 境内には多くの稲荷社や天神社などもあり、総称して「'''海山道神社'''」（みやまどじんじゃ）とも呼ばれている。
== 祭神 == * 神明神社：[[天照大神]]、他 * 稲荷神社：[[ウカノミタマ|倉稲魂神]]、他
== 概要 == [[876年]]（[[貞観 (日本)|貞観]]18年）創建と伝えられる。[[平安時代]]、当地は[[海浜]]で洲崎を成していたことから、古来「浜の宮」と呼ばれていた。 稲荷神社の勧請時期は不詳。
== 社殿・境内 == [[File:Miyamado-inari sandou(Mie).JPG|thumb|海山道稲荷神社]] 境内社に海山道稲荷神社があり、その規模は神明社を凌ぐ。 境内末社は稲荷社だけでも20社以上のほか、日本一の大きさの[[菅原道真]]像を祀る天神菅原社や、筆塚、眼鏡塚、狐塚など多くある。
== 祭事・年中行事 == * 例大祭：10月3 -10月5日 * 稲荷大祭：4月8日 -4月9日 * 狐の嫁入り神事：[[節分]]の日に行われる稲荷神社の[[厄祓い]]行事。 *: 境内の福徳稲荷社の娘狐が、同じ境内の助四郎稲荷社へ嫁入りするという設定で行われる。その年の[[厄年]]の男女が狐の新郎新婦に扮し、[[福引]]券入りの[[節分#豆撒き|福豆まき]]も行われる。
== 所在地・交通 == 三重県四日市市海山道町1-62 * [[近畿日本鉄道]][[近鉄名古屋線|名古屋線]] [[海山道駅]]下車すぐ。

== 社殿・境内 == [[File:Miyamado-inari(Mie).JPG|thumb|海山道稲荷神社]] 境内社に海山道稲荷神社があり、その規模は神明社を凌ぐ。 境内末社は稲荷社だけでも20社以上のほか、日本一の大きさの[[菅原道真]]像を祀る天神菅原社や、筆塚、眼鏡塚、狐塚など多くある。
== 祭事・年中行事 == * 例大祭：10月3 -10月5日 * 稲荷大祭：4月8日 -4月9日 * 狐の嫁入り神事：[[節分]]の日に行われる稲荷神社の[[厄祓い]]行事。 *: 境内の福徳稲荷社の娘狐が、同じ境内の助四郎稲荷社へ嫁入りするという設定で行われる。その年の[[厄年]]の男女が狐の新郎新婦に扮し、[[福引]]券入りの[[節分#豆撒き|福豆まき]]も行われる。
== 所在地・交通 == 三重県四日市市海山道町1-62 * [[近畿日本鉄道]][[近鉄名古屋線|名古屋線]] [[海山道駅]]下車すぐ。
== 関連項目 == {{Commonscat|Miyamado-jinja|海山道神社}} * [[神明神社]] * [[稲荷神]]
{{神道 横}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:すさきはまみやしんめいしんしや}} [[Category:三重県の神社]] [[Category:四日市市の歴史]] [[Category:四日市市の建築物]] [[Category:神明神社]]

== 社殿・境内 == 境内社に海山道稲荷神社があり、その規模は神明社を凌ぐ。
境内末社は稲荷社だけでも20社以上のほか、日本一の大きさの[[菅原道真]]像を祀る天神菅原社や、筆塚、眼鏡塚、狐塚など多くある。
== 祭事・年中行事 == * 例大祭：10月3 - 10月5日 * 稲荷大祭：4月8日 - 4月9日 * 狐の嫁入り神事：[[節分]]の日に行われる稲荷神社の[[厄祓い]]行事。 ** 境内の福徳稲荷社の娘狐が、同じ境内の助四郎稲荷社へ嫁入りするという設定で行われる。その年の[[厄年]]の男女が狐の新郎新婦に扮し、[[福引]]券入りの[[節分#豆まき|福豆まき]]も行われる。
== 所在地 == * [[三重県]][[四日市市]]海山道町1-62
== 交通 == * [[近畿日本鉄道|近鉄]][[近鉄名古屋線|名古屋線]] [[海山道駅]]下車、徒歩ですぐ。
== 関連項目 == {{Commonscat|Miyamado-jinja|海山道神社}} * [[神明神社]] * [[稲荷神]]
{{神道 横}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:すさきはまみやしんめいしんしや}} [[Category:三重県の神社]] [[Category:四日市市の歴史]] [[Category:四日市市の建築物]] [[Category:神明神社]]

revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building | name = Suzaki Hamamiya Shinmei Shrine | image = [[File:Suzakihamamiya(Mie).JPG|250px]] | location = 1-62 Miyamado Town, Yokkaichi, Mie Prefecture, Japan | latitude = 34.9428 | longitude = 136.6217 | deity = [[Amaterasu Ōmikami]] and others | founded = [[876]] (18th year of the [[Jōgan era]]) | main_building_style = | festivals = October 3–5 (Annual Festival); February (Fox Wedding Ceremony) | map_type = Japan Mie }}
'''Suzaki Hamamiya Shinmei Shrine''' ({{ill|Suzaki Hamamiya Shinmei Shrine|ja|洲崎濱宮神明神社}}, ''Suzaki Hamamiya Shinmei Jinja'') is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in the [[Shiobama District]], [[Yokkaichi]], [[Mie Prefecture]], Japan. It is often collectively referred to as "Miyamado Shrine" due to the large presence of its [[Inari Shrine|Inari shrines]].
== Overview == The shrine's name "濱" (''Hama'') is also written as "浜" or "濵."
The subordinate shrine, known as '''Miyamado Inari Shrine''', is larger than the main Shinmei Shrine and is famous for the "Fox Wedding Ceremony" (狐の嫁入り神事).
The grounds also include multiple Inari shrines, Tenjin shrines, and unique features such as Japan’s largest statue of [[Sugawara no Michizane]].
== Enshrined Deities == === Shinmei Shrine === {{Div col}} * [[Amenokoyane-no-Mikoto]] * [[Amaterasu Ōmikami]] * [[Takemikazuchi-no-Mikoto]] * [[Nintoku Tennō]] * [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] * [[Ukanomitama-no-Kami]] * [[Ōyamakui-no-Kami]] * [[Kagutsuchi-no-Kami]] * [[Sugawara no Michizane]] * [[Ho-musubi-no-Kami]] * [[Ukemochi-no-Kami]] * [[Yamatohime-no-Mikoto]] {{Div col end}}
=== Inari Shrine === * [[Ukanomitama-no-Kami]] * [[Ōkuninushi-no-Kami]] * [[Ōta-no-Kami]] * [[Ukemochi-no-Kami]] * [[Ōmiyanome-no-Kami]]
== History == The shrine is traditionally said to have been founded in [[876]] (the 18th year of the [[Jōgan era]]). During the [[Heian period]], the area was coastal and formed a sandbar called "Suzaki." Thus, the shrine became known as "Hama-no-Miya" (Shrine of the Beach).
The exact date of the establishment of the Inari Shrine is unknown.
== Shrine Buildings and Grounds == The grounds include the Miyamado Inari Shrine, which surpasses the Shinmei Shrine in scale.
There are over 20 subsidiary Inari shrines, a Tenjin Sugawara Shrine housing Japan’s largest statue of [[Sugawara no Michizane]], and various monuments such as the Pen Mound, Eyeglass Mound, and Fox Mound.
== Festivals and Events == * Annual Festival: October 3–5 * Inari Grand Festival: April 8–9 * Fox Wedding Ceremony: Held on [[Setsubun]] (February), this purification ritual involves a mock wedding procession. Participants representing the bride and groom wear fox masks, and a "bean-throwing" event is also held.
== Location == 1-62 Miyamado Town, [[Yokkaichi]], [[Mie Prefecture]], Japan
== Access == * A short walk from [[Kintetsu Nagoya Line]]'s [[Miyamado Station]].
== Related Topics == {{Commonscat|Miyamado-jinja|Miyamado Shrine}} * [[Shinmei Shrine]] * [[Inari Shrine]]
{{Shinto-stub}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Yokkaichi]] [[Category:Shinmei shrines]] [[Category:Inari shrines]] [[ja:洲崎濱宮神明神社]]

=== Inari Shrine === * {{ill|Ukanomitama-no-Kami|ja|倉稲魂命}} * {{ill|Ōkuninushi-no-Kami|ja|大己貴命}} * {{ill|Ōta-no-Kami|ja|大田命}} * {{ill|Ukemochi-no-Kami|ja|保食神}} * {{ill|Ōmiyanome-no-Kami|ja|大宮能売神}}
== History == The shrine is traditionally said to have been founded in [[876]] (the 18th year of the {{ill|Jōgan era|ja|貞観}}). During the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}, the area was coastal and formed a sandbar called "Suzaki." Thus, the shrine became known as "Hama-no-Miya" (Shrine of the Beach).
The exact date of the establishment of the {{ill|Inari Shrine|ja|稲荷神社}} is unknown.

The exact date of the establishment of the {{ill|Inari Shrine|ja|稲荷神社}} is unknown.
== Shrine Buildings and Grounds == The grounds include the {{ill|Miyamado Inari Shrine|ja|海山道稲荷神社}}, which surpasses the Shinmei Shrine in scale.
There are over 20 subsidiary {{ill|Inari shrines|ja|稲荷神社}}, a {{ill|Tenjin shrine|ja|天神社}} housing Japan’s largest statue of {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}}, and various monuments such as the Pen Mound, Eyeglass Mound, and Fox Mound.
== Festivals and Events == * Annual Festival: October 3–5 * Inari Grand Festival: April 8–9 * Fox Wedding Ceremony: Held on {{ill|Setsubun|ja|節分}} (February), this purification ritual involves a mock wedding procession. Participants representing the bride and groom wear fox masks, and a "bean-throwing" event is also held.
== location == 1-62 Miyamado Town, [[Yokkaichi]], [[Mie Prefecture]], Japan
== Access == * A short walk from [[Kintetsu Nagoya Line]]'s [[Miyamado Station]].

{{Infobox religious building | name = Suzaki Hamamiya Shinmei Shrine | image = [[File:Suzakihamamiya(Mie).JPG|250px]] | location = 1-62 Miyamado Town, Yokkaichi, Mie Prefecture, Japan | latitude = 34.9428 | longitude = 136.6217 | deity = {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神}} and others | founded = [[876]] (18th year of the {{ill|Jōgan era|ja|貞観}}) | main_building_style = | festivals = October 3–5 (Annual Festival); February (Fox Wedding Ceremony) | map_type = Japan Mie }}

{{Infobox religious building | name = Suzaki Hamamiya Shinmei Shrine | image = [[File:Suzakihamamiya(Mie).JPG|250px]] | location = 1-62 Miyamado Town, Yokkaichi, Mie Prefecture, Japan | latitude = 34.9428 | longitude = 136.6217 | deity = {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神}} and others | founded = [[876]] (18th year of the {{ill|Jōgan era|ja|貞観}}) | main_building_style = | festivals = October 3–5 (Annual Festival); February (Fox Wedding Ceremony) | map_type = Japan Mie }}
'''Suzaki Hamamiya Shinmei Shrine''' ({{ill|Suzaki Hamamiya Shinmei Shrine|ja|洲崎濱宮神明神社}}, ''Suzaki Hamamiya Shinmei Jinja'') is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in the [[Shiobama District]], [[Yokkaichi]], [[Mie Prefecture]], Japan. It is often collectively referred to as "Miyamado Shrine" due to the large presence of its {{ill|Inari Shrine|ja|稲荷神社}}.
== Overview == The shrine's name "濱" (''Hama'') is also written as "浜" or "濵."
The subordinate shrine, known as '''Miyamado Inari Shrine''' ({{ill|Miyamado Inari Shrine|ja|海山道稲荷神社}}), is larger than the main Shinmei Shrine and is famous for the "Fox Wedding Ceremony" ({{ill|Fox Wedding|ja|狐の嫁入り}}).
The grounds also include multiple Inari shrines, {{ill|Tenjin shrines|ja|天神社}}, and unique features such as Japan’s largest statue of {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}}.
== Enshrined Deities == === Shinmei Shrine === {{Div col}} * {{ill|Amenokoyane-no-Mikoto|ja|天児屋根命}} * {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神}} * {{ill|Takemikazuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|建御雷命}} * {{ill|Nintoku Tennō|ja|仁徳天皇}} * {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚尊}} * {{ill|Ukanomitama-no-Kami|ja|倉稲魂命}} * {{ill|Ōyamakui-no-Kami|ja|大山祇神}} * {{ill|Kagutsuchi-no-Kami|ja|火之迦具土神}} * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}} * {{ill|Ho-musubi-no-Kami|ja|火産霊神}} * {{ill|Ukemochi-no-Kami|ja|保食神}} * {{ill|Yamatohime-no-Mikoto|ja|倭姫命}} {{Div col end}}
=== Inari Shrine === * {{ill|Ukanomitama-no-Kami|ja|倉稲魂命}} * {{ill|Ōkuninushi-no-Kami|ja|大己貴命}} * {{ill|Ōta-no-Kami|ja|大田命}} * {{ill|Ukemochi-no-Kami|ja|保食神}} * {{ill|Ōmiyanome-no-Kami|ja|大宮能売神}}
== History == The shrine is traditionally said to have been founded in [[876]] (the 18th year of the {{ill|Jōgan era|ja|貞観}}). During the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}, the area was coastal and formed a sandbar called "Suzaki." Thus, the shrine became known as "Hama-no-Miya" (Shrine of the Beach).
The exact date of the establishment of the {{ill|Inari Shrine|ja|稲荷神社}} is unknown.
== Shrine Buildings and Grounds == The grounds include the {{ill|Miyamado Inari Shrine|ja|海山道稲荷神社}}, which surpasses the Shinmei Shrine in scale.
There are over 20 subsidiary {{ill|Inari shrines|ja|稲荷神社}}, a {{ill|Tenjin shrine|ja|天神社}} housing Japan’s largest statue of {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}}, and various monuments such as the Pen Mound, Eyeglass Mound, and Fox Mound.
== Festivals and Events == * Annual Festival: October 3–5 * Inari Grand Festival: April 8–9 * Fox Wedding Ceremony: Held on {{ill|Setsubun|ja|節分}} (February), this purification ritual involves a mock wedding procession. Participants representing the bride and groom wear fox masks, and a "bean-throwing" event is also held.
== location == 1-62 Miyamado Town, [[Yokkaichi]], [[Mie Prefecture]], Japan
== Access == * A short walk from [[Kintetsu Nagoya Line]]'s [[Miyamado Station]].
== Related Topics == {{Commonscat|Miyamado-jinja|Miyamado Shrine}} * {{ill|Shinmei Shrine|ja|神明神社}} * {{ill|Inari Shrine|ja|稲荷神社}}

{{Infobox religious building | name = Suzaki Hamamiya Shinmei Shrine | image = [[File:Suzakihamamiya(Mie).JPG|250px]] | location = 1-62 Miyamado Town, Yokkaichi, Mie Prefecture, Japan | latitude = 34.9428 | longitude = 136.6217 | deity = {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神}} and others | founded = [[876]] (18th year of the {{ill|Jōgan era|ja|貞観}}) | main_building_style = | festivals = October 3–5 (Annual Festival); February (Fox Wedding Ceremony) | map_type = Japan Mie }}
'''Suzaki Hamamiya Shinmei Shrine''' ({{ill|Suzaki Hamamiya Shinmei Shrine|ja|洲崎濱宮神明神社}}, ''Suzaki Hamamiya Shinmei Jinja'') is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in the [[Shiobama District]], [[Yokkaichi]], [[Mie Prefecture]], Japan. It is often collectively referred to as "Miyamado Shrine" due to the large presence of its {{ill|Inari Shrine|ja|稲荷神社}}.
== Overview == The shrine's name "濱" (''Hama'') is also written as "浜" or "濵."
The subordinate shrine, known as '''Miyamado Inari Shrine''' ({{ill|Miyamado Inari Shrine|ja|海山道稲荷神社}}), is larger than the main Shinmei Shrine and is famous for the "Fox Wedding Ceremony" ({{ill|Fox Wedding|ja|狐の嫁入り}}).
The grounds also include multiple Inari shrines, {{ill|Tenjin shrines|ja|天神社}}, and unique features such as Japan’s largest statue of {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}}.
== Enshrined Deities == === Shinmei Shrine === {{Div col}} * {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|lt=Amenokoyane-no-Mikoto|ja|天児屋根命}} * {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神}} * {{ill|Takemikazuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|建御雷命}} * {{ill|Nintoku Tennō|ja|仁徳天皇}} * {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚尊}} * {{ill|Ukanomitama-no-Kami|ja|倉稲魂命}} * {{ill|Ōyamakui-no-Kami|ja|大山祇神}} * {{ill|Kagutsuchi-no-Kami|ja|火之迦具土神}} * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}} * {{ill|Ho-musubi-no-Kami|ja|火産霊神}} * {{ill|Ukemochi-no-Kami|ja|保食神}} * {{ill|Yamatohime-no-Mikoto|ja|倭姫命}} {{Div col end}}
=== Inari Shrine === * {{ill|Ukanomitama-no-Kami|ja|倉稲魂命}} * {{ill|Ōkuninushi-no-Kami|ja|大己貴命}} * {{ill|Ōta-no-Kami|ja|大田命}} * {{ill|Ukemochi-no-Kami|ja|保食神}} * {{ill|Ōmiyanome-no-Kami|ja|大宮能売神}}
== History == The shrine is traditionally said to have been founded in [[876]] (the 18th year of the {{ill|Jōgan era|ja|貞観}}). During the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}, the area was coastal and formed a sandbar called "Suzaki." Thus, the shrine became known as "Hama-no-Miya" (Shrine of the Beach).
The exact date of the establishment of the {{ill|Inari Shrine|ja|稲荷神社}} is unknown.
== Shrine Buildings and Grounds == The grounds include the {{ill|Miyamado Inari Shrine|ja|海山道稲荷神社}}, which surpasses the Shinmei Shrine in scale.
There are over 20 subsidiary {{ill|Inari shrines|ja|稲荷神社}}, a {{ill|Tenjin shrine|ja|天神社}} housing Japan’s largest statue of {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}}, and various monuments such as the Pen Mound, Eyeglass Mound, and Fox Mound.
== Festivals and Events == * Annual Festival: October 3–5 * Inari Grand Festival: April 8–9 * Fox Wedding Ceremony: Held on {{ill|Setsubun|ja|節分}} (February), this purification ritual involves a mock wedding procession. Participants representing the bride and groom wear fox masks, and a "bean-throwing" event is also held.
== location == 1-62 Miyamado Town, [[Yokkaichi]], [[Mie Prefecture]], Japan
== Access == * A short walk from [[Kintetsu Nagoya Line]]'s [[Miyamado Station]].
== Related Topics == {{Commonscat|Miyamado-jinja|Miyamado Shrine}} * {{ill|Shinmei Shrine|ja|神明神社}} * {{ill|Inari Shrine|ja|稲荷神社}}

revision.text:
{{Shrine |Name = Suzaki Hamamiya Shinmei Shrine |Image = [[File:Suzakihamamiya(Mie).JPG|250px]] |Location = Mie Prefecture, Yokkaichi City, Miyamado-cho 1-62 |Coordinates = {{Coord|34|56|34|N|136|37|18|E|type:landmark_region:JP-24|display=inline,title}} |Deities = Amaterasu Ōmikami |Rank = |Founded = [[876]] (18th year of [[Jōgan (Japan)|Jōgan]]) |Main hall = |Alias = Miyamado Shrine |Annual festival = October 3 - October 5 |Rituals = Kitsune no Yomeiri Ritual }}
[[File:Miyamado-inari(Mie).JPG|thumb|250px|right|Miyamado Inari Shrine]]
'''Suzaki Hamamiya Shinmei Shrine''' (洲崎濱宮神明神社, Suzaki Hamamiya Shinmei Jinja) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in the Miyamado area of the [[Shiohama District]] in [[Yokkaichi City]], [[Mie Prefecture]].
== Overview == The character 濱 (Hama) can also be written as '''浜''' or '''濵'''.
The shrine's subordinate shrine, '''Miyamado Inari Shrine''' (みやまどいなりじんじゃ), is larger in scale and is known for the "[[Kitsune no Yomeiri]]" (Fox Wedding) ritual.
Within the shrine precincts, there are many Inari shrines, Tenjin shrines, and others. Collectively, they are also referred to as "'''Miyamado Shrine'''" (みやまどじんじゃ).
== Enshrined Deities == === Shinmei Shrine === {{Div col}} * [[Amenomahitotsu no Mikoto|Omotaru no Mikoto]] * [[Amaterasu Ōmikami]] * [[Kashikone no Mikoto]] * [[Emperor Nintoku]] * [[Takehaya Susanoo no Mikoto]] * [[Ukanomitama no Mikoto]] * [[Ōyamatsumi no Mikoto]] * [[Kagutsuchi]] * [[Sugawara no Michizane]] * [[Hi no Kagutsuchi no Kami]] * [[Ukemochi no Kami]] * [[Yamato Hime no Mikoto]] {{Div col end}}
=== Inari Shrine === * Ukanomitama no Mikoto * [[Ōkuninushi no Mikoto]] * [[Ōtanojigami]] * Ukemochi no Kami * [[Ōmiyanome no Kami]]
== Overview == The shrine was founded in [[876]] (18th year of [[Jōgan (Japan)|Jōgan]]) according to tradition. During the [[Heian period]], this area formed a sandbank by the coast and was historically called "Hama no Miya" (Shrine of the Coast).
The time of the Inari Shrine's dedication is unknown.
== Structures and Grounds == The subordinate Miyamado Inari Shrine within the grounds surpasses Shinmei Shrine in scale.
In addition to more than 20 subsidiary Inari shrines, the precinct includes Tenjin Sugawara Shrine, which enshrines a statue of [[Sugawara no Michizane]] reputed to be the largest in Japan. Other features include a calligraphy monument, eyeglass monument, and fox monument.
== Festivals and Annual Events == * Grand Festival: October 3 - October 5 * Inari Grand Festival: April 8 - April 9 * Kitsune no Yomeiri Ritual: Held on [[Setsubun]], an Inari Shrine exorcism event. ** The ritual involves the "daughter fox" of Fukutoku Inari Shrine within the precincts marrying into Sukeshiro Inari Shrine within the same precincts. Men and women of the year's [[Yakudoshi]] (unlucky years) dress as fox bride and groom, and [[bean throwing]] with lottery tickets is also held.
== Location == * Mie Prefecture, Yokkaichi City, Miyamado-cho 1-62
== Access == * [[Kintetsu]] [[Nagoya Line]] [[Miyamado Station]], immediately accessible on foot.
== Related Topics == {{Commonscat|Miyamado-jinja|Miyamado Shrine}} * [[Shinmei Shrine]] * [[Inari Ōkami]]

{{Shrine |Name = Suzaki Hamamiya Shinmei Shrine |Image = [[File:Suzakihamamiya(Mie).JPG|250px]] |Location = Mie Prefecture, Yokkaichi City, Miyamado-cho 1-62 |Coordinates = {{Coord|34|56|34|N|136|37|18|E|type:landmark_region:JP-24|display=inline,title}} |Deities = Amaterasu Ōmikami |Rank = |Founded = [[876]] (18th year of [[Jōgan (Japan)|Jōgan]]) |Main hall = |Alias = Miyamado Shrine |Annual festival = October 3 - October 5 |Rituals = Kitsune no Yomeiri Ritual }}
[[File:Miyamado-inari(Mie).JPG|thumb|250px|right|Miyamado Inari Shrine]]
'''Suzaki Hamamiya Shinmei Shrine''' (洲崎濱宮神明神社, Suzaki Hamamiya Shinmei Jinja) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in the Miyamado area of the [[Shiohama District]] in [[Yokkaichi City]], [[Mie Prefecture]].
== Overview == The character 濱 (Hama) can also be written as '''浜''' or '''濵'''.
The shrine's subordinate shrine, '''Miyamado Inari Shrine''' (みやまどいなりじんじゃ), is larger in scale and is known for the "[[Kitsune no Yomeiri]]" (Fox Wedding) ritual.
Within the shrine precincts, there are many Inari shrines, Tenjin shrines, and others. Collectively, they are also referred to as "'''Miyamado Shrine'''" (みやまどじんじゃ).
== Enshrined Deities == === Shinmei Shrine === {{Div col}} * [[Amenomahitotsu no Mikoto|Omotaru no Mikoto]] * [[Amaterasu Ōmikami]] * [[Kashikone no Mikoto]] * [[Emperor Nintoku]] * [[Takehaya Susanoo no Mikoto]] * [[Ukanomitama no Mikoto]] * [[Ōyamatsumi no Mikoto]] * [[Kagutsuchi]] * [[Sugawara no Michizane]] * [[Hi no Kagutsuchi no Kami]] * [[Ukemochi no Kami]] * [[Yamato Hime no Mikoto]] {{Div col end}}
=== Inari Shrine === * Ukanomitama no Mikoto * [[Ōkuninushi no Mikoto]] * [[Ōtanojigami]] * Ukemochi no Kami * [[Ōmiyanome no Kami]]
== Overview == The shrine was founded in [[876]] (18th year of [[Jōgan (Japan)|Jōgan]]) according to tradition. During the [[Heian period]], this area formed a sandbank by the coast and was historically called "Hama no Miya" (Shrine of the Coast).
The time of the Inari Shrine's dedication is unknown.
== Structures and Grounds == The subordinate Miyamado Inari Shrine within the grounds surpasses Shinmei Shrine in scale.
In addition to more than 20 subsidiary Inari shrines, the precinct includes Tenjin Sugawara Shrine, which enshrines a statue of [[Sugawara no Michizane]] reputed to be the largest in Japan. Other features include a calligraphy monument, eyeglass monument, and fox monument.
== Festivals and Annual Events == * Grand Festival: October 3 - October 5 * Inari Grand Festival: April 8 - April 9 * Kitsune no Yomeiri Ritual: Held on [[Setsubun]], an Inari Shrine exorcism event. ** The ritual involves the "daughter fox" of Fukutoku Inari Shrine within the precincts marrying into Sukeshiro Inari Shrine within the same precincts. Men and women of the year's [[Yakudoshi]] (unlucky years) dress as fox bride and groom, and [[bean throwing]] with lottery tickets is also held.
== Location == * Mie Prefecture, Yokkaichi City, Miyamado-cho 1-62
== Access == * [[Kintetsu]] [[Nagoya Line]] [[Miyamado Station]], immediately accessible on foot.
== Related Topics == {{Commonscat|Miyamado-jinja|Miyamado Shrine}} * [[Shinmei Shrine]] * [[Inari Ōkami]]

revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building | name = Suzaki Hamamiya Shinmei Shrine | image = [[File:Suzakihamamiya(Mie).JPG|250px]] | location = 1-62 Miyamado Town, Yokkaichi, Mie Prefecture, Japan | latitude = 34.9428 | longitude = 136.6217 | deity = {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神}} and others | founded = [[876]] (18th year of the {{ill|Jōgan era|ja|貞観}}) | main_building_style = | festivals = October 3–5 (Annual Festival); February (Fox Wedding Ceremony) | map_type = Japan Mie }}
'''Suzaki Hamamiya Shinmei Shrine''' ({{ill|Suzaki Hamamiya Shinmei Shrine|ja|洲崎濱宮神明神社}}, ''Suzaki Hamamiya Shinmei Jinja'') is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in the [[Shiobama District]], [[Yokkaichi]], [[Mie Prefecture]], Japan. It is often collectively referred to as "Miyamado Shrine" due to the large presence of its {{ill|Inari Shrine|ja|稲荷神社}}.
== Overview == The shrine's name "濱" (''Hama'') is also written as "浜" or "濵."
The subordinate shrine, known as '''Miyamado Inari Shrine''' ({{ill|Miyamado Inari Shrine|ja|海山道稲荷神社}}), is larger than the main Shinmei Shrine and is famous for the "Fox Wedding Ceremony" ({{ill|Fox Wedding|ja|狐の嫁入り}}).
The grounds also include multiple Inari shrines, {{ill|Tenjin shrines|ja|天神社}}, and unique features such as Japan’s largest statue of {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}}.
== Enshrined Deities == === Shinmei Shrine === {{Div col}} * {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|lt=Amenokoyane-no-Mikoto|ja|天児屋根命}} * {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神}} * {{ill|Takemikazuchi|lt=Takemikazuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|建御雷命}} * {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}} * {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚尊}} * {{ill|Ukanomitama|lt-Ukanomitama-no-Kami|ja|倉稲魂命}} * {{ill|Oyamakui no Kami|lt=Ōyamakui-no-Kami|ja|大山祇神}} * {{ill|Kagu-tsuchi|lt=Kagutsuchi-no-Kami|ja|火之迦具土神}} * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}} * {{ill|Kagu-tsuchi|lt=Ho-musubi-no-Kami|ja|火産霊神}} * {{ill|Ukemochi|lt=Ukemochi-no-Kami|ja|保食神}} * {{ill|Yamatohime-no-mikoto|lt=Yamatohime-no-Mikoto|ja|倭姫命}} {{Div col end}}
=== Inari Shrine === * {{ill|Ukanomitama|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Kami|ja|倉稲魂命}} * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|lt=Ōkuninushi-no-Kami|ja|大己貴命}} * {{ill|Ōta-no-Kami|ja|大田命}} * {{ill|Ukemochi|lt=Ukemochi-no-Kami|ja|保食神}} * {{ill|Ōmiyahime-no-Mikoto|lt=Ōmiyanome-no-Kami|ja|大宮能売神}}
== History == The shrine is traditionally said to have been founded in [[876]] (the 18th year of the {{ill|Jōgan era|ja|貞観}}). During the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}, the area was coastal and formed a sandbar called "Suzaki." Thus, the shrine became known as "Hama-no-Miya" (Shrine of the Beach).
The exact date of the establishment of the {{ill|Inari Shrine|ja|稲荷神社}} is unknown.
== Shrine Buildings and Grounds == The grounds include the {{ill|Miyamado Inari Shrine|ja|海山道稲荷神社}}, which surpasses the Shinmei Shrine in scale.
There are over 20 subsidiary {{ill|Inari shrines|ja|稲荷神社}}, a {{ill|Tenjin shrine|ja|天神社}} housing Japan’s largest statue of {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}}, and various monuments such as the Pen Mound, Eyeglass Mound, and Fox Mound.
== Festivals and Events == * Annual Festival: October 3–5 * Inari Grand Festival: April 8–9 * Fox Wedding Ceremony: Held on {{ill|Setsubun|ja|節分}} (February), this purification ritual involves a mock wedding procession. Participants representing the bride and groom wear fox masks, and a "bean-throwing" event is also held.
== location == 1-62 Miyamado Town, [[Yokkaichi]], [[Mie Prefecture]], Japan
== Access == * A short walk from [[Kintetsu Nagoya Line]]'s [[Miyamado Station]].
== Related Topics == {{Commonscat|Miyamado-jinja|Miyamado Shrine}} * {{ill|Shinmei Shrine|ja|神明神社}} * {{ill|Inari Shrine|ja|稲荷神社}}

== History == The shrine is traditionally said to have been founded in [[876]] (the 18th year of the {{ill|Jōgan era|ja|貞観}}). During the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}, the area was coastal and formed a sandbar called "Suzaki." Thus, the shrine became known as "Hama-no-Miya" (Shrine of the Beach).
The exact date of the establishment of the {{ill|Inari Shrine|ja|稲荷神社}} is unknown.
== Shrine Buildings and Grounds == The grounds include the {{ill|Miyamado Inari Shrine|ja|海山道稲荷神社}}, which surpasses the Shinmei Shrine in scale.
There are over 20 subsidiary {{ill|Inari shrines|ja|稲荷神社}}, a {{ill|Tenjin shrine|ja|天神社}} housing Japan’s largest statue of {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}}, and various monuments such as the Pen Mound, Eyeglass Mound, and Fox Mound.
== Festivals and Events == * Annual Festival: October 3–5 * Inari Grand Festival: April 8–9 * Fox Wedding Ceremony: Held on {{ill|Setsubun|ja|節分}} (February), this purification ritual involves a mock wedding procession. Participants representing the bride and groom wear fox masks, and a "bean-throwing" event is also held.
== location == 1-62 Miyamado Town, [[Yokkaichi]], [[Mie Prefecture]], Japan
== Access == * A short walk from [[Kintetsu Nagoya Line]]'s [[Miyamado Station]].
== Related Topics == {{Commonscat|Miyamado-jinja|Miyamado Shrine}} * {{ill|Shinmei Shrine|ja|神明神社}} * {{ill|Inari Shrine|ja|稲荷神社}}

{{Infobox religious building | name = Suzaki Hamamiya Shinmei Shrine | image = [[File:Suzakihamamiya(Mie).JPG|250px]] | location = 1-62 Miyamado Town, Yokkaichi, Mie Prefecture, Japan | latitude = 34.9428 | longitude = 136.6217 | deity = {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神}} and others | founded = [[876]] (18th year of the {{ill|Jōgan era|ja|貞観}}) | main_building_style = | festivals = October 3–5 (Annual Festival); February (Fox Wedding Ceremony) | map_type = Japan Mie }}
'''Suzaki Hamamiya Shinmei Shrine''' ({{ill|Suzaki Hamamiya Shinmei Shrine|ja|洲崎濱宮神明神社}}, ''Suzaki Hamamiya Shinmei Jinja'') is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in the [[Shiobama District]], [[Yokkaichi]], [[Mie Prefecture]], Japan. It is often collectively referred to as "Miyamado Shrine" due to the large presence of its {{ill|Inari Shrine|ja|稲荷神社}}.
== Overview == [[File:Miyamado-inari(Mie).JPG|thumb|Miyamado Inari Shrine]] The shrine's name "濱" (''Hama'') is also written as "浜" or "濵."
The subordinate shrine, known as '''Miyamado Inari Shrine''' ({{ill|Miyamado Inari Shrine|ja|海山道稲荷神社}}), is larger than the main Shinmei Shrine and is famous for the "Fox Wedding Ceremony" ({{ill|Kitsune no yomeiri|lt=Fox Wedding|ja|狐の嫁入り}}).
The grounds also include multiple Inari shrines, {{ill|Tenjinja|lt=Tenjin shrines|ja|天神社}}, and unique features such as Japan’s largest statue of {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}}.

=== Inari Shrine === * {{ill|Ukanomitama|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Kami|ja|倉稲魂命}} * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|lt=Ōkuninushi-no-Kami|ja|大己貴命}} * {{ill|Ōta-no-Kami|ja|大田命}} * {{ill|Ukemochi|lt=Ukemochi-no-Kami|ja|保食神}} * {{ill|Ōmiyahime-no-Mikoto|lt=Ōmiyanome-no-Kami|ja|大宮能売神}}
== History == The shrine is traditionally said to have been founded in [[876]] (the 18th year of the {{ill|Jōgan era|ja|貞観}}). During the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}, the area was coastal and formed a sandbar called "Suzaki." Thus, the shrine became known as "Hama-no-Miya" (Shrine of the Beach).
The exact date of the establishment of the {{ill|Inari Shrine|ja|稲荷神社}} is unknown.
== Shrine Buildings and Grounds == The grounds include the {{ill|Miyamado Inari Shrine|ja|海山道稲荷神社}}, which surpasses the Shinmei Shrine in scale.
There are over 20 subsidiary {{ill|Inari shrines|ja|稲荷神社}}, a {{ill|Tenjin shrine|ja|天神社}} housing Japan’s largest statue of {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}}, and various monuments such as the Pen Mound, Eyeglass Mound, and Fox Mound.
== Festivals and Events == * Annual Festival: October 3–5 * Inari Grand Festival: April 8–9 * Fox Wedding Ceremony: Held on {{ill|Setsubun|ja|節分}} (February), this purification ritual involves a mock wedding procession. Participants representing the bride and groom wear fox masks, and a "bean-throwing" event is also held.

The subordinate shrine, known as '''Miyamado Inari Shrine''' ({{ill|Miyamado Inari Shrine|ja|海山道稲荷神社}}), is larger than the main Shinmei Shrine and is famous for the "Fox Wedding Ceremony" ({{ill|Kitsune no yomeiri|lt=Fox Wedding|ja|狐の嫁入り}}).
The grounds also include multiple Inari shrines, {{ill|Tenjinja|lt=Tenjin shrines|ja|天神社}}, and unique features such as Japan’s largest statue of {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}}.
== Enshrined Deities == === Shinmei Shrine === {{Div col}} * {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|lt=Amenokoyane-no-Mikoto|ja|天児屋根命}} * {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神}} * {{ill|Takemikazuchi|lt=Takemikazuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|建御雷命}} * {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}} * {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚尊}} * {{ill|Ukanomitama|lt-Ukanomitama-no-Kami|ja|倉稲魂命}} * {{ill|Oyamakui no Kami|lt=Ōyamakui-no-Kami|ja|大山祇神}} * {{ill|Kagu-tsuchi|lt=Kagutsuchi-no-Kami|ja|火之迦具土神}} * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}} * {{ill|Kagu-tsuchi|lt=Ho-musubi-no-Kami|ja|火産霊神}} * {{ill|Ukemochi|lt=Ukemochi-no-Kami|ja|保食神}} * {{ill|Yamatohime-no-mikoto|lt=Yamatohime-no-Mikoto|ja|倭姫命}} {{Div col end}}
=== Inari Shrine === * {{ill|Ukanomitama|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Kami|ja|倉稲魂命}} * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|lt=Ōkuninushi-no-Kami|ja|大己貴命}} * {{ill|Ōta-no-Kami|ja|大田命}} * {{ill|Ukemochi|lt=Ukemochi-no-Kami|ja|保食神}} * {{ill|Ōmiyahime-no-Mikoto|lt=Ōmiyanome-no-Kami|ja|大宮能売神}}
== History == The shrine is traditionally said to have been founded in [[876]] (the 18th year of the {{ill|Jōgan era|ja|貞観}}). During the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}, the area was coastal and formed a sandbar called "Suzaki." Thus, the shrine became known as "Hama-no-Miya" (Shrine of the Beach).
The exact date of the establishment of the {{ill|Inari Shrine|ja|稲荷神社}} is unknown.
== Shrine Buildings and Grounds == The grounds include the {{ill|Miyamado Inari Shrine|ja|海山道稲荷神社}}, which surpasses the Shinmei Shrine in scale.
There are over 20 subsidiary {{ill|Inari shrines|ja|稲荷神社}}, a {{ill|Tenjin shrine|ja|天神社}} housing Japan’s largest statue of {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}}, and various monuments such as the Pen Mound, Eyeglass Mound, and Fox Mound.
== Festivals and Events == * festival: October 3–5 * Inari Grand Festival: April 8–9 * Fox Wedding Ceremony: Held on {{ill|Setsubun|ja|節分}} (February), this purification ritual involves a mock wedding procession. Participants representing the bride and groom wear fox masks, and a "bean-throwing" event is also held.

== Access == * A short walk from [[Kintetsu Nagoya Line]]'s [[Miyamado Station]].

== location == 1-62 Miyamado Town, [[Yokkaichi]], [[Mie Prefecture]], Japan
== Access == * A short walk from [[Kintetsu Nagoya Line]]'s [[Miyamado Station]].

revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building | name = Suzaki Hamamiya Shinmei Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Suzakihamamiya(Mie).JPG|250px]] | location = 1-62 Miyamado Town, Yokkaichi, Mie Prefecture, Japan | latitude = 34.9428 | longitude = 136.6217 | deity = {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神}} and others | founded = [[876]] (18th year of the {{ill|Jōgan era|ja|貞観}}) | main_building_style = | festivals = October 3–5 (festival); February (Fox Wedding Ceremony) | map_type = Japan Mie }}
'''Suzaki Hamamiya Shinmei Shrine''' ({{ill|Suzaki Hamamiya Shinmei Shrine|ja|洲崎濱宮神明神社}}, ''Suzaki Hamamiya Shinmei Jinja'') is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in the [[Shiobama District]], [[Yokkaichi]], [[Mie Prefecture]], Japan. It is often collectively referred to as "Miyamado Shrine" due to the large presence of its {{ill|Inari Shrine|ja|稲荷神社}}.
== Overview == [[File:Miyamado-inari(Mie).JPG|thumb|Miyamado Inari Shrine]] The shrine's name "濱" (''Hama'') is also written as "浜" or "濵."
The subordinate shrine, known as '''Miyamado Inari Shrine''' ({{ill|Miyamado Inari Shrine|ja|海山道稲荷神社}}), is larger than the main Shinmei Shrine and is famous for the "Fox Wedding Ceremony" ({{ill|Kitsune no yomeiri|lt=Fox Wedding|ja|狐の嫁入り}}).
The grounds also include multiple Inari shrines, {{ill|Tenjinja|lt=Tenjin shrines|ja|天神社}}, and unique features such as Japan’s largest statue of {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}}.
== Enshrined Deities == === Shinmei Shrine === {{Div col}} * {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|lt=Amenokoyane-no-Mikoto|ja|天児屋根命}} * {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神}} * {{ill|Takemikazuchi|lt=Takemikazuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|建御雷命}} * {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}} * {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚尊}} * {{ill|Ukanomitama|lt-Ukanomitama-no-Kami|ja|倉稲魂命}} * {{ill|Oyamakui no Kami|lt=Ōyamakui-no-Kami|ja|大山祇神}} * {{ill|Kagu-tsuchi|lt=Kagutsuchi-no-Kami|ja|火之迦具土神}} * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}} * {{ill|Kagu-tsuchi|lt=Ho-musubi-no-Kami|ja|火産霊神}} * {{ill|Ukemochi|lt=Ukemochi-no-Kami|ja|保食神}} * {{ill|Yamatohime-no-mikoto|lt=Yamatohime-no-Mikoto|ja|倭姫命}} {{Div col end}}
=== Inari Shrine === * {{ill|Ukanomitama|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Kami|ja|倉稲魂命}} * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|lt=Ōkuninushi-no-Kami|ja|大己貴命}} * {{ill|Ōta-no-Kami|ja|大田命}} * {{ill|Ukemochi|lt=Ukemochi-no-Kami|ja|保食神}} * {{ill|Ōmiyahime-no-Mikoto|lt=Ōmiyanome-no-Kami|ja|大宮能売神}}
== History == The shrine is traditionally said to have been founded in [[876]] (the 18th year of the {{ill|Jōgan era|ja|貞観}}). During the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}, the area was coastal and formed a sandbar called "Suzaki." Thus, the shrine became known as "Hama-no-Miya" (Shrine of the Beach).
The exact date of the establishment of the {{ill|Inari Shrine|ja|稲荷神社}} is unknown.
== Shrine Buildings and Grounds == The grounds include the {{ill|Miyamado Inari Shrine|ja|海山道稲荷神社}}, which surpasses the Shinmei Shrine in scale.
There are over 20 subsidiary {{ill|Inari shrines|ja|稲荷神社}}, a {{ill|Tenjin shrine|ja|天神社}} housing Japan’s largest statue of {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}}, and various monuments such as the Pen Mound, Eyeglass Mound, and Fox Mound.
== Festivals and Events == * festival: October 3–5 * Inari Grand Festival: April 8–9 * Fox Wedding Ceremony: Held on {{ill|Setsubun|ja|節分}} (February), this purification ritual involves a mock wedding procession. Participants representing the bride and groom wear fox masks, and a "bean-throwing" event is also held.
== location == 1-62 Miyamado Town, [[Yokkaichi]], [[Mie Prefecture]], Japan
== Access == * A short walk from [[Kintetsu Nagoya Line]]'s [[Miyamado Station]].
== Related Topics == {{Commonscat|Miyamado-jinja|Miyamado Shrine}} * {{ill|Shinmei Shrine|ja|神明神社}} * {{ill|Inari Shrine|ja|稲荷神社}}

{{Infobox religious building | name = Suzaki Hamamiya Shinmei Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Suzakihamamiya(Mie).JPG|250px]] | location = 1-62 Miyamado Town, Yokkaichi, Mie Prefecture, Japan | latitude = 34.9428 | longitude = 136.6217 | deity = {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神}} and others | founded = [[876]] (18th year of the {{ill|Jōgan era|ja|貞観}}) | main_building_style = | festivals = October 3–5 (festival); February (Fox Wedding Ceremony) | map_type = Japan Mie }}
'''Suzaki Hamamiya Shinmei Shrine''' ({{ill|Suzaki Hamamiya Shinmei Shrine|ja|洲崎濱宮神明神社}}, ''Suzaki Hamamiya Shinmei Jinja'') is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in the [[Shiobama District]], [[Yokkaichi]], [[Mie Prefecture]], Japan. It is often collectively referred to as "Miyamado Shrine" due to the large presence of its {{ill|Inari Shrine|ja|稲荷神社}}.
== Overview == [[File:Miyamado-inari(Mie).JPG|thumb|Miyamado Inari Shrine]] The shrine's name "濱" (''Hama'') is also written as "浜" or "濵."
The subordinate shrine, known as '''Miyamado Inari Shrine''' ({{ill|Miyamado Inari Shrine|ja|海山道稲荷神社}}), is larger than the main Shinmei Shrine and is famous for the "Fox Wedding Ceremony" ({{ill|Kitsune no yomeiri|lt=Fox Wedding|ja|狐の嫁入り}}).
The grounds also include multiple Inari shrines, {{ill|Tenjinja|lt=Tenjin shrines|ja|天神社}}, and unique features such as Japan’s largest statue of {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}}.
== Enshrined Deities == === Shinmei Shrine === {{Div col}} * {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|lt=Amenokoyane-no-Mikoto|ja|天児屋根命}} * {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神}} * {{ill|Takemikazuchi|lt=Takemikazuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|建御雷命}} * {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}} * {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚尊}} * {{ill|Ukanomitama|lt-Ukanomitama-no-Kami|ja|倉稲魂命}} * {{ill|Oyamakui no Kami|lt=Ōyamakui-no-Kami|ja|大山祇神}} * {{ill|Kagu-tsuchi|lt=Kagutsuchi-no-Kami|ja|火之迦具土神}} * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}} * {{ill|Kagu-tsuchi|lt=Ho-musubi-no-Kami|ja|火産霊神}} * {{ill|Ukemochi|lt=Ukemochi-no-Kami|ja|保食神}} * {{ill|Yamatohime-no-mikoto|lt=Yamatohime-no-Mikoto|ja|倭姫命}} {{Div col end}}
=== Inari Shrine === * {{ill|Ukanomitama|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Kami|ja|倉稲魂命}} * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|lt=Ōkuninushi-no-Kami|ja|大己貴命}} * {{ill|Ōta-no-Kami|ja|大田命}} * {{ill|Ukemochi|lt=Ukemochi-no-Kami|ja|保食神}} * {{ill|Ōmiyahime-no-Mikoto|lt=Ōmiyanome-no-Kami|ja|大宮能売神}}
== History == The shrine is traditionally said to have been founded in [[876]] (the 18th year of the {{ill|Jōgan era|ja|貞観}}). During the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}, the area was coastal and formed a sandbar called "Suzaki." Thus, the shrine became known as "Hama-no-Miya" (Shrine of the Beach).
The exact date of the establishment of the {{ill|Inari Shrine|ja|稲荷神社}} is unknown.
== Shrine Buildings and Grounds == The grounds include the {{ill|Miyamado Inari Shrine|ja|海山道稲荷神社}}, which surpasses the Shinmei Shrine in scale.
There are over 20 subsidiary {{ill|Inari shrines|ja|稲荷神社}}, a {{ill|Tenjin shrine|ja|天神社}} housing Japan’s largest statue of {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}}, and various monuments such as the Pen Mound, Eyeglass Mound, and Fox Mound.
== Festivals and Events == * festival: October 3–5 * Inari Grand Festival: April 8–9 * Fox Wedding Ceremony: Held on {{ill|Setsubun|ja|節分}} (February), this purification ritual involves a mock wedding procession. Participants representing the bride and groom wear fox masks, and a "bean-throwing" event is also held.
== location == 1-62 Miyamado Town, [[Yokkaichi]], [[Mie Prefecture]], Japan
== Access == * A short walk from [[Kintetsu Nagoya Line]]'s [[Miyamado Station]].

== Enshrined Deities == === Shinmei Shrine === {{Div col}} * {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|lt=Amenokoyane-no-Mikoto|ja|天児屋根命}} * {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神}} * {{ill|Takemikazuchi|lt=Takemikazuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|建御雷命}} * {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}} * {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚尊}} * {{ill|Ukanomitama|lt-Ukanomitama-no-Kami|ja|倉稲魂命}} * {{ill|Oyamakui no Kami|lt=Ōyamakui-no-Kami|ja|大山祇神}} * {{ill|Kagu-tsuchi|lt=Kagutsuchi-no-Kami|ja|火之迦具土神}} * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}} * {{ill|Kagu-tsuchi|lt=Ho-musubi-no-Kami|ja|火産霊神}} * {{ill|Ukemochi|lt=Ukemochi-no-Kami|ja|保食神}} * {{ill|Yamatohime-no-mikoto|lt=Yamatohime-no-Mikoto|ja|倭姫命}} {{Div col end}}
=== Inari Shrine === * {{ill|Ukanomitama|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Kami|ja|倉稲魂命}} * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|lt=Ōkuninushi-no-Kami|ja|大己貴命}} * {{ill|Ōta-no-Kami|ja|大田命}} * {{ill|Ukemochi|lt=Ukemochi-no-Kami|ja|保食神}} * {{ill|Ōmiyahime-no-Mikoto|lt=Ōmiyanome-no-Kami|ja|大宮能売神}}
== History == The shrine is traditionally said to have been founded in [[876]] (the 18th year of the {{ill|Jōgan era|ja|貞観}}). During the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}, the area was coastal and formed a sandbar called "Suzaki." Thus, the shrine became known as "Hama-no-Miya" (Shrine of the Beach).
The exact date of the establishment of the {{ill|Inari Shrine|ja|稲荷神社}} is unknown.
== Shrine Buildings and Grounds == The grounds include the {{ill|Miyamado Inari Shrine|ja|海山道稲荷神社}}, which surpasses the Shinmei Shrine in scale.
There are over 20 subsidiary {{ill|Inari shrines|ja|稲荷神社}}, a {{ill|Tenjin shrine|ja|天神社}} housing Japan’s largest statue of {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}}, and various monuments such as the Pen Mound, Eyeglass Mound, and Fox Mound.
== Festivals and Events == * festival: October 3–5 * Inari Grand Festival: April 8–9 * Fox Wedding Ceremony: Held on {{ill|Setsubun|ja|節分}} (February), this purification ritual involves a mock wedding procession. Participants representing the bride and groom wear fox masks, and a "bean-throwing" event is also held.
== location == 1-62 Miyamado Town, [[Yokkaichi]], [[Mie Prefecture]], Japan
== Access == * A short walk from [[Kintetsu Nagoya Line]]'s [[Miyamado Station]].

=== Inari Shrine === * {{ill|Ukanomitama|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Kami|ja|倉稲魂命}} * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|lt=Ōkuninushi-no-Kami|ja|大己貴命}} * {{ill|Ōta-no-Kami|ja|大田命}} * {{ill|Ukemochi|lt=Ukemochi-no-Kami|ja|保食神}} * {{ill|Ōmiyahime-no-Mikoto|lt=Ōmiyanome-no-Kami|ja|大宮能売神}}
== History == The shrine is traditionally said to have been founded in [[876]] (the 18th year of the {{ill|Jōgan era|ja|貞観}}). During the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}, the area was coastal and formed a sandbar called "Suzaki." Thus, the shrine became known as "Hama-no-Miya" (Shrine of the Beach).
The exact date of the establishment of the {{ill|Inari Shrine|ja|稲荷神社}} is unknown.
== Shrine Buildings and Grounds == The grounds include the {{ill|Miyamado Inari Shrine|ja|海山道稲荷神社}}, which surpasses the Shinmei Shrine in scale.
There are over 20 subsidiary {{ill|Inari shrines|ja|稲荷神社}}, a {{ill|Tenjin shrine|ja|天神社}} housing Japan’s largest statue of {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}}, and various monuments such as the Pen Mound, Eyeglass Mound, and Fox Mound.
== Festivals and Events == * festival: October 3–5 * Inari Grand Festival: April 8–9 * Fox Wedding Ceremony: Held on {{ill|Setsubun|ja|節分}} (February), this purification ritual involves a mock wedding procession. Participants representing the bride and groom wear fox masks, and a "bean-throwing" event is also held.
== location == 1-62 Miyamado Town, [[Yokkaichi]], [[Mie Prefecture]], Japan
== Access == * A short walk from [[Kintetsu Nagoya Line]]'s [[Miyamado Station]].

The subordinate shrine, known as '''Miyamado Inari Shrine''' ({{ill|Miyamado Inari Shrine|ja|海山道稲荷神社}}), is larger than the main Shinmei Shrine and is famous for the "Fox Wedding Ceremony" ({{ill|Kitsune no yomeiri|lt=Fox Wedding|ja|狐の嫁入り}}).
The grounds also include multiple Inari shrines, {{ill|Tenjinja|lt=Tenjin shrines|ja|天神社}}, and unique features such as Japan’s largest statue of {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}}.
== Enshrined Deities == === Shinmei Shrine === {{Div col}} * {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|lt=Amenokoyane-no-Mikoto|ja|天児屋根命}} * {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神}} * {{ill|Takemikazuchi|lt=Takemikazuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|建御雷命}} * {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}} * {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚尊}} * {{ill|Ukanomitama|lt-Ukanomitama-no-Kami|ja|倉稲魂命}} * {{ill|Oyamakui no Kami|lt=Ōyamakui-no-Kami|ja|大山祇神}} * {{ill|Kagu-tsuchi|lt=Kagutsuchi-no-Kami|ja|火之迦具土神}} * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}} * {{ill|Kagu-tsuchi|lt=Ho-musubi-no-Kami|ja|火産霊神}} * {{ill|Ukemochi|lt=Ukemochi-no-Kami|ja|保食神}} * {{ill|Yamatohime-no-mikoto|lt=Yamatohime-no-Mikoto|ja|倭姫命}} {{Div col end}}
=== Inari Shrine === * {{ill|Ukanomitama|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Kami|ja|倉稲魂命}} * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|lt=Ōkuninushi-no-Kami|ja|大己貴命}} * {{ill|Ōta-no-Kami|ja|大田命}} * {{ill|Ukemochi|lt=Ukemochi-no-Kami|ja|保食神}} * {{ill|Ōmiyahime-no-Mikoto|lt=Ōmiyanome-no-Kami|ja|大宮能売神}}
== History == The shrine is traditionally said to have been founded in [[876]] (the 18th year of the {{ill|Jōgan era|ja|貞観}}). During the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}, the area was coastal and formed a sandbar called "Suzaki." Thus, the shrine became known as "Hama-no-Miya" (Shrine of the Beach).
The exact date of the establishment of the {{ill|Inari Shrine|ja|稲荷神社}} is unknown.
== Shrine Buildings and Grounds == The grounds include the {{ill|Miyamado Inari Shrine|ja|海山道稲荷神社}}, which surpasses the Shinmei Shrine in scale.
There are over 20 subsidiary {{ill|Inari shrines|ja|稲荷神社}}, a {{ill|Tenjin shrine|ja|天神社}} housing Japan’s largest statue of {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}}, and various monuments such as the Pen Mound, Eyeglass Mound, and Fox Mound.
== Festivals and Events == * festival: October 3–5 * Inari Grand Festival: April 8–9 * Fox Wedding Ceremony: Held on {{ill|Setsubun|ja|節分}} (February), this purification ritual involves a mock wedding procession. Participants representing the bride and groom wear fox masks, and a "bean-throwing" event is also held.
== location == 1-62 Miyamado Town, [[Yokkaichi]], [[Mie Prefecture]], Japan
== Access == * A short walk from [[Kintetsu Nagoya Line]]'s [[Miyamado Station]].

'''Suzaki Hamamiya Shinmei Shrine''' ({{ill|Suzaki Hamamiya Shinmei Shrine|ja|洲崎濱宮神明神社}}, ''Suzaki Hamamiya Shinmei Jinja'') is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in the [[Shiobama District]], [[Yokkaichi]], [[Mie Prefecture]], Japan. It is often collectively referred to as "Miyamado Shrine" due to the large presence of its {{ill|Inari Shrine|ja|稲荷神社}}.
== Overview == [[File:Miyamado-inari(Mie).JPG|thumb|Miyamado Inari Shrine]] The shrine's name "濱" (''Hama'') is also written as "浜" or "濵."
The subordinate shrine, known as '''Miyamado Inari Shrine''' ({{ill|Miyamado Inari Shrine|ja|海山道稲荷神社}}), is larger than the main Shinmei Shrine and is famous for the "Fox Wedding Ceremony" ({{ill|Kitsune no yomeiri|lt=Fox Wedding|ja|狐の嫁入り}}).
The grounds also include multiple Inari shrines, {{ill|Tenjinja|lt=Tenjin shrines|ja|天神社}}, and unique features such as Japan’s largest statue of {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}}.
== Enshrined Deities == === Shinmei Shrine === {{Div col}} * {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|lt=Amenokoyane-no-Mikoto|ja|天児屋根命}} * {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神}} * {{ill|Takemikazuchi|lt=Takemikazuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|建御雷命}} * {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}} * {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚尊}} * {{ill|Ukanomitama|lt-Ukanomitama-no-Kami|ja|倉稲魂命}} * {{ill|Oyamakui no Kami|lt=Ōyamakui-no-Kami|ja|大山祇神}} * {{ill|Kagu-tsuchi|lt=Kagutsuchi-no-Kami|ja|火之迦具土神}} * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}} * {{ill|Kagu-tsuchi|lt=Ho-musubi-no-Kami|ja|火産霊神}} * {{ill|Ukemochi|lt=Ukemochi-no-Kami|ja|保食神}} * {{ill|Yamatohime-no-mikoto|lt=Yamatohime-no-Mikoto|ja|倭姫命}} {{Div col end}}
=== Inari Shrine === * {{ill|Ukanomitama|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Kami|ja|倉稲魂命}} * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|lt=Ōkuninushi-no-Kami|ja|大己貴命}} * {{ill|Ōta-no-Kami|ja|大田命}} * {{ill|Ukemochi|lt=Ukemochi-no-Kami|ja|保食神}} * {{ill|Ōmiyahime-no-Mikoto|lt=Ōmiyanome-no-Kami|ja|大宮能売神}}
== History == The shrine is traditionally said to have been founded in [[876]] (the 18th year of the {{ill|Jōgan era|ja|貞観}}). During the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}, the area was coastal and formed a sandbar called "Suzaki." Thus, the shrine became known as "Hama-no-Miya" (Shrine of the Beach).
The exact date of the establishment of the {{ill|Inari Shrine|ja|稲荷神社}} is unknown.
== Shrine Buildings and Grounds == The grounds include the {{ill|Miyamado Inari Shrine|ja|海山道稲荷神社}}, which surpasses the Shinmei Shrine in scale.
There are over 20 subsidiary {{ill|Inari shrines|ja|稲荷神社}}, a {{ill|Tenjin shrine|ja|天神社}} housing Japan’s largest statue of {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}}, and various monuments such as the Pen Mound, Eyeglass Mound, and Fox Mound.
== Festivals and Events == * festival: October 3–5 * Inari Grand Festival: April 8–9 * Fox Wedding Ceremony: Held on {{ill|Setsubun|ja|節分}} (February), this purification ritual involves a mock wedding procession. Participants representing the bride and groom wear fox masks, and a "bean-throwing" event is also held.
== location == 1-62 Miyamado Town, [[Yokkaichi]], [[Mie Prefecture]], Japan
== Access == * A short walk from [[Kintetsu Nagoya Line]]'s [[Miyamado Station]].

{{Infobox religious building | name = Suzaki Hamamiya Shinmei Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Suzakihamamiya(Mie).JPG|250px]] | location = 1-62 Miyamado Town, Yokkaichi, Mie Prefecture, Japan | latitude = 34.9428 | longitude = 136.6217 | deity = {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神}} and others | founded = [[876]] (18th year of the {{ill|Jōgan era|ja|貞観}}) | main_building_style = | festivals = October 3–5 (festival); February (Fox Wedding Ceremony) | map_type = Japan Mie }}
'''Suzaki Hamamiya Shinmei Shrine''' ({{ill|Suzaki Hamamiya Shinmei Shrine|ja|洲崎濱宮神明神社}}, ''Suzaki Hamamiya Shinmei Jinja'') is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in the [[Shiobama District]], [[Yokkaichi]], [[Mie Prefecture]], Japan. It is often collectively referred to as "Miyamado Shrine" due to the large presence of its {{ill|Inari Shrine|ja|稲荷神社}}.
== Overview == [[File:Miyamado-inari(Mie).JPG|thumb|Miyamado Inari Shrine]] The shrine's name "濱" (''Hama'') is also written as "浜" or "濵."
The subordinate shrine, known as '''Miyamado Inari Shrine''' ({{ill|Miyamado Inari Shrine|ja|海山道稲荷神社}}), is larger than the main Shinmei Shrine and is famous for the "Fox Wedding Ceremony" ({{ill|Kitsune no yomeiri|lt=Fox Wedding|ja|狐の嫁入り}}).
The grounds also include multiple Inari shrines, {{ill|Tenjinja|lt=Tenjin shrines|ja|天神社}}, and unique features such as Japan’s largest statue of {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}}.
== Enshrined Deities == === Shinmei Shrine === {{Div col}} * {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|lt=Amenokoyane-no-Mikoto|ja|天児屋根命}} * {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神}} * {{ill|Takemikazuchi|lt=Takemikazuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|建御雷命}} * {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}} * {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚尊}} * {{ill|Ukanomitama|lt-Ukanomitama-no-Kami|ja|倉稲魂命}} * {{ill|Oyamakui no Kami|lt=Ōyamakui-no-Kami|ja|大山祇神}} * {{ill|Kagu-tsuchi|lt=Kagutsuchi-no-Kami|ja|火之迦具土神}} * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}} * {{ill|Kagu-tsuchi|lt=Ho-musubi-no-Kami|ja|火産霊神}} * {{ill|Ukemochi|lt=Ukemochi-no-Kami|ja|保食神}} * {{ill|Yamatohime-no-mikoto|lt=Yamatohime-no-Mikoto|ja|倭姫命}} {{Div col end}}
=== Inari Shrine === * {{ill|Ukanomitama|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Kami|ja|倉稲魂命}} * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|lt=Ōkuninushi-no-Kami|ja|大己貴命}} * {{ill|Ōta-no-Kami|ja|大田命}} * {{ill|Ukemochi|lt=Ukemochi-no-Kami|ja|保食神}} * {{ill|Ōmiyahime-no-Mikoto|lt=Ōmiyanome-no-Kami|ja|大宮能売神}}
== History == The shrine is traditionally said to have been founded in [[876]] (the 18th year of the {{ill|Jōgan era|ja|貞観}}). During the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}, the area was coastal and formed a sandbar called "Suzaki." Thus, the shrine became known as "Hama-no-Miya" (Shrine of the Beach).
The exact date of the establishment of the {{ill|Inari Shrine|ja|稲荷神社}} is unknown.
== Shrine Buildings and Grounds == The grounds include the {{ill|Miyamado Inari Shrine|ja|海山道稲荷神社}}, which surpasses the Shinmei Shrine in scale.
There are over 20 subsidiary {{ill|Inari shrines|ja|稲荷神社}}, a {{ill|Tenjin shrine|ja|天神社}} housing Japan’s largest statue of {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}}, and various monuments such as the Pen Mound, Eyeglass Mound, and Fox Mound.
== Festivals and Events == * festival: October 3–5 * Inari Grand Festival: April 8–9 * Fox Wedding Ceremony: Held on {{ill|Setsubun|ja|節分}} (February), this purification ritual involves a mock wedding procession. Participants representing the bride and groom wear fox masks, and a "bean-throwing" event is also held.
== location == 1-62 Miyamado Town, [[Yokkaichi]], [[Mie Prefecture]], Japan
== Access == * A short walk from [[Kintetsu Nagoya Line]]'s [[Miyamado Station]].

{{Infobox religious building | name = Suzaki Hamamiya Shinmei Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Suzakihamamiya(Mie).JPG|250px]] | location = 1-62 Miyamado Town, Yokkaichi, Mie Prefecture, Japan | latitude = 34.9428 | longitude = 136.6217 | deity = {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神}} and others | founded = [[876]] (18th year of the {{ill|Jōgan era|ja|貞観}}) | main_building_style = | festivals = October 3–5 (festival); February (Fox Wedding Ceremony) | map_type = Japan Mie }}
'''Suzaki Hamamiya Shinmei Shrine''' ({{ill|Suzaki Hamamiya Shinmei Shrine|ja|洲崎濱宮神明神社}}, ''Suzaki Hamamiya Shinmei Jinja'') is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in the [[Shiobama District]], [[Yokkaichi]], [[Mie Prefecture]], Japan. It is often collectively referred to as "Miyamado Shrine" due to the large presence of its {{ill|Inari Shrine|ja|稲荷神社}}.
== Overview == [[File:Miyamado-inari(Mie).JPG|thumb|Miyamado Inari Shrine]] The shrine's name "濱" (''Hama'') is also written as "浜" or "濵."
The subordinate shrine, known as '''Miyamado Inari Shrine''' ({{ill|Miyamado Inari Shrine|ja|海山道稲荷神社}}), is larger than the main Shinmei Shrine and is famous for the "Fox Wedding Ceremony" ({{ill|Kitsune no yomeiri|lt=Fox Wedding|ja|狐の嫁入り}}).
The grounds also include multiple Inari shrines, {{ill|Tenjinja|lt=Tenjin shrines|ja|天神社}}, and unique features such as Japan’s largest statue of {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}}.
== Enshrined Deities == === Shinmei Shrine === {{Div col}} * {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|lt=Amenokoyane-no-Mikoto|ja|天児屋根命}} * {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神}} * {{ill|Takemikazuchi|lt=Takemikazuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|建御雷命}} * {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}} * {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚尊}} * {{ill|Ukanomitama|lt-Ukanomitama-no-Kami|ja|倉稲魂命}} * {{ill|Oyamakui no Kami|lt=Ōyamakui-no-Kami|ja|大山祇神}} * {{ill|Kagu-tsuchi|lt=Kagutsuchi-no-Kami|ja|火之迦具土神}} * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}} * {{ill|Kagu-tsuchi|lt=Ho-musubi-no-Kami|ja|火産霊神}} * {{ill|Ukemochi|lt=Ukemochi-no-Kami|ja|保食神}} * {{ill|Yamatohime-no-mikoto|lt=Yamatohime-no-Mikoto|ja|倭姫命}} {{Div col end}}
=== Inari Shrine === * {{ill|Ukanomitama|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Kami|ja|倉稲魂命}} * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|lt=Ōkuninushi-no-Kami|ja|大己貴命}} * {{ill|Ōta-no-Kami|ja|大田命}} * {{ill|Ukemochi|lt=Ukemochi-no-Kami|ja|保食神}} * {{ill|Ōmiyahime-no-Mikoto|lt=Ōmiyanome-no-Kami|ja|大宮能売神}}
== History == The shrine is traditionally said to have been founded in [[876]] (the 18th year of the {{ill|Jōgan era|ja|貞観}}). During the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}, the area was coastal and formed a sandbar called "Suzaki." Thus, the shrine became known as "Hama-no-Miya" (Shrine of the Beach).
The exact date of the establishment of the {{ill|Inari Shrine|ja|稲荷神社}} is unknown.
== Shrine Buildings and Grounds == The grounds include the {{ill|Miyamado Inari Shrine|ja|海山道稲荷神社}}, which surpasses the Shinmei Shrine in scale.
There are over 20 subsidiary {{ill|Inari shrines|ja|稲荷神社}}, a {{ill|Tenjin shrine|ja|天神社}} housing Japan’s largest statue of {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}}, and various monuments such as the Pen Mound, Eyeglass Mound, and Fox Mound.
== Festivals and Events == * festival: October 3–5 * Inari Grand Festival: April 8–9 * Fox Wedding Ceremony: Held on {{ill|Setsubun|ja|節分}} (February), this purification ritual involves a mock wedding procession. Participants representing the bride and groom wear fox masks, and a "bean-throwing" event is also held.
== location == 1-62 Miyamado Town, [[Yokkaichi]], [[Mie Prefecture]], Japan
== Access == * A short walk from [[Kintetsu Nagoya Line]]'s [[Miyamado Station]].
== Related Topics == {{Commonscat|Miyamado-jinja|Miyamado Shrine}} * {{ill|Shinmei Shrine|ja|神明神社}} * {{ill|Inari Shrine|ja|稲荷神社}}

== Enshrined Deities == === Shinmei Shrine === {{Div col}} * {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|lt=Amenokoyane-no-Mikoto|ja|天児屋根命}} * {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神}} * {{ill|Takemikazuchi|lt=Takemikazuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|建御雷命}} * {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}} * {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚尊}} * {{ill|Ukanomitama|lt-Ukanomitama-no-Kami|ja|倉稲魂命}} * {{ill|Oyamakui no Kami|lt=Ōyamakui-no-Kami|ja|大山祇神}} * {{ill|Kagu-tsuchi|lt=Kagutsuchi-no-Kami|ja|火之迦具土神}} * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}} * {{ill|Kagu-tsuchi|lt=Ho-musubi-no-Kami|ja|火産霊神}} * {{ill|Ukemochi|lt=Ukemochi-no-Kami|ja|保食神}} * {{ill|Yamatohime-no-mikoto|lt=Yamatohime-no-Mikoto|ja|倭姫命}} {{Div col end}}
=== Inari Shrine === * {{ill|Ukanomitama|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Kami|ja|倉稲魂命}} * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|lt=Ōkuninushi-no-Kami|ja|大己貴命}} * {{ill|Ōta-no-Kami|ja|大田命}} * {{ill|Ukemochi|lt=Ukemochi-no-Kami|ja|保食神}} * {{ill|Ōmiyahime-no-Mikoto|lt=Ōmiyanome-no-Kami|ja|大宮能売神}}
== History == The shrine is traditionally said to have been founded in [[876]] (the 18th year of the {{ill|Jōgan era|ja|貞観}}). During the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}, the area was coastal and formed a sandbar called "Suzaki." Thus, the shrine became known as "Hama-no-Miya" (Shrine of the Beach).
The exact date of the establishment of the {{ill|Inari Shrine|ja|稲荷神社}} is unknown.
== Shrine Buildings and Grounds == The grounds include the {{ill|Miyamado Inari Shrine|ja|海山道稲荷神社}}, which surpasses the Shinmei Shrine in scale.
There are over 20 subsidiary {{ill|Inari shrines|ja|稲荷神社}}, a {{ill|Tenjin shrine|ja|天神社}} housing Japan’s largest statue of {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}}, and various monuments such as the Pen Mound, Eyeglass Mound, and Fox Mound.
== Festivals and Events == * festival: October 3–5 * Inari Grand Festival: April 8–9 * Fox Wedding Ceremony: Held on {{ill|Setsubun|ja|節分}} (February), this purification ritual involves a mock wedding procession. Participants representing the bride and groom wear fox masks, and a "bean-throwing" event is also held.
== location == 1-62 Miyamado Town, [[Yokkaichi]], [[Mie Prefecture]], Japan
== Access == * A short walk from [[Kintetsu Nagoya Line]]'s [[Miyamado Station]].

{{Infobox religious building | name = Suzaki Hamamiya Shinmei Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Suzakihamamiya(Mie).JPG|250px]] | location = 1-62 Miyamado Town, Yokkaichi, Mie Prefecture, Japan | latitude = 34.9428 | longitude = 136.6217 | deity = {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神}} and others | founded = [[876]] (18th year of the {{ill|Jōgan era|ja|貞観}}) | main_building_style = | festivals = October 3–5 (festival); February (Fox Wedding Ceremony) | map_type = Japan Mie }}
'''Suzaki Hamamiya Shinmei Shrine''' ({{ill|Suzaki Hamamiya Shinmei Shrine|ja|洲崎濱宮神明神社}}, ''Suzaki Hamamiya Shinmei Jinja'') is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in the [[Shiobama District]], [[Yokkaichi]], [[Mie Prefecture]], Japan. It is often collectively referred to as "Miyamado Shrine" due to the large presence of its {{ill|Inari Shrine|ja|稲荷神社}}.
== Overview == [[File:Miyamado-inari(Mie).JPG|thumb|Miyamado Inari Shrine]] The shrine's name "濱" (''Hama'') is also written as "浜" or "濵."
The subordinate shrine, known as '''Miyamado Inari Shrine''' ({{ill|Miyamado Inari Shrine|ja|海山道稲荷神社}}), is larger than the main Shinmei Shrine and is famous for the "Fox Wedding Ceremony" ({{ill|Kitsune no yomeiri|lt=Fox Wedding|ja|狐の嫁入り}}).
The grounds also include multiple Inari shrines, {{ill|Tenjinja|lt=Tenjin shrines|ja|天神社}}, and unique features such as Japan’s largest statue of {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}}.
== Enshrined Deities == === Shinmei Shrine === {{Div col}} * {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|lt=Amenokoyane-no-Mikoto|ja|天児屋根命}} * {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神}} * {{ill|Takemikazuchi|lt=Takemikazuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|建御雷命}} * {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}} * {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚尊}} * {{ill|Ukanomitama|lt-Ukanomitama-no-Kami|ja|倉稲魂命}} * {{ill|Oyamakui no Kami|lt=Ōyamakui-no-Kami|ja|大山祇神}} * {{ill|Kagu-tsuchi|lt=Kagutsuchi-no-Kami|ja|火之迦具土神}} * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}} * {{ill|Kagu-tsuchi|lt=Ho-musubi-no-Kami|ja|火産霊神}} * {{ill|Ukemochi|lt=Ukemochi-no-Kami|ja|保食神}} * {{ill|Yamatohime-no-mikoto|lt=Yamatohime-no-Mikoto|ja|倭姫命}} {{Div col end}}
=== Inari Shrine === * {{ill|Ukanomitama|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Kami|ja|倉稲魂命}} * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|lt=Ōkuninushi-no-Kami|ja|大己貴命}} * {{ill|Ōta-no-Kami|ja|大田命}} * {{ill|Ukemochi|lt=Ukemochi-no-Kami|ja|保食神}} * {{ill|Ōmiyahime-no-Mikoto|lt=Ōmiyanome-no-Kami|ja|大宮能売神}}
== History == The shrine is traditionally said to have been founded in [[876]] (the 18th year of the {{ill|Jōgan era|ja|貞観}}). During the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}, the area was coastal and formed a sandbar called "Suzaki." Thus, the shrine became known as "Hama-no-Miya" (Shrine of the Beach).
The exact date of the establishment of the {{ill|Inari Shrine|ja|稲荷神社}} is unknown.
== Shrine Buildings and Grounds == The grounds include the {{ill|Miyamado Inari Shrine|ja|海山道稲荷神社}}, which surpasses the Shinmei Shrine in scale.
There are over 20 subsidiary {{ill|Inari shrines|ja|稲荷神社}}, a {{ill|Tenjin shrine|ja|天神社}} housing Japan’s largest statue of {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}}, and various monuments such as the Pen Mound, Eyeglass Mound, and Fox Mound.
== Festivals and Events == * festival: October 3–5 * Inari Grand Festival: April 8–9 * Fox Wedding Ceremony: Held on {{ill|Setsubun|ja|節分}} (February), this purification ritual involves a mock wedding procession. Participants representing the bride and groom wear fox masks, and a "bean-throwing" event is also held.
== location == 1-62 Miyamado Town, [[Yokkaichi]], [[Mie Prefecture]], Japan
== Access == * A short walk from [[Kintetsu Nagoya Line]]'s [[Miyamado Station]].
== Related Topics == {{Commonscat|Miyamado-jinja|Miyamado Shrine}} * {{ill|Shinmei Shrine|ja|神明神社}} * {{ill|Inari Shrine|ja|稲荷神社}}

=== Inari Shrine === * {{ill|Ukanomitama|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Kami|ja|倉稲魂命}} * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|lt=Ōkuninushi-no-Kami|ja|大己貴命}} * {{ill|Ōta-no-Kami|ja|大田命}} * {{ill|Ukemochi|lt=Ukemochi-no-Kami|ja|保食神}} * {{ill|Ōmiyahime-no-Mikoto|lt=Ōmiyanome-no-Kami|ja|大宮能売神}}
== History == The shrine is traditionally said to have been founded in [[876]] (the 18th year of the {{ill|Jōgan era|ja|貞観}}). During the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}, the area was coastal and formed a sandbar called "Suzaki." Thus, the shrine became known as "Hama-no-Miya" (Shrine of the Beach).
The exact date of the establishment of the {{ill|Inari Shrine|ja|稲荷神社}} is unknown.
== Shrine Buildings and Grounds == The grounds include the {{ill|Miyamado Inari Shrine|ja|海山道稲荷神社}}, which surpasses the Shinmei Shrine in scale.
There are over 20 subsidiary {{ill|Inari shrines|ja|稲荷神社}}, a {{ill|Tenjin shrine|ja|天神社}} housing Japan’s largest statue of {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}}, and various monuments such as the Pen Mound, Eyeglass Mound, and Fox Mound.
== Festivals and Events == * festival: October 3–5 * Inari Grand Festival: April 8–9 * Fox Wedding Ceremony: Held on {{ill|Setsubun|ja|節分}} (February), this purification ritual involves a mock wedding procession. Participants representing the bride and groom wear fox masks, and a "bean-throwing" event is also held.
== location == 1-62 Miyamado Town, [[Yokkaichi]], [[Mie Prefecture]], Japan
== Access == * A short walk from [[Kintetsu Nagoya Line]]'s [[Miyamado Station]].
== Related Topics == {{Commonscat|Miyamado-jinja|Miyamado Shrine}} * {{ill|Shinmei Shrine|ja|神明神社}} * {{ill|Inari Shrine|ja|稲荷神社}}

{{Infobox religious building | name = Suzaki Hamamiya Shinmei Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Suzakihamamiya(Mie).JPG|250px]] | location = 1-62 Miyamado Town, Yokkaichi, Mie Prefecture, Japan | latitude = 34.9428 | longitude = 136.6217 | deity = {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神}} and others | founded = [[876]] (18th year of the {{ill|Jōgan era|ja|貞観}}) | main_building_style = | festivals = October 3–5 (festival); February (Fox Wedding Ceremony) | map_type = Japan Mie }}
'''Suzaki Hamamiya Shinmei Shrine''' ({{ill|Suzaki Hamamiya Shinmei Shrine|ja|洲崎濱宮神明神社}}, ''Suzaki Hamamiya Shinmei Jinja'') is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in the [[Shiobama District]], [[Yokkaichi]], [[Mie Prefecture]], Japan. It is often collectively referred to as "Miyamado Shrine" due to the large presence of its {{ill|Inari Shrine|ja|稲荷神社}}.
== Overview == [[File:Miyamado-inari(Mie).JPG|thumb|Miyamado Inari Shrine]] The shrine's name "濱" (''Hama'') is also written as "浜" or "濵."
The subordinate shrine, known as '''Miyamado Inari Shrine''' ({{ill|Miyamado Inari Shrine|ja|海山道稲荷神社}}), is larger than the main Shinmei Shrine and is famous for the "Fox Wedding Ceremony" ({{ill|Kitsune no yomeiri|lt=Fox Wedding|ja|狐の嫁入り}}).
The grounds also include multiple Inari shrines, {{ill|Tenjinja|lt=Tenjin shrines|ja|天神社}}, and unique features such as Japan’s largest statue of {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}}.
== Enshrined Deities == === Shinmei Shrine === {{Div col}} * {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|lt=Amenokoyane-no-Mikoto|ja|天児屋根命}} * {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神}} * {{ill|Takemikazuchi|lt=Takemikazuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|建御雷命}} * {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}} * {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚尊}} * {{ill|Ukanomitama|lt-Ukanomitama-no-Kami|ja|倉稲魂命}} * {{ill|Oyamakui no Kami|lt=Ōyamakui-no-Kami|ja|大山祇神}} * {{ill|Kagu-tsuchi|lt=Kagutsuchi-no-Kami|ja|火之迦具土神}} * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}} * {{ill|Kagu-tsuchi|lt=Ho-musubi-no-Kami|ja|火産霊神}} * {{ill|Ukemochi|lt=Ukemochi-no-Kami|ja|保食神}} * {{ill|Yamatohime-no-mikoto|lt=Yamatohime-no-Mikoto|ja|倭姫命|1=Yamatohime-no-mikoto}} {{Div col end}}
=== Inari Shrine === * {{ill|Ukanomitama|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Kami|ja|倉稲魂命}} * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|lt=Ōkuninushi-no-Kami|ja|大己貴命}} * {{ill|Ōta-no-Kami|ja|大田命}} * {{ill|Ukemochi|lt=Ukemochi-no-Kami|ja|保食神}} * {{ill|Ōmiyahime-no-Mikoto|lt=Ōmiyanome-no-Kami|ja|大宮能売神}}
== History == The shrine is traditionally said to have been founded in [[876]] (the 18th year of the {{ill|Jōgan era|ja|貞観}}). During the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}, the area was coastal and formed a sandbar called "Suzaki." Thus, the shrine became known as "Hama-no-Miya" (Shrine of the Beach).
The exact date of the establishment of the {{ill|Inari Shrine|ja|稲荷神社}} is unknown.
== Shrine Buildings and Grounds == The grounds include the {{ill|Miyamado Inari Shrine|ja|海山道稲荷神社}}, which surpasses the Shinmei Shrine in scale.
There are over 20 subsidiary {{ill|Inari shrines|ja|稲荷神社}}, a {{ill|Tenjin shrine|ja|天神社}} housing Japan’s largest statue of {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}}, and various monuments such as the Pen Mound, Eyeglass Mound, and Fox Mound.
== Festivals and Events == * festival: October 3–5 * Inari Grand Festival: April 8–9 * Fox Wedding Ceremony: Held on {{ill|Setsubun|ja|節分}} (February), this purification ritual involves a mock wedding procession. Participants representing the bride and groom wear fox masks, and a "bean-throwing" event is also held.
== location == 1-62 Miyamado Town, [[Yokkaichi]], [[Mie Prefecture]], Japan
== Access == * A short walk from [[Kintetsu Nagoya Line]]'s [[Miyamado Station]].
== See Also == {{Commonscat|Miyamado-jinja|Miyamado Shrine}} * {{ill|Shinmei Shrine|ja|神明神社}} * {{ill|Inari Shrine|ja|稲荷神社}}

{{Infobox religious building | name = Suzaki Hamamiya Shinmei Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Suzakihamamiya(Mie).JPG|250px]] | location = 1-62 Miyamado Town, Yokkaichi, Mie Prefecture, Japan | latitude = 34.9428 | longitude = 136.6217 | deity = {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神}} and others | founded = [[876]] (18th year of the {{ill|Jōgan era|ja|貞観}}) | main_building_style = | festivals = October 3–5 (festival); February (Fox Wedding Ceremony) | map_type = Japan Mie }}
'''Suzaki Hamamiya Shinmei Shrine''' ({{ill|Suzaki Hamamiya Shinmei Shrine|ja|洲崎濱宮神明神社}}, ''Suzaki Hamamiya Shinmei Jinja'') is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in the [[Shiobama District]], [[Yokkaichi]], [[Mie Prefecture]], Japan. It is often collectively referred to as "Miyamado Shrine" due to the large presence of its {{ill|Inari Shrine|ja|稲荷神社}}.
== Overview == [[File:Miyamado-inari(Mie).JPG|thumb|Miyamado Inari Shrine]] The shrine's name "濱" (''Hama'') is also written as "浜" or "濵."
The subordinate shrine, known as '''Miyamado Inari Shrine''' ({{ill|Miyamado Inari Shrine|ja|海山道稲荷神社}}), is larger than the main Shinmei Shrine and is famous for the "Fox Wedding Ceremony" ({{ill|Kitsune no yomeiri|lt=Fox Wedding|ja|狐の嫁入り}}).
The grounds also include multiple Inari shrines, {{ill|Tenjinja|lt=Tenjin shrines|ja|天神社}}, and unique features such as Japan’s largest statue of {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}}.
== Enshrined Deities == === Shinmei Shrine === {{Div col}} * {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|lt=Amenokoyane-no-Mikoto|ja|天児屋根命}} * {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神}} * {{ill|Takemikazuchi|lt=Takemikazuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|建御雷命}} * {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}} * {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚尊}} * {{ill|Ukanomitama|lt-Ukanomitama-no-Kami|ja|倉稲魂命}} * {{ill|Oyamakui no Kami|lt=Ōyamakui-no-Kami|ja|大山祇神}} * {{ill|Kagu-tsuchi|lt=Kagutsuchi-no-Kami|ja|火之迦具土神}} * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}} * {{ill|Kagu-tsuchi|lt=Ho-musubi-no-Kami|ja|火産霊神}} * {{ill|Ukemochi|lt=Ukemochi-no-Kami|ja|保食神}} * {{ill|Yamatohime-no-mikoto|lt=Yamatohime-no-Mikoto|ja|倭姫命|1=Yamatohime-no-mikoto}} {{Div col end}}
=== Inari Shrine === * {{ill|Ukanomitama|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Kami|ja|倉稲魂命}} * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|lt=Ōkuninushi-no-Kami|ja|大己貴命}} * {{ill|Ōta-no-Kami|ja|大田命}} * {{ill|Ukemochi|lt=Ukemochi-no-Kami|ja|保食神}} * {{ill|Ōmiyahime-no-Mikoto|lt=Ōmiyanome-no-Kami|ja|大宮能売神}}
== History == The shrine is traditionally said to have been founded in [[876]] (the 18th year of the {{ill|Jōgan era|ja|貞観}}). During the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}, the area was coastal and formed a sandbar called "Suzaki." Thus, the shrine became known as "Hama-no-Miya" (Shrine of the Beach).
The exact date of the establishment of the {{ill|Inari Shrine|ja|稲荷神社}} is unknown.
== Shrine Buildings and Grounds == The grounds include the {{ill|Miyamado Inari Shrine|ja|海山道稲荷神社}}, which surpasses the Shinmei Shrine in scale.
There are over 20 subsidiary {{ill|Inari shrines|ja|稲荷神社}}, a {{ill|Tenjin shrine|ja|天神社}} housing Japan’s largest statue of {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}}, and various monuments such as the Pen Mound, Eyeglass Mound, and Fox Mound.
== Festivals and Events == * festival: October 3–5 * Inari Grand Festival: April 8–9 * Fox Wedding Ceremony: Held on {{ill|Setsubun|ja|節分}} (February), this purification ritual involves a mock wedding procession. Participants representing the bride and groom wear fox masks, and a "bean-throwing" event is also held.
== location == 1-62 Miyamado Town, [[Yokkaichi]], [[Mie Prefecture]], Japan
== Access == * A short walk from [[Kintetsu Nagoya Line]]'s [[Miyamado Station]].
== See Also == {{Commonscat|Miyamado-jinja|Miyamado Shrine}} * {{ill|Shinmei Shrine|ja|神明神社}} * {{ill|Inari Shrine|ja|稲荷神社}}

== History == The shrine is traditionally said to have been founded in [[876]] (the 18th year of the {{ill|Jōgan era|ja|貞観}}). During the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}, the area was coastal and formed a sandbar called "Suzaki." Thus, the shrine became known as "Hama-no-Miya" (Shrine of the Beach).
The exact date of the establishment of the {{ill|Inari Shrine|ja|稲荷神社}} is unknown.
== Shrine Buildings and Grounds == The grounds include the {{ill|Miyamado Inari Shrine|ja|海山道稲荷神社}}, which surpasses the Shinmei Shrine in scale.
There are over 20 subsidiary {{ill|Inari shrines|ja|稲荷神社}}, a {{ill|Tenjin shrine|ja|天神社}} housing Japan’s largest statue of {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}}, and various monuments such as the Pen Mound, Eyeglass Mound, and Fox Mound.
== Festivals and Events == * festival: October 3–5 * Inari Grand Festival: April 8–9 * Fox Wedding Ceremony: Held on {{ill|Setsubun|ja|節分}} (February), this purification ritual involves a mock wedding procession. Participants representing the bride and groom wear fox masks, and a "bean-throwing" event is also held.
== location == 1-62 Miyamado Town, [[Yokkaichi]], [[Mie Prefecture]], Japan
== Access == * A short walk from [[Kintetsu Nagoya Line]]'s [[Miyamado Station]].
== See Also == {{Commonscat|Miyamado-jinja|Miyamado Shrine}} * {{ill|Shinmei Shrine|ja|神明神社}} * {{ill|Inari Shrine|ja|稲荷神社}}

{{Infobox religious building | name = Suzaki Hamamiya Shinmei Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Suzakihamamiya(Mie).JPG|250px]] | location = 1-62 Miyamado Town, Yokkaichi, Mie Prefecture, Japan | latitude = 34.9428 | longitude = 136.6217 | deity = {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神}} and others | founded = [[876]] (18th year of the {{ill|Jōgan era|ja|貞観}}) | main_building_style = | festivals = October 3–5 (festival); February (Fox Wedding Ceremony) | map_type = Japan Mie }}
'''Suzaki Hamamiya Shinmei Shrine''' ({{ill|Suzaki Hamamiya Shinmei Shrine|ja|洲崎濱宮神明神社}}, ''Suzaki Hamamiya Shinmei Jinja'') is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in the [[Shiobama District]], [[Yokkaichi]], [[Mie Prefecture]], Japan. It is often collectively referred to as "Miyamado Shrine" due to the large presence of its {{ill|Inari Shrine|ja|稲荷神社}}.
== Overview == [[File:Miyamado-inari(Mie).JPG|thumb|Miyamado Inari Shrine]] The shrine's name "濱" (''Hama'') is also written as "浜" or "濵."
The subordinate shrine, known as '''Miyamado Inari Shrine''' ({{ill|Miyamado Inari Shrine|ja|海山道稲荷神社}}), is larger than the main Shinmei Shrine and is famous for the "Fox Wedding Ceremony" ({{ill|Kitsune no yomeiri|lt=Fox Wedding|ja|狐の嫁入り}}).
The grounds also include multiple Inari shrines, {{ill|Tenjinja|lt=Tenjin shrines|ja|天神社}}, and unique features such as Japan’s largest statue of {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}}.
== Enshrined Deities == === Shinmei Shrine === {{Div col}} * {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|lt=Amenokoyane-no-Mikoto|ja|天児屋根命}} * {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神}} * {{ill|Takemikazuchi|lt=Takemikazuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|建御雷命}} * {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}} * {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚尊}} * {{ill|Ukanomitama|lt-Ukanomitama-no-Kami|ja|倉稲魂命}} * {{ill|Oyamakui no Kami|lt=Ōyamakui-no-Kami|ja|大山祇神}} * {{ill|Kagu-tsuchi|lt=Kagutsuchi-no-Kami|ja|火之迦具土神}} * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}} * {{ill|Kagu-tsuchi|lt=Ho-musubi-no-Kami|ja|火産霊神}} * {{ill|Ukemochi|lt=Ukemochi-no-Kami|ja|保食神}} * {{ill|Yamatohime-no-mikoto|lt=Yamatohime-no-Mikoto|ja|倭姫命|1=Yamatohime-no-mikoto}} {{Div col end}}
=== Inari Shrine === * {{ill|Ukanomitama|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Kami|ja|倉稲魂命}} * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|lt=Ōkuninushi-no-Kami|ja|大己貴命}} * {{ill|Ōta-no-Kami|ja|大田命}} * {{ill|Ukemochi|lt=Ukemochi-no-Kami|ja|保食神}} * {{ill|Ōmiyahime-no-Mikoto|lt=Ōmiyanome-no-Kami|ja|大宮能売神}}
== History == The shrine is traditionally said to have been founded in [[876]] (the 18th year of the {{ill|Jōgan era|ja|貞観}}). During the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}, the area was coastal and formed a sandbar called "Suzaki." Thus, the shrine became known as "Hama-no-Miya" (Shrine of the Beach).
The exact date of the establishment of the {{ill|Inari Shrine|ja|稲荷神社}} is unknown.
== Shrine Buildings and Grounds == The grounds include the {{ill|Miyamado Inari Shrine|ja|海山道稲荷神社}}, which surpasses the Shinmei Shrine in scale.
There are over 20 subsidiary {{ill|Inari shrines|ja|稲荷神社}}, a {{ill|Tenjin shrine|ja|天神社}} housing Japan’s largest statue of {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}}, and various monuments such as the Pen Mound, Eyeglass Mound, and Fox Mound.
== Festivals and Events == * festival: October 3–5 * Inari Grand Festival: April 8–9 * Fox Wedding Ceremony: Held on {{ill|Setsubun|ja|節分}} (February), this purification ritual involves a mock wedding procession. Participants representing the bride and groom wear fox masks, and a "bean-throwing" event is also held.
