{{Infobox religious building | name = Suzaki Hamamiya Shinmei Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Suzakihamamiya(Mie).JPG|250px]] | location = 1-62 Miyamado Town, Yokkaichi, Mie Prefecture, Japan | latitude = 34.9428 | longitude = 136.6217 | deity = {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神}} and others | founded = [[876]] (18th year of the {{ill|Jōgan era|ja|貞観}}) | main_building_style = | festivals = October 3–5 (festival); February (Fox Wedding Ceremony) | map_type = Japan Mie }}
'''Suzaki Hamamiya Shinmei Shrine''' ({{ill|Suzaki Hamamiya Shinmei Shrine|ja|洲崎濱宮神明神社}}, ''Suzaki Hamamiya Shinmei Jinja'') is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in the [[Shiobama District]], [[Yokkaichi]], [[Mie Prefecture]], Japan. It is often collectively referred to as "Miyamado Shrine" due to the large presence of its {{ill|Inari Shrine|ja|稲荷神社}}.
== Overview == [[File:Miyamado-inari(Mie).JPG|thumb|Miyamado Inari Shrine]] The shrine's name "濱" (''Hama'') is also written as "浜" or "濵."
The subordinate shrine, known as '''Miyamado Inari Shrine''' ({{ill|Miyamado Inari Shrine|ja|海山道稲荷神社}}), is larger than the main Shinmei Shrine and is famous for the "Fox Wedding Ceremony" ({{ill|Kitsune no yomeiri|lt=Fox Wedding|ja|狐の嫁入り}}).
The grounds also include multiple Inari shrines, {{ill|Tenjinja|lt=Tenjin shrines|ja|天神社}}, and unique features such as Japan’s largest statue of {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane}}.
== Enshrined Deities == === Shinmei Shrine === {{Div col}} * {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|lt=Amenokoyane-no-Mikoto|ja|天児屋根命|1=Ame-no-Koyane}} * {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神}} * {{ill|Takemikazuchi|lt=Takemikazuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|建御雷命}} * {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}} * {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚尊}} * {{ill|Ukanomitama|lt-Ukanomitama-no-Kami|ja|倉稲魂命}} * {{ill|Oyamakui no Kami|lt=Ōyamakui-no-Kami|ja|大山祇神}} * {{ill|Kagu-tsuchi|lt=Kagutsuchi-no-Kami|ja|火之迦具土神}} * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane}} * {{ill|Kagu-tsuchi|lt=Ho-musubi-no-Kami|ja|火産霊神}} * {{ill|Ukemochi|lt=Ukemochi-no-Kami|ja|保食神}} * {{ill|Yamatohime-no-mikoto|lt=Yamatohime-no-Mikoto|ja|倭姫命|1=Yamatohime-no-mikoto}} {{Div col end}}
=== Inari Shrine === * {{ill|Ukanomitama|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Kami|ja|倉稲魂命}} * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|lt=Ōkuninushi-no-Kami|ja|大己貴命}} * {{ill|Ōta-no-Kami|ja|大田命}} * {{ill|Ukemochi|lt=Ukemochi-no-Kami|ja|保食神}} * {{ill|Ōmiyahime-no-Mikoto|lt=Ōmiyanome-no-Kami|ja|大宮能売神}}
== History == The shrine is traditionally said to have been founded in [[876]] (the 18th year of the {{ill|Jōgan era|ja|貞観}}). During the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}, the area was coastal and formed a sandbar called "Suzaki." Thus, the shrine became known as "Hama-no-Miya" (Shrine of the Beach).
The exact date of the establishment of the {{ill|Inari Shrine|ja|稲荷神社}} is unknown.
== Shrine Buildings and Grounds == The grounds include the {{ill|Miyamado Inari Shrine|ja|海山道稲荷神社}}, which surpasses the Shinmei Shrine in scale.
There are over 20 subsidiary {{ill|Inari shrines|ja|稲荷神社}}, a {{ill|Tenjin shrine|ja|天神社}} housing Japan’s largest statue of {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane}}, and various monuments such as the Pen Mound, Eyeglass Mound, and Fox Mound.
== Festivals and Events == * festival: October 3–5 * Inari Grand Festival: April 8–9 * Fox Wedding Ceremony: Held on {{ill|Setsubun|ja|節分}} (February), this purification ritual involves a mock wedding procession. Participants representing the bride and groom wear fox masks, and a "bean-throwing" event is also held.
== location == 1-62 Miyamado Town, [[Yokkaichi]], [[Mie Prefecture]], Japan
== Access == * A short walk from [[Kintetsu Nagoya Line]]'s [[Miyamado Station]].== See Also == {{Commonscat|Miyamado-jinja|Miyamado Shrine}} * {{ill|Shinmei Shrine|ja|神明神社}} * {{ill|Inari Shrine|ja|稲荷神社}}

{{Infobox religious building | name = Suzaki Hamamiya Shinmei Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Suzakihamamiya(Mie).JPG|250px]] | location = 1-62 Miyamado Town, Yokkaichi, Mie Prefecture, Japan | latitude = 34.9428 | longitude = 136.6217 | deity = {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神}} and others | founded = [[876]] (18th year of the {{ill|Jōgan era|ja|貞観}}) | main_building_style = | festivals = October 3–5 (festival); February (Fox Wedding Ceremony) | map_type = Japan Mie }}
'''Suzaki Hamamiya Shinmei Shrine''' ({{ill|Suzaki Hamamiya Shinmei Shrine|ja|洲崎濱宮神明神社}}, ''Suzaki Hamamiya Shinmei Jinja'') is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in the [[Shiobama District]], [[Yokkaichi]], [[Mie Prefecture]], Japan. It is often collectively referred to as "Miyamado Shrine" due to the large presence of its {{ill|Inari Shrine|ja|稲荷神社}}.
== Overview == [[File:Miyamado-inari(Mie).JPG|thumb|Miyamado Inari Shrine]] The shrine's name "濱" (''Hama'') is also written as "浜" or "濵."
The subordinate shrine, known as '''Miyamado Inari Shrine''' ({{ill|Miyamado Inari Shrine|ja|海山道稲荷神社}}), is larger than the main Shinmei Shrine and is famous for the "Fox Wedding Ceremony" ({{ill|Kitsune no yomeiri|lt=Fox Wedding|ja|狐の嫁入り}}).
The grounds also include multiple Inari shrines, {{ill|Tenjinja|lt=Tenjin shrines|ja|天神社}}, and unique features such as Japan’s largest statue of {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane}}.
== Enshrined Deities == === Shinmei Shrine === {{Div col}} * {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|lt=Amenokoyane-no-Mikoto|ja|天児屋根命|1=Ame-no-Koyane}} * {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神}} * {{ill|Takemikazuchi|lt=Takemikazuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|建御雷命}} * {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}} * {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚尊}} * {{ill|Ukanomitama|lt-Ukanomitama-no-Kami|ja|倉稲魂命}} * {{ill|Oyamakui no Kami|lt=Ōyamakui-no-Kami|ja|大山祇神}} * {{ill|Kagu-tsuchi|lt=Kagutsuchi-no-Kami|ja|火之迦具土神}} * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane}} * {{ill|Kagu-tsuchi|lt=Ho-musubi-no-Kami|ja|火産霊神}} * {{ill|Ukemochi|lt=Ukemochi-no-Kami|ja|保食神}} * {{ill|Yamatohime-no-mikoto|lt=Yamatohime-no-Mikoto|ja|倭姫命|1=Yamatohime-no-mikoto}} {{Div col end}}
=== Inari Shrine === * {{ill|Ukanomitama|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Kami|ja|倉稲魂命}} * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|lt=Ōkuninushi-no-Kami|ja|大己貴命}} * {{ill|Ōta-no-Kami|ja|大田命}} * {{ill|Ukemochi|lt=Ukemochi-no-Kami|ja|保食神}} * {{ill|Ōmiyahime-no-Mikoto|lt=Ōmiyanome-no-Kami|ja|大宮能売神}}
== History == The shrine is traditionally said to have been founded in [[876]] (the 18th year of the {{ill|Jōgan era|ja|貞観}}). During the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}, the area was coastal and formed a sandbar called "Suzaki." Thus, the shrine became known as "Hama-no-Miya" (Shrine of the Beach).
The exact date of the establishment of the {{ill|Inari Shrine|ja|稲荷神社}} is unknown.
== Shrine Buildings and Grounds == The grounds include the {{ill|Miyamado Inari Shrine|ja|海山道稲荷神社}}, which surpasses the Shinmei Shrine in scale.
There are over 20 subsidiary {{ill|Inari shrines|ja|稲荷神社}}, a {{ill|Tenjin shrine|ja|天神社}} housing Japan’s largest statue of {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane}}, and various monuments such as the Pen Mound, Eyeglass Mound, and Fox Mound.
== Festivals and Events == * festival: October 3–5 * Inari Grand Festival: April 8–9 * Fox Wedding Ceremony: Held on {{ill|Setsubun|ja|節分}} (February), this purification ritual involves a mock wedding procession. Participants representing the bride and groom wear fox masks, and a "bean-throwing" event is also held.
== location == 1-62 Miyamado Town, [[Yokkaichi]], [[Mie Prefecture]], Japan

revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building | name = Suzaki Hamamiya Shinmei Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Suzakihamamiya(Mie).JPG|250px]] | location = 1-62 Miyamado Town, Yokkaichi, Mie Prefecture, Japan | latitude = 34.9428 | longitude = 136.6217 | deity = {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神}} and others | founded = [[876]] (18th year of the {{ill|Jōgan era|ja|貞観}}) | main_building_style = | festivals = October 3–5 (festival); February (Fox Wedding Ceremony) | map_type = Japan Mie }}
'''Suzaki Hamamiya Shinmei Shrine''' ({{ill|Suzaki Hamamiya Shinmei Shrine|ja|洲崎濱宮神明神社}}, ''Suzaki Hamamiya Shinmei Jinja'') is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in the [[Shiobama District]], [[Yokkaichi]], [[Mie Prefecture]], Japan. It is often collectively referred to as "Miyamado Shrine" due to the large presence of its {{ill|Inari Shrine|ja|稲荷神社}}.
== Overview == [[File:Miyamado-inari(Mie).JPG|thumb|Miyamado Inari Shrine]] The shrine's name "濱" (''Hama'') is also written as "浜" or "濵."
The subordinate shrine, known as '''Miyamado Inari Shrine''' ({{ill|Miyamado Inari Shrine|ja|海山道稲荷神社}}), is larger than the main Shinmei Shrine and is famous for the "Fox Wedding Ceremony" ({{ill|Kitsune no yomeiri|lt=Fox Wedding|ja|狐の嫁入り}}).
The grounds also include multiple Inari shrines, {{ill|Tenjinja|lt=Tenjin shrines|ja|天神社}}, and unique features such as Japan’s largest statue of {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane}}.
== Enshrined Deities == === Shinmei Shrine === {{Div col}} * {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|lt=Amenokoyane-no-Mikoto|ja|天児屋根命|1=Ame-no-Koyane}} * {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神}} * {{ill|Takemikazuchi|lt=Takemikazuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|建御雷命}} * {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}} * {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚尊}} * {{ill|Ukanomitama|lt-Ukanomitama-no-Kami|ja|倉稲魂命}} * {{ill|Oyamakui no Kami|lt=Ōyamakui-no-Kami|ja|大山祇神}} * {{ill|Kagu-tsuchi|lt=Kagutsuchi-no-Kami|ja|火之迦具土神}} * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane}} * {{ill|Kagu-tsuchi|lt=Ho-musubi-no-Kami|ja|火産霊神}} * {{ill|Ukemochi|lt=Ukemochi-no-Kami|ja|保食神}} * {{ill|Yamatohime-no-mikoto|lt=Yamatohime-no-Mikoto|ja|倭姫命|1=Yamatohime-no-mikoto}} {{Div col end}}
=== Inari Shrine === * {{ill|Ukanomitama|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Kami|ja|倉稲魂命}} * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|lt=Ōkuninushi-no-Kami|ja|大己貴命}} * {{ill|Ōta-no-Kami|ja|大田命}} * {{ill|Ukemochi|lt=Ukemochi-no-Kami|ja|保食神}} * {{ill|Ōmiyahime-no-Mikoto|lt=Ōmiyanome-no-Kami|ja|大宮能売神}}
== History == The shrine is traditionally said to have been founded in [[876]] (the 18th year of the {{ill|Jōgan era|ja|貞観}}). During the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}, the area was coastal and formed a sandbar called "Suzaki." Thus, the shrine became known as "Hama-no-Miya" (Shrine of the Beach).
The exact date of the establishment of the {{ill|Inari Shrine|ja|稲荷神社}} is unknown.
== Shrine Buildings and Grounds == The grounds include the {{ill|Miyamado Inari Shrine|ja|海山道稲荷神社}}, which surpasses the Shinmei Shrine in scale.
There are over 20 subsidiary {{ill|Inari shrines|ja|稲荷神社}}, a {{ill|Tenjin shrine|ja|天神社}} housing Japan’s largest statue of {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane}}, and various monuments such as the Pen Mound, Eyeglass Mound, and Fox Mound.
== Festivals and Events == * festival: October 3–5 * Inari Grand Festival: April 8–9 * Fox Wedding Ceremony: Held on {{ill|Setsubun|ja|節分}} (February), this purification ritual involves a mock wedding procession. Participants representing the bride and groom wear fox masks, and a "bean-throwing" event is also held.
== location == 1-62 Miyamado Town, [[Yokkaichi]], [[Mie Prefecture]], Japan
== Access == * A short walk from [[Kintetsu Nagoya Line]]'s [[Miyamado Station]]. == See Also! == {{Commonscat|Miyamado-jinja|Miyamado Shrine}} * {{ill|Shinmei Shrine|ja|神明神社}} * {{ill|Inari Shrine|ja|稲荷神社}}

{{Infobox religious building | name = Suzaki Hamamiya Shinmei Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Suzakihamamiya(Mie).JPG|250px]] | location = 1-62 Miyamado Town, Yokkaichi, Mie Prefecture, Japan | latitude = 34.9428 | longitude = 136.6217 | deity = {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神}} and others | founded = [[876]] (18th year of the {{ill|Jōgan era|ja|貞観}}) | main_building_style = | festivals = October 3–5 (festival); February (Fox Wedding Ceremony) | map_type = Japan Mie }}
'''Suzaki Hamamiya Shinmei Shrine''' ({{ill|Suzaki Hamamiya Shinmei Shrine|ja|洲崎濱宮神明神社}}, ''Suzaki Hamamiya Shinmei Jinja'') is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in the [[Shiobama District]], [[Yokkaichi]], [[Mie Prefecture]], Japan. It is often collectively referred to as "Miyamado Shrine" due to the large presence of its {{ill|Inari Shrine|ja|稲荷神社}}.
== Overview == [[File:Miyamado-inari(Mie).JPG|thumb|Miyamado Inari Shrine]] The shrine's name "濱" (''Hama'') is also written as "浜" or "濵."
The subordinate shrine, known as '''Miyamado Inari Shrine''' ({{ill|Miyamado Inari Shrine|ja|海山道稲荷神社}}), is larger than the main Shinmei Shrine and is famous for the "Fox Wedding Ceremony" ({{ill|Kitsune no yomeiri|lt=Fox Wedding|ja|狐の嫁入り}}).
The grounds also include multiple Inari shrines, {{ill|Tenjinja|lt=Tenjin shrines|ja|天神社}}, and unique features such as Japan’s largest statue of {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane}}.
== Enshrined Deities == === Shinmei Shrine === {{Div col}} * {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|lt=Amenokoyane-no-Mikoto|ja|天児屋根命|1=Ame-no-Koyane}} * {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神}} * {{ill|Takemikazuchi|lt=Takemikazuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|建御雷命}} * {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}} * {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚尊}} * {{ill|Ukanomitama|lt-Ukanomitama-no-Kami|ja|倉稲魂命}} * {{ill|Oyamakui no Kami|lt=Ōyamakui-no-Kami|ja|大山祇神}} * {{ill|Kagu-tsuchi|lt=Kagutsuchi-no-Kami|ja|火之迦具土神}} * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane}} * {{ill|Kagu-tsuchi|lt=Ho-musubi-no-Kami|ja|火産霊神}} * {{ill|Ukemochi|lt=Ukemochi-no-Kami|ja|保食神}} * {{ill|Yamatohime-no-mikoto|lt=Yamatohime-no-Mikoto|ja|倭姫命|1=Yamatohime-no-mikoto}} {{Div col end}}
=== Inari Shrine === * {{ill|Ukanomitama|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Kami|ja|倉稲魂命}} * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|lt=Ōkuninushi-no-Kami|ja|大己貴命}} * {{ill|Ōta-no-Kami|ja|大田命}} * {{ill|Ukemochi|lt=Ukemochi-no-Kami|ja|保食神}} * {{ill|Ōmiyahime-no-Mikoto|lt=Ōmiyanome-no-Kami|ja|大宮能売神}}
== History == The shrine is traditionally said to have been founded in [[876]] (the 18th year of the {{ill|Jōgan era|ja|貞観}}). During the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}, the area was coastal and formed a sandbar called "Suzaki." Thus, the shrine became known as "Hama-no-Miya" (Shrine of the Beach).
The exact date of the establishment of the {{ill|Inari Shrine|ja|稲荷神社}} is unknown.
== Shrine Buildings and Grounds == The grounds include the {{ill|Miyamado Inari Shrine|ja|海山道稲荷神社}}, which surpasses the Shinmei Shrine in scale.
There are over 20 subsidiary {{ill|Inari shrines|ja|稲荷神社}}, a {{ill|Tenjin shrine|ja|天神社}} housing Japan’s largest statue of {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane}}, and various monuments such as the Pen Mound, Eyeglass Mound, and Fox Mound.
== Festivals and Events == * festival: October 3–5 * Inari Grand Festival: April 8–9 * Fox Wedding Ceremony: Held on {{ill|Setsubun|ja|節分}} (February), this purification ritual involves a mock wedding procession. Participants representing the bride and groom wear fox masks, and a "bean-throwing" event is also held.
== location == 1-62 Miyamado Town, [[Yokkaichi]], [[Mie Prefecture]], Japan
== Access == * A short walk from [[Kintetsu Nagoya Line]]'s [[Miyamado Station]]. == See Also == {{Commonscat|Miyamado-jinja|Miyamado Shrine}} * {{ill|Shinmei Shrine|ja|神明神社}} * {{ill|Inari Shrine|ja|稲荷神社}}

{{Infobox religious building | name = Suzaki Hamamiya Shinmei Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Suzakihamamiya(Mie).JPG|250px]] | location = 1-62 Miyamado Town, Yokkaichi, Mie Prefecture, Japan | latitude = 34.9428 | longitude = 136.6217 | deity = {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神}} and others | founded = [[876]] (18th year of the {{ill|Jōgan era|ja|貞観}}) | main_building_style = | festivals = October 3–5 (festival); February (Fox Wedding Ceremony) | map_type = Japan Mie }}
'''Suzaki Hamamiya Shinmei Shrine''' ({{ill|Suzaki Hamamiya Shinmei Shrine|ja|洲崎濱宮神明神社}}, ''Suzaki Hamamiya Shinmei Jinja'') is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in the [[Shiobama District]], [[Yokkaichi]], [[Mie Prefecture]], Japan. It is often collectively referred to as "Miyamado Shrine" due to the large presence of its {{ill|Inari Shrine|ja|稲荷神社}}.
== Overview == [[File:Miyamado-inari(Mie).JPG|thumb|Miyamado Inari Shrine]] The shrine's name "濱" (''Hama'') is also written as "浜" or "濵."
The subordinate shrine, known as '''Miyamado Inari Shrine''' ({{ill|Miyamado Inari Shrine|ja|海山道稲荷神社}}), is larger than the main Shinmei Shrine and is famous for the "Fox Wedding Ceremony" ({{ill|Kitsune no yomeiri|lt=Fox Wedding|ja|狐の嫁入り}}).
The grounds also include multiple Inari shrines, {{ill|Tenjinja|lt=Tenjin shrines|ja|天神社}}, and unique features such as Japan’s largest statue of {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane}}.
== Enshrined Deities == === Shinmei Shrine === {{Div col}} * {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|lt=Amenokoyane-no-Mikoto|ja|天児屋根命|1=Ame-no-Koyane}} * {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神}} * {{ill|Takemikazuchi|lt=Takemikazuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|建御雷命}} * {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}} * {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚尊}} * {{ill|Ukanomitama|lt-Ukanomitama-no-Kami|ja|倉稲魂命}} * {{ill|Oyamakui no Kami|lt=Ōyamakui-no-Kami|ja|大山祇神}} * {{ill|Kagu-tsuchi|lt=Kagutsuchi-no-Kami|ja|火之迦具土神}} * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane}} * {{ill|Kagu-tsuchi|lt=Ho-musubi-no-Kami|ja|火産霊神}} * {{ill|Ukemochi|lt=Ukemochi-no-Kami|ja|保食神}} * {{ill|Yamatohime-no-mikoto|lt=Yamatohime-no-Mikoto|ja|倭姫命|1=Yamatohime-no-mikoto}} {{Div col end}}
=== Inari Shrine === * {{ill|Ukanomitama|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Kami|ja|倉稲魂命}} * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|lt=Ōkuninushi-no-Kami|ja|大己貴命}} * {{ill|Ōta-no-Kami|ja|大田命}} * {{ill|Ukemochi|lt=Ukemochi-no-Kami|ja|保食神}} * {{ill|Ōmiyahime-no-Mikoto|lt=Ōmiyanome-no-Kami|ja|大宮能売神}}
== History == The shrine is traditionally said to have been founded in [[876]] (the 18th year of the {{ill|Jōgan era|ja|貞観}}). During the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}, the area was coastal and formed a sandbar called "Suzaki." Thus, the shrine became known as "Hama-no-Miya" (Shrine of the Beach).
The exact date of the establishment of the {{ill|Inari Shrine|ja|稲荷神社}} is unknown.
== Shrine Buildings and Grounds == The grounds include the {{ill|Miyamado Inari Shrine|ja|海山道稲荷神社}}, which surpasses the Shinmei Shrine in scale.
There are over 20 subsidiary {{ill|Inari shrines|ja|稲荷神社}}, a {{ill|Tenjin shrine|ja|天神社}} housing Japan’s largest statue of {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane}}, and various monuments such as the Pen Mound, Eyeglass Mound, and Fox Mound.
== Festivals and Events == * festival: October 3–5 * Inari Grand Festival: April 8–9 * Fox Wedding Ceremony: Held on {{ill|Setsubun|ja|節分}} (February), this purification ritual involves a mock wedding procession. Participants representing the bride and groom wear fox masks, and a "bean-throwing" event is also held.
== location == 1-62 Miyamado Town, [[Yokkaichi]], [[Mie Prefecture]], Japan
== Access == * A short walk from [[Kintetsu Nagoya Line]]'s [[Miyamado Station]]. == See Also == {{Commonscat|Miyamado-jinja|Miyamado Shrine}} * {{ill|Shinmei Shrine|ja|神明神社}} * {{ill|Inari Shrine|ja|稲荷神社}}

{{Infobox religious building | name = Suzaki Hamamiya Shinmei Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Suzakihamamiya(Mie).JPG|250px]] | location = 1-62 Miyamado Town, Yokkaichi, Mie Prefecture, Japan | latitude = 34.9428 | longitude = 136.6217 | deity = {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神}} and others | founded = [[876]] (18th year of the {{ill|Jōgan era|ja|貞観}}) | main_building_style = | festivals = October 3–5 (festival); February (Fox Wedding Ceremony) | map_type = Japan Mie }}
'''Suzaki Hamamiya Shinmei Shrine''' ({{ill|Suzaki Hamamiya Shinmei Shrine|ja|洲崎濱宮神明神社}}, ''Suzaki Hamamiya Shinmei Jinja'') is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in the [[Shiobama District]], [[Yokkaichi]], [[Mie Prefecture]], Japan. It is often collectively referred to as "Miyamado Shrine" due to the large presence of its {{ill|Inari Shrine|ja|稲荷神社}}.
== Overview == [[File:Miyamado-inari(Mie).JPG|thumb|Miyamado Inari Shrine]] The shrine's name "濱" (''Hama'') is also written as "浜" or "濵."
The subordinate shrine, known as '''Miyamado Inari Shrine''' ({{ill|Miyamado Inari Shrine|ja|海山道稲荷神社}}), is larger than the main Shinmei Shrine and is famous for the "Fox Wedding Ceremony" ({{ill|Kitsune no yomeiri|lt=Fox Wedding|ja|狐の嫁入り}}).
The grounds also include multiple Inari shrines, {{ill|Tenjinja|lt=Tenjin shrines|ja|天神社}}, and unique features such as Japan’s largest statue of {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane}}.
== Enshrined Deities == === Shinmei Shrine === {{Div col}} * {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|lt=Amenokoyane-no-Mikoto|ja|天児屋根命|1=Ame-no-Koyane}} * {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神}} * {{ill|Takemikazuchi|lt=Takemikazuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|建御雷命}} * {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}} * {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚尊}} * {{ill|Ukanomitama|lt-Ukanomitama-no-Kami|ja|倉稲魂命}} * {{ill|Oyamakui no Kami|lt=Ōyamakui-no-Kami|ja|大山祇神}} * {{ill|Kagu-tsuchi|lt=Kagutsuchi-no-Kami|ja|火之迦具土神}} * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane}} * {{ill|Kagu-tsuchi|lt=Ho-musubi-no-Kami|ja|火産霊神}} * {{ill|Ukemochi|lt=Ukemochi-no-Kami|ja|保食神}} * {{ill|Yamatohime-no-mikoto|lt=Yamatohime-no-Mikoto|ja|倭姫命|1=Yamatohime-no-mikoto}} {{Div col end}}
=== Inari Shrine === * {{ill|Ukanomitama|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Kami|ja|倉稲魂命}} * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|lt=Ōkuninushi-no-Kami|ja|大己貴命}} * {{ill|Ōta-no-Kami|ja|大田命}} * {{ill|Ukemochi|lt=Ukemochi-no-Kami|ja|保食神}} * {{ill|Ōmiyahime-no-Mikoto|lt=Ōmiyanome-no-Kami|ja|大宮能売神}}
== History == The shrine is traditionally said to have been founded in [[876]] (the 18th year of the {{ill|Jōgan era|ja|貞観}}). During the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}, the area was coastal and formed a sandbar called "Suzaki." Thus, the shrine became known as "Hama-no-Miya" (Shrine of the Beach).
The exact date of the establishment of the {{ill|Inari Shrine|ja|稲荷神社}} is unknown.
== Shrine Buildings and Grounds == The grounds include the {{ill|Miyamado Inari Shrine|ja|海山道稲荷神社}}, which surpasses the Shinmei Shrine in scale.
There are over 20 subsidiary {{ill|Inari shrines|ja|稲荷神社}}, a {{ill|Tenjin shrine|ja|天神社}} housing Japan’s largest statue of {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane}}, and various monuments such as the Pen Mound, Eyeglass Mound, and Fox Mound.
== Festivals and Events == * festival: October 3–5 * Inari Grand Festival: April 8–9 * Fox Wedding Ceremony: Held on {{ill|Setsubun|ja|節分}} (February), this purification ritual involves a mock wedding procession. Participants representing the bride and groom wear fox masks, and a "bean-throwing" event is also held.
== location == 1-62 Miyamado Town, [[Yokkaichi]], [[Mie Prefecture]], Japan
== Access == * A short walk from [[Kintetsu Nagoya Line]]'s [[Miyamado Station]]. == See Also == {{Commonscat|Miyamado-jinja|Miyamado Shrine}} * {{ill|Shinmei Shrine|ja|神明神社}} * {{ill|Inari Shrine|ja|稲荷神社}}

{{Infobox religious building | name = Suzaki Hamamiya Shinmei Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Suzakihamamiya(Mie).JPG|250px]] | location = 1-62 Miyamado Town, Yokkaichi, Mie Prefecture, Japan | latitude = 34.9428 | longitude = 136.6217 | deity = {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神}} and others | founded = [[876]] (18th year of the {{ill|Jōgan era|ja|貞観}}) | main_building_style = | festivals = October 3–5 (festival); February (Fox Wedding Ceremony) | map_type = Japan Mie }}
'''Suzaki Hamamiya Shinmei Shrine''' ({{ill|Suzaki Hamamiya Shinmei Shrine|ja|洲崎濱宮神明神社}}, ''Suzaki Hamamiya Shinmei Jinja'') is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in the [[Shiobama District]], [[Yokkaichi]], [[Mie Prefecture]], Japan. It is often collectively referred to as "Miyamado Shrine" due to the large presence of its {{ill|Inari Shrine|ja|稲荷神社}}.
== Overview == [[File:Miyamado-inari(Mie).JPG|thumb|Miyamado Inari Shrine]] The shrine's name "濱" (''Hama'') is also written as "浜" or "濵."
The subordinate shrine, known as '''Miyamado Inari Shrine''' ({{ill|Miyamado Inari Shrine|ja|海山道稲荷神社}}), is larger than the main Shinmei Shrine and is famous for the "Fox Wedding Ceremony" ({{ill|Kitsune no yomeiri|lt=Fox Wedding|ja|狐の嫁入り}}).
The grounds also include multiple Inari shrines, {{ill|Tenjinja|lt=Tenjin shrines|ja|天神社}}, and unique features such as Japan’s largest statue of {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane}}.
== Enshrined Deities == === Shinmei Shrine === {{Div col}} * {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|lt=Amenokoyane-no-Mikoto|ja|天児屋根命|1=Ame-no-Koyane}} * {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神}} * {{ill|Takemikazuchi|lt=Takemikazuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|建御雷命}} * {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}} * {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚尊}} * {{ill|Ukanomitama|lt-Ukanomitama-no-Kami|ja|倉稲魂命}} * {{ill|Oyamakui no Kami|lt=Ōyamakui-no-Kami|ja|大山祇神}} * {{ill|Kagu-tsuchi|lt=Kagutsuchi-no-Kami|ja|火之迦具土神}} * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane}} * {{ill|Kagu-tsuchi|lt=Ho-musubi-no-Kami|ja|火産霊神}} * {{ill|Ukemochi|lt=Ukemochi-no-Kami|ja|保食神}} * {{ill|Yamatohime-no-mikoto|lt=Yamatohime-no-Mikoto|ja|倭姫命|1=Yamatohime-no-mikoto}} {{Div col end}}
=== Inari Shrine === * {{ill|Ukanomitama|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Kami|ja|倉稲魂命}} * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|lt=Ōkuninushi-no-Kami|ja|大己貴命}} * {{ill|Ōta-no-Kami|ja|大田命}} * {{ill|Ukemochi|lt=Ukemochi-no-Kami|ja|保食神}} * {{ill|Ōmiyahime-no-Mikoto|lt=Ōmiyanome-no-Kami|ja|大宮能売神}}
== History == The shrine is traditionally said to have been founded in [[876]] (the 18th year of the {{ill|Jōgan era|ja|貞観}}). During the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}, the area was coastal and formed a sandbar called "Suzaki." Thus, the shrine became known as "Hama-no-Miya" (Shrine of the Beach).
The exact date of the establishment of the {{ill|Inari Shrine|ja|稲荷神社}} is unknown.
== Shrine Buildings and Grounds == The grounds include the {{ill|Miyamado Inari Shrine|ja|海山道稲荷神社}}, which surpasses the Shinmei Shrine in scale.
There are over 20 subsidiary {{ill|Inari shrines|ja|稲荷神社}}, a {{ill|Tenjin shrine|ja|天神社}} housing Japan’s largest statue of {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane}}, and various monuments such as the Pen Mound, Eyeglass Mound, and Fox Mound.
== Festivals and Events == * festival: October 3–5 * Inari Grand Festival: April 8–9 * Fox Wedding Ceremony: Held on {{ill|Setsubun|ja|節分}} (February), this purification ritual involves a mock wedding procession. Participants representing the bride and groom wear fox masks, and a "bean-throwing" event is also held.
== location == 1-62 Miyamado Town, [[Yokkaichi]], [[Mie Prefecture]], Japan
== Access == * A short walk from [[Kintetsu Nagoya Line]]'s [[Miyamado Station]]. == See Also == {{Commonscat|Miyamado-jinja|Miyamado Shrine}} * {{ill|Shinmei Shrine|ja|神明神社}} * {{ill|Inari Shrine|ja|稲荷神社}}

{{Infobox religious building | name = Suzaki Hamamiya Shinmei Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Suzakihamamiya(Mie).JPG|250px]] | location = 1-62 Miyamado Town, Yokkaichi, Mie Prefecture, Japan | latitude = 34.9428 | longitude = 136.6217 | deity = {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神}} and others | founded = [[876]] (18th year of the {{ill|Jōgan era|ja|貞観}}) | main_building_style = | festivals = October 3–5 (festival); February (Fox Wedding Ceremony) | map_type = Japan Mie }}
'''Suzaki Hamamiya Shinmei Shrine''' ({{ill|Suzaki Hamamiya Shinmei Shrine|ja|洲崎濱宮神明神社}}, ''Suzaki Hamamiya Shinmei Jinja'') is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in the [[Shiobama District]], [[Yokkaichi]], [[Mie Prefecture]], Japan. It is often collectively referred to as "Miyamado Shrine" due to the large presence of its {{ill|Inari Shrine|ja|稲荷神社}}.
== Overview == [[File:Miyamado-inari(Mie).JPG|thumb|Miyamado Inari Shrine]] The shrine's name "濱" (''Hama'') is also written as "浜" or "濵."
The subordinate shrine, known as '''Miyamado Inari Shrine''' ({{ill|Miyamado Inari Shrine|ja|海山道稲荷神社}}), is larger than the main Shinmei Shrine and is famous for the "Fox Wedding Ceremony" ({{ill|Kitsune no yomeiri|lt=Fox Wedding|ja|狐の嫁入り}}).
The grounds also include multiple Inari shrines, {{ill|Tenjinja|lt=Tenjin shrines|ja|天神社}}, and unique features such as Japan’s largest statue of {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane}}.
== Enshrined Deities == === Shinmei Shrine === {{Div col}} * {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|lt=Amenokoyane-no-Mikoto|ja|天児屋根命|1=Ame-no-Koyane}} * {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神}} * {{ill|Takemikazuchi|lt=Takemikazuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|建御雷命}} * {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}} * {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚尊}} * {{ill|Ukanomitama|lt-Ukanomitama-no-Kami|ja|倉稲魂命}} * {{ill|Oyamakui no Kami|lt=Ōyamakui-no-Kami|ja|大山祇神}} * {{ill|Kagu-tsuchi|lt=Kagutsuchi-no-Kami|ja|火之迦具土神}} * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane}} * {{ill|Kagu-tsuchi|lt=Ho-musubi-no-Kami|ja|火産霊神}} * {{ill|Ukemochi|lt=Ukemochi-no-Kami|ja|保食神}} * {{ill|Yamatohime-no-mikoto|lt=Yamatohime-no-Mikoto|ja|倭姫命|1=Yamatohime-no-mikoto}} {{Div col end}}
=== Inari Shrine === * {{ill|Ukanomitama|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Kami|ja|倉稲魂命}} * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|lt=Ōkuninushi-no-Kami|ja|大己貴命}} * {{ill|Ōta-no-Kami|ja|大田命}} * {{ill|Ukemochi|lt=Ukemochi-no-Kami|ja|保食神}} * {{ill|Ōmiyahime-no-Mikoto|lt=Ōmiyanome-no-Kami|ja|大宮能売神}}
== History == The shrine is traditionally said to have been founded in [[876]] (the 18th year of the {{ill|Jōgan era|ja|貞観}}). During the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}, the area was coastal and formed a sandbar called "Suzaki." Thus, the shrine became known as "Hama-no-Miya" (Shrine of the Beach).
The exact date of the establishment of the {{ill|Inari Shrine|ja|稲荷神社}} is unknown.
== Shrine Buildings and Grounds == The grounds include the {{ill|Miyamado Inari Shrine|ja|海山道稲荷神社}}, which surpasses the Shinmei Shrine in scale.
There are over 20 subsidiary {{ill|Inari shrines|ja|稲荷神社}}, a {{ill|Tenjin shrine|ja|天神社}} housing Japan’s largest statue of {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane}}, and various monuments such as the Pen Mound, Eyeglass Mound, and Fox Mound.
== Festivals and Events == * festival: October 3–5 * Inari Grand Festival: April 8–9 * Fox Wedding Ceremony: Held on {{ill|Setsubun|ja|節分}} (February), this purification ritual involves a mock wedding procession. Participants representing the bride and groom wear fox masks, and a "bean-throwing" event is also held.
== location == 1-62 Miyamado Town, [[Yokkaichi]], [[Mie Prefecture]], Japan
== Access == * A short walk from [[Kintetsu Nagoya Line]]'s [[Miyamado Station]]. == See Also == {{Commonscat|Miyamado-jinja|Miyamado Shrine}} * {{ill|Shinmei Shrine|ja|神明神社}} * {{ill|Inari Shrine|ja|稲荷神社}}

revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = 洲崎濱宮神明神社 |画像 = [[File:Suzakihamamiya(Mie).JPG|250px]] |所在地 = 三重県四日市市海山道町1-62 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|56|34|N|136|37|18|E|type:landmark_region:JP-24|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = 天照大神 |社格 = |創建 = [[876年]]（[[貞観 (日本)|貞観]]18年） |本殿 = |別名 = 海山道神社 |例祭 = 10月3日 - 10月5日 |神事 = 狐の嫁入り神事 }}
[[File:Miyamado-inari(Mie).JPG|thumb|250px|right|海山道稲荷神社]]
'''洲崎濱宮神明神社'''（すざきはまみやしんめいじんじゃ）は[[三重県]][[四日市市]][[塩浜地区]]の海山道（みやまど）にある[[神社]]である。
== 概要 == 濱（はま）の字は、'''浜'''や'''濵'''にも書かれる。
境内社である'''海山道稲荷神社'''（みやまどいなりじんじゃ）の方が大きく、「[[狐の嫁入り]]神事」で知られる。
境内には多くの稲荷社や天神社などもあり、総称して「'''海山道神社'''」（みやまどじんじゃ）とも呼ばれている。
== 祭神 == === 神明神社 === {{Div col}} * [[面足尊|面足命]] * [[天照大御神]] * [[惶根尊|惶根神]] * [[仁徳天皇]] * [[建速須佐之男命]] * [[宇迦之御魂神]] * [[大山祇命]] * [[火之迦具土神]] * [[菅原道真公]] * [[火産霊神]] * [[保食神]] * [[倭姫命]] {{Div col end}}
=== 稲荷神社 === * 倉稲魂神 * [[大己貴命]] * [[太田神]] * 保食神 * [[大宮能売神]]
== 概要 == [[876年]]（[[貞観 (日本)|貞観]]18年）創建と伝えられる。[[平安時代]]、当地は[[海浜]]で洲崎を成していたことから、古来「浜の宮」と呼ばれていた。
稲荷神社の勧請時期は不詳。
== 社殿・境内 == 境内社に海山道稲荷神社があり、その規模は神明社を凌ぐ。
境内末社は稲荷社だけでも20社以上のほか、日本一の大きさの[[菅原道真]]像を祀る天神菅原社や、筆塚、眼鏡塚、狐塚など多くある。
== 祭事・年中行事 == * 例大祭：10月3 - 10月5日 * 稲荷大祭：4月8日 - 4月9日 * 狐の嫁入り神事：[[節分]]の日に行われる稲荷神社の[[厄祓い]]行事。 ** 境内の福徳稲荷社の娘狐が、同じ境内の助四郎稲荷社へ嫁入りするという設定で行われる。その年の[[厄年]]の男女が狐の新郎新婦に扮し、[[福引]]券入りの[[節分#豆まき|福豆まき]]も行われる。
== 所在地 == * [[三重県]][[四日市市]]海山道町1-62
== 交通 == * [[近畿日本鉄道|近鉄]][[近鉄名古屋線|名古屋線]] [[海山道駅]]下車、徒歩ですぐ。
== 関連項目 == {{Commonscat|Miyamado-jinja|海山道神社}} * [[神明神社]] * [[稲荷神]]
{{神道 横}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:すさきはまみやしんめいしんしや}} [[Category:三重県の神社]] [[Category:四日市市の歴史]] [[Category:四日市市の建築物]] [[Category:神明神社]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:洲崎濱宮神明神社]]

Wikidata name: Suzaki Hamamiya Shinmei-jinja
[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:16, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}}
{{Infobox religious building
| name = Suzaki Hamamiya Shinmei Shrine
| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]
|image = File:Suzakihamamiya(Mie).JPG
| location = 1-62 Miyamado Town, Yokkaichi, Mie Prefecture, Japan
| latitude = 34.9428
| longitude = 136.6217
| deity = [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]] and others
| founded = [[:en:876|876]] (18th year of the [[:en:Zhenguan|Jōgan era]])
| main_building_style = 
| festivals = October 3–5 (festival); February (Fox Wedding Ceremony)
| map_type = Japan Mie
| location = {{ill|Yokkaichi|qid=Q26410}}
| coordinates = {{Coord|34.942778|N|136.621667|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}
}}

'''Suzaki Hamamiya Shinmei Shrine''' ({{ill|Suzaki Hamamiya Shinmei Shrine|ja|洲崎濱宮神明神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suzaki Hamamiya Shinmei Shrine|qq=|1=Suzaki Hamamiya Shinmei Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11556511}}, ''Suzaki Hamamiya Shinmei Jinja'') is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in the [[Shiobama District]], [[:en:Yokkaichi|Yokkaichi]], [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]], Japan. It is often collectively referred to as "Miyamado Shrine" due to the large presence of its [[:en:Inari shrine|Inari Shrine]].

== Overview ==
[[File:Miyamado-inari(Mie).JPG|thumb|Miyamado Inari Shrine]]
The shrine's name "濱" (''Hama'') is also written as "浜" or "濵."

The subordinate shrine, known as '''Miyamado Inari Shrine''' ({{ill|Miyamado Inari Shrine|ja|海山道稲荷神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyamado Inari Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 洲崎濱宮神明神社}}), is larger than the main Shinmei Shrine and is famous for the "Fox Wedding Ceremony" ([[:en:Kitsune no yomeiri|Fox Wedding]]). 

The grounds also include multiple Inari shrines, {{ill|Tenjinsha|lt=Tenjin shrines|ja|天神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenjinsha|qq=}}, and unique features such as Japan’s largest statue of {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}}.

== Enshrined Deities ==
=== Shinmei Shrine ===
{{Div col}}
* {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|lt=Amenokoyane-no-Mikoto|ja|天児屋根命|1=Ame-no-Koyane|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天児屋命}}
* [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]]
* {{ill|Takemikazuchi|lt=Takemikazuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|建御雷命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takemikazuchi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タケミカヅチ}}
* [[:en:Emperor Nintoku|Emperor Nintoku]]
* {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚尊|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}}
* {{ill|Ukanomitama|lt-Ukanomitama-no-Kami|ja|倉稲魂命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ}}
* {{ill|Oyamakui no Kami|lt=Ōyamakui-no-Kami|ja|大山祇神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oyamakui no Kami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ|1=Ōyamatsumi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q386563}}
* {{ill|Kagu-tsuchi|lt=Kagutsuchi-no-Kami|ja|火之迦具土神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagu-tsuchi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to カグツチ}}
* {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}}
* {{ill|Kagu-tsuchi|lt=Ho-musubi-no-Kami|ja|火産霊神|1=Kagu-tsuchi|lt=Kagutsuchi|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagu-tsuchi|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagutsuchi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to カグツチ}}
* [[:en:Ukemochi|Ukemochi-no-Kami]]
* [[:en:Yamatohime-no-mikoto|Yamatohime-no-Mikoto]]
{{Div col end}}

=== Inari Shrine ===
* {{ill|Ukanomitama|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Kami|ja|倉稲魂命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ}}
* {{ill|Ōkuninushi|lt=Ōkuninushi-no-Kami|ja|大己貴命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}
* {{ill|Ōta-no-Kami|ja|大田命|1=Ōta-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=太田命|4=fr|5=大田命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōta-no-Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōta-no-Mikoto|qq=}}
* [[:en:Ukemochi|Ukemochi-no-Kami]]
* {{ill|Ōmiya-no-Me|lt=Ōmiya-no-Me|lt=Ōmiyahime-no-Mikoto|lt=Ōmiyanome-no-Kami|ja|大宮能売神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmiyahime-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオミヤノメ}}

== History ==
The shrine is traditionally said to have been founded in [[:en:876|876]] (the 18th year of the [[:en:Zhenguan|Jōgan era]]). During the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]], the area was coastal and formed a sandbar called "Suzaki." Thus, the shrine became known as "Hama-no-Miya" (Shrine of the Beach).

The exact date of the establishment of the [[:en:Inari shrine|Inari Shrine]] is unknown.

== Shrine Buildings and Grounds ==
The grounds include the {{ill|Miyamado Inari Shrine|ja|海山道稲荷神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyamado Inari Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 洲崎濱宮神明神社}}, which surpasses the Shinmei Shrine in scale.

There are over 20 subsidiary [[:en:Inari shrine|Inari shrines]], a {{ill|Tenjin shrine|ja|天神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenjin shrine|qq=|1=Tenjinsha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11443279}} housing Japan’s largest statue of {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}}, and various monuments such as the Pen Mound, Eyeglass Mound, and Fox Mound.

== Festivals and Events ==
* festival: October 3–5
* Inari Grand Festival: April 8–9
* Fox Wedding Ceremony: Held on [[:en:Setsubun|Setsubun]] (February), this purification ritual involves a mock wedding procession. Participants representing the bride and groom wear fox masks, and a "bean-throwing" event is also held.

== location ==
1-62 Miyamado Town, [[:en:Yokkaichi|Yokkaichi]], [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]], Japan

== Access ==
* A short walk from [[:en:Kintetsu Nagoya Line|Kintetsu Nagoya Line]]'s [[:en:Miyamado Station|Miyamado Station]].

== See Also ==
{{Commonscat|Miyamado-jinja|Miyamado Shrine}}
* [[:en:Shinmei shrines|Shinmei Shrine]]
* [[:en:Inari shrine|Inari Shrine]]

'''Suzaki Hamamiya Shinmei Shrine''' ({{ill|Suzaki Hamamiya Shinmei Shrine|ja|洲崎濱宮神明神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suzaki Hamamiya Shinmei Shrine|qq=|1=Suzaki Hamamiya Shinmei Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11556511}}, ''Suzaki Hamamiya Shinmei Jinja'') is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in the [[Shiobama District]], [[:en:Yokkaichi|Yokkaichi]], [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]], Japan. It is often collectively referred to as "Miyamado Shrine" due to the large presence of its [[:en:Inari shrine|Inari Shrine]].

== Overview ==
[[File:Miyamado-inari(Mie).JPG|thumb|Miyamado Inari Shrine]]
The shrine's name "濱" (''Hama'') is also written as "浜" or "濵."

The subordinate shrine, known as '''Miyamado Inari Shrine''' ({{ill|Miyamado Inari Shrine|ja|海山道稲荷神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyamado Inari Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 洲崎濱宮神明神社}}), is larger than the main Shinmei Shrine and is famous for the "Fox Wedding Ceremony" ([[:en:Kitsune no yomeiri|Fox Wedding]]). 

The grounds also include multiple Inari shrines, {{ill|Tenjinsha|lt=Tenjin shrines|ja|天神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenjinsha|qq=}}, and unique features such as Japan’s largest statue of {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}}.

{{Div col}}

* {{ill|Ukanomitama|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Kami|ja|倉稲魂命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ}}

* [[:en:Ukemochi|Ukemochi-no-Kami]]
* {{ill|Ōmiya-no-Me|lt=Ōmiya-no-Me|lt=Ōmiyahime-no-Mikoto|lt=Ōmiyanome-no-Kami|ja|大宮能売神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmiyahime-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオミヤノメ}}

== History ==
The shrine is traditionally said to have been founded in [[:en:876|876]] (the 18th year of the [[:en:Zhenguan|Jōgan era]]). During the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]], the area was coastal and formed a sandbar called "Suzaki." Thus, the shrine became known as "Hama-no-Miya" (Shrine of the Beach).

The exact date of the establishment of the [[:en:Inari shrine|Inari Shrine]] is unknown.

== Shrine Buildings and Grounds ==
The grounds include the {{ill|Miyamado Inari Shrine|ja|海山道稲荷神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyamado Inari Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 洲崎濱宮神明神社}}, which surpasses the Shinmei Shrine in scale.

There are over 20 subsidiary [[:en:Inari shrine|Inari shrines]], a {{ill|Tenjin shrine|ja|天神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenjin shrine|qq=|1=Tenjinsha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11443279}} housing Japan’s largest statue of {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}}, and various monuments such as the Pen Mound, Eyeglass Mound, and Fox Mound.

== Festivals and Events ==
* festival: October 3–5
* Inari Grand Festival: April 8–9
* Fox Wedding Ceremony: Held on [[:en:Setsubun|Setsubun]] (February), this purification ritual involves a mock wedding procession. Participants representing the bride and groom wear fox masks, and a "bean-throwing" event is also held.

== location ==
1-62 Miyamado Town, [[:en:Yokkaichi|Yokkaichi]], [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]], Japan

== Access ==
* A short walk from [[:en:Kintetsu Nagoya Line|Kintetsu Nagoya Line]]'s [[:en:Miyamado Station|Miyamado Station]].

* [[:en:Ukemochi|Ukemochi-no-Kami]]
* {{ill|Ōmiya-no-Me|lt=Ōmiya-no-Me|lt=Ōmiyahime-no-Mikoto|lt=Ōmiyanome-no-Kami|ja|大宮能売神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmiyahime-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオミヤノメ}}

== History ==
The shrine is traditionally said to have been founded in [[:en:876|876]] (the 18th year of the [[:en:Zhenguan|Jōgan era]]). During the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]], the area was coastal and formed a sandbar called "Suzaki." Thus, the shrine became known as "Hama-no-Miya" (Shrine of the Beach).

The exact date of the establishment of the [[:en:Inari shrine|Inari Shrine]] is unknown.

== Shrine Buildings and Grounds ==
The grounds include the {{ill|Miyamado Inari Shrine|ja|海山道稲荷神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyamado Inari Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 洲崎濱宮神明神社}}, which surpasses the Shinmei Shrine in scale.

There are over 20 subsidiary [[:en:Inari shrine|Inari shrines]], a {{ill|Tenjin shrine|ja|天神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenjin shrine|1=Tenjinsha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11443279}} housing Japan’s largest statue of {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}}, and various monuments such as the Pen Mound, Eyeglass Mound, and Fox Mound.

== Festivals and Events ==
* festival: October 3–5
* Inari Grand Festival: April 8–9
* Fox Wedding Ceremony: Held on [[:en:Setsubun|Setsubun]] (February), this purification ritual involves a mock wedding procession. Participants representing the bride and groom wear fox masks, and a "bean-throwing" event is also held.

== location ==
1-62 Miyamado Town, [[:en:Yokkaichi|Yokkaichi]], [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]], Japan

== Access ==
* A short walk from [[:en:Kintetsu Nagoya Line|Kintetsu Nagoya Line]]'s [[:en:Miyamado Station|Miyamado Station]].

'''Suzaki Hamamiya Shinmei Shrine''' ({{ill|Suzaki Hamamiya Shinmei Shrine|ja|洲崎濱宮神明神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suzaki Hamamiya Shinmei Shrine|1=Suzaki Hamamiya Shinmei Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11556511}}, ''Suzaki Hamamiya Shinmei Jinja'') is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in the [[Shiobama District]], [[:en:Yokkaichi|Yokkaichi]], [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]], Japan. It is often collectively referred to as "Miyamado Shrine" due to the large presence of its [[:en:Inari shrine|Inari Shrine]].

== Overview ==
[[File:Miyamado-inari(Mie).JPG|thumb|Miyamado Inari Shrine]]
The shrine's name "濱" (''Hama'') is also written as "浜" or "濵."

The subordinate shrine, known as '''Miyamado Inari Shrine''' ({{ill|Miyamado Inari Shrine|ja|海山道稲荷神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyamado Inari Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 洲崎濱宮神明神社}}), is larger than the main Shinmei Shrine and is famous for the "Fox Wedding Ceremony" ([[:en:Kitsune no yomeiri|Fox Wedding]]). 

The grounds also include multiple Inari shrines, {{ill|Tenjinsha|lt=Tenjin shrines|ja|天神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenjinsha|qq=}}, and unique features such as Japan’s largest statue of {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}}.

'''Suzaki Hamamiya Shinmei Shrine''' ({{ill|Suzaki Hamamiya Shinmei Shrine|ja|洲崎濱宮神明神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suzaki Hamamiya Shinmei Shrine|1=Suzaki Hamamiya Shinmei Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11556511}}, ''Suzaki Hamamiya Shinmei Jinja'') is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in the [[Shiobama District]], [[:en:Yokkaichi|Yokkaichi]], [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]], Japan. It is often collectively referred to as "Miyamado Shrine" due to the large presence of its [[:en:Inari shrine|Inari Shrine]].

== Overview ==
[[File:Miyamado-inari(Mie).JPG|thumb|Miyamado Inari Shrine]]
The shrine's name "濱" (''Hama'') is also written as "浜" or "濵."

The subordinate shrine, known as '''Miyamado Inari Shrine''' ({{ill|Miyamado Inari Shrine|ja|海山道稲荷神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyamado Inari Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 洲崎濱宮神明神社}}), is larger than the main Shinmei Shrine and is famous for the "Fox Wedding Ceremony" ([[:en:Kitsune no yomeiri|Fox Wedding]]). 

The grounds also include multiple Inari shrines, {{ill|Tenjinsha|lt=Tenjin shrines|ja|天神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenjinsha|qq=}}, and unique features such as Japan’s largest statue of {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}}.

== Enshrined Deities ==
=== Shinmei Shrine ===
{{Div col}}
* {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|lt=Amenokoyane-no-Mikoto|ja|天児屋根命|1=Ame-no-Koyane|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天児屋命}}
* [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]]
* {{ill|Takemikazuchi|lt=Takemikazuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|建御雷命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takemikazuchi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タケミカヅチ}}
* [[:en:Emperor Nintoku|Emperor Nintoku]]
* {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}}
* {{ill|Ukanomitama|lt-Ukanomitama-no-Kami|ja|倉稲魂命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ}}
* {{ill|Oyamakui no Kami|lt=Ōyamakui-no-Kami|ja|大山祇神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oyamakui no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ|1=Ōyamatsumi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q386563}}
* {{ill|Kagu-tsuchi|lt=Kagutsuchi-no-Kami|ja|火之迦具土神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagu-tsuchi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to カグツチ}}
* {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}}
* {{ill|Kagu-tsuchi|lt=Ho-musubi-no-Kami|ja|火産霊神|1=Kagu-tsuchi|lt=Kagutsuchi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagu-tsuchi|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagutsuchi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to カグツチ}}
* [[:en:Ukemochi|Ukemochi-no-Kami]]
* [[:en:Yamatohime-no-mikoto|Yamatohime-no-Mikoto]]
{{Div col end}}

=== Inari Shrine ===
* {{ill|Ukanomitama|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Kami|ja|倉稲魂命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ}}
* {{ill|Ōkuninushi|lt=Ōkuninushi-no-Kami|ja|大己貴命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}
* {{ill|Ōta-no-Kami|ja|大田命|1=Ōta-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=太田命|4=fr|5=大田命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōta-no-Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōta-no-Mikoto|qq=}}
* [[:en:Ukemochi|Ukemochi-no-Kami]]
* {{ill|Ōmiya-no-Me|lt=Ōmiya-no-Me|lt=Ōmiyahime-no-Mikoto|lt=Ōmiyanome-no-Kami|ja|大宮能売神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmiyahime-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオミヤノメ}}

== History ==
The shrine is traditionally said to have been founded in [[:en:876|876]] (the 18th year of the [[:en:Zhenguan|Jōgan era]]). During the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]], the area was coastal and formed a sandbar called "Suzaki." Thus, the shrine became known as "Hama-no-Miya" (Shrine of the Beach).

The exact date of the establishment of the [[:en:Inari shrine|Inari Shrine]] is unknown.

== Shrine Buildings and Grounds ==
The grounds include the {{ill|Miyamado Inari Shrine|ja|海山道稲荷神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyamado Inari Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 洲崎濱宮神明神社}}, which surpasses the Shinmei Shrine in scale.

There are over 20 subsidiary [[:en:Inari shrine|Inari shrines]], a {{ill|Tenjin shrine|ja|天神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenjin shrine|1=Tenjinsha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11443279}} housing Japan’s largest statue of {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}}, and various monuments such as the Pen Mound, Eyeglass Mound, and Fox Mound.

== Festivals and Events ==
* festival: October 3–5
* Inari Grand Festival: April 8–9
* Fox Wedding Ceremony: Held on [[:en:Setsubun|Setsubun]] (February), this purification ritual involves a mock wedding procession. Participants representing the bride and groom wear fox masks, and a "bean-throwing" event is also held.

== location ==
1-62 Miyamado Town, [[:en:Yokkaichi|Yokkaichi]], [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]], Japan

== Access ==
* A short walk from [[:en:Kintetsu Nagoya Line|Kintetsu Nagoya Line]]'s [[:en:Miyamado Station|Miyamado Station]].

* [[:en:Inari shrine|Inari Shrine]]

* {{ill|Ukanomitama|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Kami|ja|倉稲魂命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ}}
* {{ill|Ōkuninushi|lt=Ōkuninushi-no-Kami|ja|大己貴命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}
* {{ill|Ōta-no-Kami|ja|大田命|1=Ōta-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=太田命|4=fr|5=大田命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōta-no-Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōta-no-Mikoto|qq=}}
* [[:en:Ukemochi|Ukemochi-no-Kami]]
* {{ill|Ōmiya-no-Me|lt=Ōmiya-no-Me|lt=Ōmiyahime-no-Mikoto|lt=Ōmiyanome-no-Kami|ja|大宮能売神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmiyahime-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオミヤノメ}}

== History ==
The shrine is traditionally said to have been founded in [[:en:876|876]] (the 18th year of the [[:en:Zhenguan|Jōgan era]]). During the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]], the area was coastal and formed a sandbar called "Suzaki." Thus, the shrine became known as "Hama-no-Miya" (Shrine of the Beach).

The exact date of the establishment of the [[:en:Inari shrine|Inari Shrine]] is unknown.

== Shrine Buildings and Grounds ==
The grounds include the {{ill|Miyamado Inari Shrine|ja|海山道稲荷神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyamado Inari Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 洲崎濱宮神明神社}}, which surpasses the Shinmei Shrine in scale.

There are over 20 subsidiary [[:en:Inari shrine|Inari shrines]], a {{ill|Tenjin shrine|ja|天神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenjin shrine|1=Tenjinsha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11443279}} housing Japan’s largest statue of {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}}, and various monuments such as the Pen Mound, Eyeglass Mound, and Fox Mound.

== Festivals and Events ==
* festival: October 3–5
* Inari Grand Festival: April 8–9
* Fox Wedding Ceremony: Held on [[:en:Setsubun|Setsubun]] (February), this purification ritual involves a mock wedding procession. Participants representing the bride and groom wear fox masks, and a "bean-throwing" event is also held.

== location ==
1-62 Miyamado Town, [[:en:Yokkaichi|Yokkaichi]], [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]], Japan

== Access ==
* A short walk from [[:en:Kintetsu Nagoya Line|Kintetsu Nagoya Line]]'s [[:en:Miyamado Station|Miyamado Station]].

'''Suzaki Hamamiya Shinmei Shrine''' ({{ill|Suzaki Hamamiya Shinmei Shrine|ja|洲崎濱宮神明神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suzaki Hamamiya Shinmei Shrine|1=Suzaki Hamamiya Shinmei Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11556511}}, ''Suzaki Hamamiya Shinmei Jinja'') is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in the [[Shiobama District]], [[:en:Yokkaichi|Yokkaichi]], [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]], Japan. It is often collectively referred to as "Miyamado Shrine" due to the large presence of its [[:en:Inari shrine|Inari Shrine]].

== Overview ==
[[File:Miyamado-inari(Mie).JPG|thumb|Miyamado Inari Shrine]]
The shrine's name "濱" (''Hama'') is also written as "浜" or "濵."

The subordinate shrine, known as '''Miyamado Inari Shrine''' ({{ill|Miyamado Inari Shrine|ja|海山道稲荷神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyamado Inari Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 洲崎濱宮神明神社}}), is larger than the main Shinmei Shrine and is famous for the "Fox Wedding Ceremony" ([[:en:Kitsune no yomeiri|Fox Wedding]]). 

The grounds also include multiple Inari shrines, {{ill|Tenjinsha|lt=Tenjin shrines|ja|天神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenjinsha|qq=}}, and unique features such as Japan’s largest statue of {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}}.

'''Suzaki Hamamiya Shinmei Shrine''' ({{ill|Suzaki Hamamiya Shinmei Shrine|ja|洲崎濱宮神明神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suzaki Hamamiya Shinmei Shrine|1=Suzaki Hamamiya Shinmei Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11556511}}, ''Suzaki Hamamiya Shinmei Jinja'') is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in the [[Shiobama District]], [[:en:Yokkaichi|Yokkaichi]], [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]], Japan. It is often collectively referred to as "Miyamado Shrine" due to the large presence of its [[:en:Inari shrine|Inari Shrine]].

== Overview ==
[[File:Miyamado-inari(Mie).JPG|thumb|Miyamado Inari Shrine]]
The shrine's name "濱" (''Hama'') is also written as "浜" or "濵."

The subordinate shrine, known as '''Miyamado Inari Shrine''' ({{ill|Miyamado Inari Shrine|ja|海山道稲荷神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyamado Inari Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 洲崎濱宮神明神社}}), is larger than the main Shinmei Shrine and is famous for the "Fox Wedding Ceremony" ([[:en:Kitsune no yomeiri|Fox Wedding]]). 

The grounds also include multiple Inari shrines, {{ill|Tenjinsha|lt=Tenjin shrines|ja|天神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenjinsha|qq=}}, and unique features such as Japan’s largest statue of {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}}.

== Enshrined Deities ==
=== Shinmei Shrine ===
{{Div col}}
* {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|lt=Amenokoyane-no-Mikoto|ja|天児屋根命|1=Ame-no-Koyane|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天児屋命}}
* [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]]
* {{ill|Takemikazuchi|lt=Takemikazuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|建御雷命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takemikazuchi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タケミカヅチ}}
* [[:en:Emperor Nintoku|Emperor Nintoku]]
* {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}}
* {{ill|Ukanomitama|lt-Ukanomitama-no-Kami|ja|倉稲魂命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ}}
* {{ill|Oyamakui no Kami|lt=Ōyamakui-no-Kami|ja|大山祇神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oyamakui no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ|1=Ōyamatsumi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q386563}}
* {{ill|Kagu-tsuchi|lt=Kagutsuchi-no-Kami|ja|火之迦具土神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagu-tsuchi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to カグツチ}}
* {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}}
* {{ill|Kagu-tsuchi|lt=Ho-musubi-no-Kami|ja|火産霊神|1=Kagu-tsuchi|lt=Kagutsuchi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagu-tsuchi|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagutsuchi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to カグツチ}}
* [[:en:Ukemochi|Ukemochi-no-Kami]]
* [[:en:Yamatohime-no-mikoto|Yamatohime-no-Mikoto]]
{{Div col end}}

=== Inari Shrine ===
* {{ill|Ukanomitama|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Kami|ja|倉稲魂命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ}}
* {{ill|Ōkuninushi|lt=Ōkuninushi-no-Kami|ja|大己貴命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}
* {{ill|Ōta-no-Kami|ja|大田命|1=Ōta-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=太田命|4=fr|5=大田命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōta-no-Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōta-no-Mikoto|qq=}}
* [[:en:Ukemochi|Ukemochi-no-Kami]]
* {{ill|Ōmiya-no-Me|lt=Ōmiya-no-Me|lt=Ōmiyahime-no-Mikoto|lt=Ōmiyanome-no-Kami|ja|大宮能売神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmiyahime-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオミヤノメ}}

== History ==
The shrine is traditionally said to have been founded in [[:en:876|876]] (the 18th year of the [[:en:Zhenguan|Jōgan era]]). During the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]], the area was coastal and formed a sandbar called "Suzaki." Thus, the shrine became known as "Hama-no-Miya" (Shrine of the Beach).

The exact date of the establishment of the [[:en:Inari shrine|Inari Shrine]] is unknown.

== Shrine Buildings and Grounds ==
The grounds include the {{ill|Miyamado Inari Shrine|ja|海山道稲荷神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyamado Inari Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 洲崎濱宮神明神社}}, which surpasses the Shinmei Shrine in scale.

There are over 20 subsidiary [[:en:Inari shrine|Inari shrines]], a {{ill|Tenjin shrine|ja|天神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenjin shrine|1=Tenjinsha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11443279}} housing Japan’s largest statue of {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}}, and various monuments such as the Pen Mound, Eyeglass Mound, and Fox Mound.

== Festivals and Events ==
* festival: October 3–5
* Inari Grand Festival: April 8–9
* Fox Wedding Ceremony: Held on [[:en:Setsubun|Setsubun]] (February), this purification ritual involves a mock wedding procession. Participants representing the bride and groom wear fox masks, and a "bean-throwing" event is also held.

== location ==
1-62 Miyamado Town, [[:en:Yokkaichi|Yokkaichi]], [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]], Japan

== Access ==
* A short walk from [[:en:Kintetsu Nagoya Line|Kintetsu Nagoya Line]]'s [[:en:Miyamado Station|Miyamado Station]].

== See Also ==
{{Commonscat|Miyamado-jinja|Miyamado Shrine}}
* [[:en:Shinmei shrines|Shinmei Shrine]]
* [[:en:Inari shrine|Inari Shrine]]

It is often collectively referred to as "Miyamado Shrine" due to the large presence of its [[:en:Inari shrine|Inari Shrine]].
== Overview == [[File:Miyamado-inari(Mie).JPG|thumb|Miyamado Inari Shrine]] The shrine's name "濱" (''Hama'') is also written as "浜" or "濵."
The subordinate shrine, known as '''Miyamado Inari Shrine''' ({{ill|Miyamado Inari Shrine|ja|海山道稲荷神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyamado Inari Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 洲崎濱宮神明神社}}), is larger than the main Shinmei Shrine and is famous for the "Fox Wedding Ceremony" ([[:en:Kitsune no yomeiri|Fox Wedding]]).

== Enshrined Deities == === Shinmei Shrine === {{Div col}} * {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|lt=Amenokoyane-no-Mikoto|ja|天児屋根命|1=Ame-no-Koyane|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天児屋命}} * [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]] * {{ill|Takemikazuchi|lt=Takemikazuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|建御雷命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takemikazuchi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タケミカヅチ}} * [[:en:Emperor Nintoku|Emperor Nintoku]] * {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}} * {{ill|Ukanomitama|lt-Ukanomitama-no-Kami|ja|倉稲魂命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ}} * {{ill|Oyamakui no Kami|lt=Ōyamakui-no-Kami|ja|大山祇神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oyamakui no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ|1=Ōyamatsumi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q386563}} * {{ill|Kagu-tsuchi|lt=Kagutsuchi-no-Kami|ja|火之迦具土神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagu-tsuchi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to カグツチ}} * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}} * {{ill|Kagu-tsuchi|lt=Ho-musubi-no-Kami|ja|火産霊神|1=Kagu-tsuchi|lt=Kagutsuchi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagu-tsuchi|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagutsuchi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to カグツチ}} * [[:en:Ukemochi|Ukemochi-no-Kami]] * [[:en:Yamatohime-no-mikoto|Yamatohime-no-Mikoto]] {{Div col end}}
=== Inari Shrine === * {{ill|Ukanomitama|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Kami|ja|倉稲魂命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ}} * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|lt=Ōkuninushi-no-Kami|ja|大己貴命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}} * {{ill|Ōta-no-Kami|ja|大田命|1=Ōta-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=太田命|4=fr|5=大田命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōta-no-Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōta-no-Mikoto|qq=}} * [[:en:Ukemochi|Ukemochi-no-Kami]] * {{ill|Ōmiya-no-Me|lt=Ōmiya-no-Me|lt=Ōmiyahime-no-Mikoto|lt=Ōmiyanome-no-Kami|ja|大宮能売神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmiyahime-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオミヤノメ}}
== History == The shrine is traditionally said to have been founded in [[:en:876|876]] (the 18th year of the [[:en:Zhenguan|Jōgan era]]). During the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]], the area was coastal and formed a sandbar called "Suzaki." Thus, the shrine became known as "Hama-no-Miya" (Shrine of the Beach).
The exact date of the establishment of the [[:en:Inari shrine|Inari Shrine]] is unknown.
== Shrine Buildings and Grounds == The grounds include the {{ill|Miyamado Inari Shrine|ja|海山道稲荷神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyamado Inari Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 洲崎濱宮神明神社}}, which surpasses the Shinmei Shrine in scale.
There are over 20 subsidiary [[:en:Inari shrine|Inari shrines]], a {{ill|Tenjin shrine|ja|天神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenjin shrine|1=Tenjinsha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11443279}} housing Japan’s largest statue of {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}}, and various monuments such as the Pen Mound, Eyeglass Mound, and Fox Mound.
== Festivals and Events == * festival: October 3–5 * Inari Grand Festival: April 8–9 * Fox Wedding Ceremony: Held on [[:en:Setsubun|Setsubun]] (February), this purification ritual involves a mock wedding procession. Participants representing the bride and groom wear fox masks, and a "bean-throwing" event is also held.
== location == 1-62 Miyamado Town, [[:en:Yokkaichi|Yokkaichi]], [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]], Japan
== Access == * A short walk from [[:en:Kintetsu Nagoya Line|Kintetsu Nagoya Line]]'s [[:en:Miyamado Station|Miyamado Station]].

The subordinate shrine, known as '''Miyamado Inari Shrine''' ({{ill|Miyamado Inari Shrine|ja|海山道稲荷神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyamado Inari Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 洲崎濱宮神明神社}}), is larger than the main Shinmei Shrine and is famous for the "Fox Wedding Ceremony" ([[:en:Kitsune no yomeiri|Fox Wedding]]).
The grounds also include multiple Inari shrines, {{ill|Tenjinsha|lt=Tenjin shrines|ja|天神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenjinsha|qq=}}, and unique features such as Japan’s largest statue of {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}}.
== Enshrined Deities == === Shinmei Shrine === {{Div col}} * {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|lt=Amenokoyane-no-Mikoto|ja|天児屋根命|1=Ame-no-Koyane|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天児屋命}} * [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]] * {{ill|Takemikazuchi|lt=Takemikazuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|建御雷命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takemikazuchi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タケミカヅチ}} * [[:en:Emperor Nintoku|Emperor Nintoku]] * {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}} * {{ill|Ukanomitama|lt-Ukanomitama-no-Kami|ja|倉稲魂命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ}} * {{ill|Oyamakui no Kami|lt=Ōyamakui-no-Kami|ja|大山祇神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oyamakui no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ|1=Ōyamatsumi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q386563}} * {{ill|Kagu-tsuchi|lt=Kagutsuchi-no-Kami|ja|火之迦具土神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagu-tsuchi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to カグツチ}} * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}} * {{ill|Kagu-tsuchi|lt=Ho-musubi-no-Kami|ja|火産霊神|1=Kagu-tsuchi|lt=Kagutsuchi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagu-tsuchi|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagutsuchi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to カグツチ}} * [[:en:Ukemochi|Ukemochi-no-Kami]] * [[:en:Yamatohime-no-mikoto|Yamatohime-no-Mikoto]] {{Div col end}}
=== Inari Shrine === * {{ill|Ukanomitama|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Kami|ja|倉稲魂命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ}} * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|lt=Ōkuninushi-no-Kami|ja|大己貴命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}} * {{ill|Ōta-no-Kami|ja|大田命|1=Ōta-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=太田命|4=fr|5=大田命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōta-no-Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōta-no-Mikoto|qq=}} * [[:en:Ukemochi|Ukemochi-no-Kami]] * {{ill|Ōmiya-no-Me|lt=Ōmiya-no-Me|lt=Ōmiyahime-no-Mikoto|lt=Ōmiyanome-no-Kami|ja|大宮能売神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmiyahime-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオミヤノメ}}
== History == The shrine is traditionally said to have been founded in [[:en:876|876]] (the 18th year of the [[:en:Zhenguan|Jōgan era]]). During the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]], the area was coastal and formed a sandbar called "Suzaki." Thus, the shrine became known as "Hama-no-Miya" (Shrine of the Beach).
The exact date of the establishment of the [[:en:Inari shrine|Inari Shrine]] is unknown.
== Shrine Buildings and Grounds == The grounds include the {{ill|Miyamado Inari Shrine|ja|海山道稲荷神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyamado Inari Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 洲崎濱宮神明神社}}, which surpasses the Shinmei Shrine in scale.
There are over 20 subsidiary [[:en:Inari shrine|Inari shrines]], a {{ill|Tenjin shrine|ja|天神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenjin shrine|1=Tenjinsha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11443279}} housing Japan’s largest statue of {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}}, and various monuments such as the Pen Mound, Eyeglass Mound, and Fox Mound.
== Festivals and Events == * festival: October 3–5 * Inari Grand Festival: April 8–9 * Fox Wedding Ceremony: Held on [[:en:Setsubun|Setsubun]] (February), this purification ritual involves a mock wedding procession. Participants representing the bride and groom wear fox masks, and a "bean-throwing" event is also held.
== location == 1-62 Miyamado Town, [[:en:Yokkaichi|Yokkaichi]], [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]], Japan
== Access == * A short walk from [[:en:Kintetsu Nagoya Line|Kintetsu Nagoya Line]]'s [[:en:Miyamado Station|Miyamado Station]].
== See Also == {{Commonscat|Miyamado-jinja|Miyamado Shrine}} * [[:en:Shinmei shrines|Shinmei Shrine]] * [[:en:Inari shrine|Inari Shrine]]

'''Suzaki Hamamiya Shinmei Shrine''' ({{ill|Suzaki Hamamiya Shinmei Shrine|ja|洲崎濱宮神明神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suzaki Hamamiya Shinmei Shrine|1=Suzaki Hamamiya Shinmei Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11556511}}, ''Suzaki Hamamiya Shinmei Jinja'') is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in the [[Shiobama District]], [[:en:Yokkaichi|Yokkaichi]], [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]], Japan. It is often collectively referred to as "Miyamado Shrine" due to the large presence of its [[:en:Inari shrine|Inari Shrine]].
== Overview == [[File:Miyamado-inari(Mie).JPG|thumb|Miyamado Inari Shrine]] The shrine's name "濱" (''Hama'') is also written as "浜" or "濵."
The subordinate shrine, known as '''Miyamado Inari Shrine''' ({{ill|Miyamado Inari Shrine|ja|海山道稲荷神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyamado Inari Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 洲崎濱宮神明神社}}), is larger than the main Shinmei Shrine and is famous for the "Fox Wedding Ceremony" ([[:en:Kitsune no yomeiri|Fox Wedding]]).
The grounds also include multiple Inari shrines, {{ill|Tenjinsha|lt=Tenjin shrines|ja|天神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenjinsha|qq=}}, and unique features such as Japan’s largest statue of {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}}.
== Enshrined Deities == === Shinmei Shrine === {{Div col}} * {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|lt=Amenokoyane-no-Mikoto|ja|天児屋根命|1=Ame-no-Koyane|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天児屋命}} * [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]] * {{ill|Takemikazuchi|lt=Takemikazuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|建御雷命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takemikazuchi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タケミカヅチ}} * [[:en:Emperor Nintoku|Emperor Nintoku]] * {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}} * {{ill|Ukanomitama|lt-Ukanomitama-no-Kami|ja|倉稲魂命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ}} * {{ill|Oyamakui no Kami|lt=Ōyamakui-no-Kami|ja|大山祇神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oyamakui no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ|1=Ōyamatsumi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q386563}} * {{ill|Kagu-tsuchi|lt=Kagutsuchi-no-Kami|ja|火之迦具土神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagu-tsuchi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to カグツチ}} * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}} * {{ill|Kagu-tsuchi|lt=Ho-musubi-no-Kami|ja|火産霊神|1=Kagu-tsuchi|lt=Kagutsuchi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagu-tsuchi|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagutsuchi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to カグツチ}} * [[:en:Ukemochi|Ukemochi-no-Kami]] * [[:en:Yamatohime-no-mikoto|Yamatohime-no-Mikoto]] {{Div col end}}
=== Inari Shrine === * {{ill|Ukanomitama|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Kami|ja|倉稲魂命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ}} * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|lt=Ōkuninushi-no-Kami|ja|大己貴命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}} * {{ill|Ōta-no-Kami|ja|大田命|1=Ōta-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=太田命|4=fr|5=大田命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōta-no-Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōta-no-Mikoto|qq=}} * [[:en:Ukemochi|Ukemochi-no-Kami]] * {{ill|Ōmiya-no-Me|lt=Ōmiya-no-Me|lt=Ōmiyahime-no-Mikoto|lt=Ōmiyanome-no-Kami|ja|大宮能売神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmiyahime-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオミヤノメ}}
== History == The shrine is traditionally said to have been founded in [[:en:876|876]] (the 18th year of the [[:en:Zhenguan|Jōgan era]]). During the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]], the area was coastal and formed a sandbar called "Suzaki." Thus, the shrine became known as "Hama-no-Miya" (Shrine of the Beach).
The exact date of the establishment of the [[:en:Inari shrine|Inari Shrine]] is unknown.
== Shrine Buildings and Grounds == The grounds include the {{ill|Miyamado Inari Shrine|ja|海山道稲荷神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyamado Inari Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 洲崎濱宮神明神社}}, which surpasses the Shinmei Shrine in scale.
There are over 20 subsidiary [[:en:Inari shrine|Inari shrines]], a {{ill|Tenjin shrine|ja|天神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenjin shrine|1=Tenjinsha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11443279}} housing Japan’s largest statue of {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}}, and various monuments such as the Pen Mound, Eyeglass Mound, and Fox Mound.

Participants representing the bride and groom wear fox masks, and a "bean-throwing" event is also held.

There are over 20 subsidiary [[:en:Inari shrine|Inari shrines]], a {{ill|Tenjin shrine|ja|天神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenjin shrine|1=Tenjinsha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11443279}} housing Japan’s largest statue of {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}}, and various monuments such as the Pen Mound, Eyeglass Mound, and Fox Mound.
