'''Suzaki Hamamiya Shinmei Shrine''' ({{ill|Suzaki Hamamiya Shinmei Shrine|ja|洲崎濱宮神明神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suzaki Hamamiya Shinmei Shrine|1=Suzaki Hamamiya Shinmei Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11556511}}, ''Suzaki Hamamiya Shinmei Jinja'') is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in the [[Shiobama District]], [[:en:Yokkaichi|Yokkaichi]], [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]], Japan. It is often collectively referred to as "Miyamado Shrine" due to the large presence of its [[:en:Inari shrine|Inari Shrine]].

== Overview ==
[[File:Miyamado-inari(Mie).JPG|thumb|Miyamado Inari Shrine]]
The shrine's name "濱" (''Hama'') is also written as "浜" or "濵."

The subordinate shrine, known as '''Miyamado Inari Shrine''' ({{ill|Miyamado Inari Shrine|ja|海山道稲荷神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyamado Inari Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 洲崎濱宮神明神社}}), is larger than the main Shinmei Shrine and is famous for the "Fox Wedding Ceremony" ([[:en:Kitsune no yomeiri|Fox Wedding]]). 

The grounds also include multiple Inari shrines, {{ill|Tenjinsha|lt=Tenjin shrines|ja|天神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenjinsha|qq=}}, and unique features such as Japan’s largest statue of {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}}.

== Enshrined Deities ==
=== Shinmei Shrine ===
{{Div col}}
* {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|lt=Amenokoyane-no-Mikoto|ja|天児屋根命|1=Ame-no-Koyane|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天児屋命}}
* [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]]
* {{ill|Takemikazuchi|lt=Takemikazuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|建御雷命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takemikazuchi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タケミカヅチ}}
* [[:en:Emperor Nintoku|Emperor Nintoku]]
* {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}}
* {{ill|Ukanomitama|lt-Ukanomitama-no-Kami|ja|倉稲魂命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ}}
* {{ill|Oyamakui no Kami|lt=Ōyamakui-no-Kami|ja|大山祇神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oyamakui no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ|1=Ōyamatsumi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q386563}}
* {{ill|Kagu-tsuchi|lt=Kagutsuchi-no-Kami|ja|火之迦具土神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagu-tsuchi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to カグツチ}}
* {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}}
* {{ill|Kagu-tsuchi|lt=Ho-musubi-no-Kami|ja|火産霊神|1=Kagu-tsuchi|lt=Kagutsuchi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagu-tsuchi|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagutsuchi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to カグツチ}}
* [[:en:Ukemochi|Ukemochi-no-Kami]]
* [[:en:Yamatohime-no-mikoto|Yamatohime-no-Mikoto]]
{{Div col end}}

=== Inari Shrine ===
* {{ill|Ukanomitama|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Kami|ja|倉稲魂命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ}}
* {{ill|Ōkuninushi|lt=Ōkuninushi-no-Kami|ja|大己貴命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}
* {{ill|Ōta-no-Kami|ja|大田命|1=Ōta-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=太田命|4=fr|5=大田命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōta-no-Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōta-no-Mikoto|qq=}}
* [[:en:Ukemochi|Ukemochi-no-Kami]]
* {{ill|Ōmiya-no-Me|lt=Ōmiya-no-Me|lt=Ōmiyahime-no-Mikoto|lt=Ōmiyanome-no-Kami|ja|大宮能売神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmiyahime-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオミヤノメ}}

== History ==
The shrine is traditionally said to have been founded in [[:en:876|876]] (the 18th year of the [[:en:Zhenguan|Jōgan era]]). During the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]], the area was coastal and formed a sandbar called "Suzaki." Thus, the shrine became known as "Hama-no-Miya" (Shrine of the Beach).

The exact date of the establishment of the [[:en:Inari shrine|Inari Shrine]] is unknown.

== Shrine Buildings and Grounds ==
The grounds include the {{ill|Miyamado Inari Shrine|ja|海山道稲荷神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyamado Inari Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 洲崎濱宮神明神社}}, which surpasses the Shinmei Shrine in scale.

There are over 20 subsidiary [[:en:Inari shrine|Inari shrines]], a {{ill|Tenjin shrine|ja|天神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenjin shrine|1=Tenjinsha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11443279}} housing Japan’s largest statue of {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}}, and various monuments such as the Pen Mound, Eyeglass Mound, and Fox Mound.

== Festivals and Events ==
* festival: October 3–5
* Inari Grand Festival: April 8–9
* Fox Wedding Ceremony: Held on [[:en:Setsubun|Setsubun]] (February), this purification ritual involves a mock wedding procession. Participants representing the bride and groom wear fox masks, and a "bean-throwing" event is also held.

== location ==
1-62 Miyamado Town, [[:en:Yokkaichi|Yokkaichi]], [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]], Japan

== Access ==
* A short walk from [[:en:Kintetsu Nagoya Line|Kintetsu Nagoya Line]]'s [[:en:Miyamado Station|Miyamado Station]].

== Overview ==
[[File:Miyamado-inari(Mie).JPG|thumb|Miyamado Inari Shrine]]
The shrine's name "濱" (''Hama'') is also written as "浜" or "濵."

The subordinate shrine, known as '''Miyamado Inari Shrine''' ({{ill|Miyamado Inari Shrine|ja|海山道稲荷神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyamado Inari Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 洲崎濱宮神明神社}}), is larger than the main Shinmei Shrine and is famous for the "Fox Wedding Ceremony" ([[:en:Kitsune no yomeiri|Fox Wedding]]). 

The grounds also include multiple Inari shrines, {{ill|Tenjinsha|lt=Tenjin shrines|ja|天神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenjinsha|qq=}}, and unique features such as Japan’s largest statue of {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}}.

== Enshrined Deities ==
=== Shinmei Shrine ===
{{Div col}}
* {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|lt=Amenokoyane-no-Mikoto|ja|天児屋根命|1=Ame-no-Koyane|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天児屋命}}
* [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]]
* {{ill|Takemikazuchi|lt=Takemikazuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|建御雷命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takemikazuchi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タケミカヅチ}}
* [[:en:Emperor Nintoku|Emperor Nintoku]]

* {{ill|Ukanomitama|lt-Ukanomitama-no-Kami|ja|倉稲魂命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ}}
* {{ill|Oyamakui no Kami|lt=Ōyamakui-no-Kami|ja|大山祇神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oyamakui no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ|1=Ōyamatsumi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q386563}}

* {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}}
* {{ill|Kagu-tsuchi|lt=Ho-musubi-no-Kami|ja|火産霊神|1=Kagu-tsuchi|lt=Kagutsuchi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagu-tsuchi|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagutsuchi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to カグツチ}}
* [[:en:Ukemochi|Ukemochi-no-Kami]]
* [[:en:Yamatohime-no-mikoto|Yamatohime-no-Mikoto]]
{{Div col end}}

=== Inari Shrine ===
* {{ill|Ukanomitama|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Kami|ja|倉稲魂命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ}}
* {{ill|Ōkuninushi|lt=Ōkuninushi-no-Kami|ja|大己貴命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}
* {{ill|Ōta-no-Kami|ja|大田命|1=Ōta-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=太田命|4=fr|5=大田命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōta-no-Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōta-no-Mikoto|qq=}}
* [[:en:Ukemochi|Ukemochi-no-Kami]]
* {{ill|Ōmiya-no-Me|lt=Ōmiya-no-Me|lt=Ōmiyahime-no-Mikoto|lt=Ōmiyanome-no-Kami|ja|大宮能売神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmiyahime-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオミヤノメ}}

== History ==
The shrine is traditionally said to have been founded in [[:en:876|876]] (the 18th year of the [[:en:Zhenguan|Jōgan era]]). During the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]], the area was coastal and formed a sandbar called "Suzaki." Thus, the shrine became known as "Hama-no-Miya" (Shrine of the Beach).

The exact date of the establishment of the [[:en:Inari shrine|Inari Shrine]] is unknown.

== Shrine Buildings and Grounds ==
The grounds include the {{ill|Miyamado Inari Shrine|ja|海山道稲荷神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyamado Inari Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 洲崎濱宮神明神社}}, which surpasses the Shinmei Shrine in scale.

There are over 20 subsidiary [[:en:Inari shrine|Inari shrines]], a {{ill|Tenjin shrine|ja|天神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenjin shrine|1=Tenjinsha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11443279}} housing Japan’s largest statue of {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}}, and various monuments such as the Pen Mound, Eyeglass Mound, and Fox Mound.

== Festivals and Events ==
* festival: October 3–5
* Inari Grand Festival: April 8–9
* Fox Wedding Ceremony: Held on [[:en:Setsubun|Setsubun]] (February), this purification ritual involves a mock wedding procession. Participants representing the bride and groom wear fox masks, and a "bean-throwing" event is also held.

== location ==
1-62 Miyamado Town, [[:en:Yokkaichi|Yokkaichi]], [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]], Japan

== Access ==
* A short walk from [[:en:Kintetsu Nagoya Line|Kintetsu Nagoya Line]]'s [[:en:Miyamado Station|Miyamado Station]].

* [[:en:Ukemochi|Ukemochi-no-Kami]]
* {{ill|Ōmiya-no-Me|lt=Ōmiya-no-Me|lt=Ōmiyahime-no-Mikoto|lt=Ōmiyanome-no-Kami|ja|大宮能売神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmiyahime-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオミヤノメ}}

== History ==
The shrine is traditionally said to have been founded in [[:en:876|876]] (the 18th year of the [[:en:Zhenguan|Jōgan era]]). During the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]], the area was coastal and formed a sandbar called "Suzaki." Thus, the shrine became known as "Hama-no-Miya" (Shrine of the Beach).

The exact date of the establishment of the [[:en:Inari shrine|Inari Shrine]] is unknown.

== Shrine Buildings and Grounds ==
The grounds include the {{ill|Miyamado Inari Shrine|ja|海山道稲荷神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyamado Inari Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 洲崎濱宮神明神社}}, which surpasses the Shinmei Shrine in scale.

There are over 20 subsidiary [[:en:Inari shrine|Inari shrines]], a {{ill|Tenjin shrine|ja|天神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenjin shrine|1=Tenjinsha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11443279}} housing Japan’s largest statue of {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}}, and various monuments such as the Pen Mound, Eyeglass Mound, and Fox Mound.

== Festivals and Events ==
* festival: October 3–5
* Inari Grand Festival: April 8–9
* Fox Wedding Ceremony: Held on [[:en:Setsubun|Setsubun]] (February), this purification ritual involves a mock wedding procession. Participants representing the bride and groom wear fox masks, and a "bean-throwing" event is also held.

== location ==
1-62 Miyamado Town, [[:en:Yokkaichi|Yokkaichi]], [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]], Japan

== Access ==
* A short walk from [[:en:Kintetsu Nagoya Line|Kintetsu Nagoya Line]]'s [[:en:Miyamado Station|Miyamado Station]].

== See Also ==
{{Commonscat|Miyamado-jinja|Miyamado Shrine}}
* [[:en:Shinmei shrines|Shinmei Shrine]]
* [[:en:Inari shrine|Inari Shrine]]

'''Suzaki Hamamiya Shinmei Shrine''' ({{ill|Suzaki Hamamiya Shinmei Shrine|ja|洲崎濱宮神明神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suzaki Hamamiya Shinmei Shrine|1=Suzaki Hamamiya Shinmei Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11556511}}, ''Suzaki Hamamiya Shinmei Jinja'') is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in the [[Shiobama District]], [[:en:Yokkaichi|Yokkaichi]], [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]], Japan. It is often collectively referred to as "Miyamado Shrine" due to the large presence of its [[:en:Inari shrine|Inari Shrine]].
== Overview == [[File:Miyamado-inari(Mie).JPG|thumb|Miyamado Inari Shrine]] The shrine's name "濱" (''Hama'') is also written as "浜" or "濵."
The subordinate shrine, known as '''Miyamado Inari Shrine''' ({{ill|Miyamado Inari Shrine|ja|海山道稲荷神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyamado Inari Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 洲崎濱宮神明神社}}), is larger than the main Shinmei Shrine and is famous for the "Fox Wedding Ceremony" ([[:en:Kitsune no yomeiri|Fox Wedding]]).
The grounds also include multiple Inari shrines, {{ill|Tenjinsha|lt=Tenjin shrines|ja|天神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenjinsha|qq=}}, and unique features such as Japan’s largest statue of {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}}.
== Enshrined Deities == === Shinmei Shrine === {{Div col}} * {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|lt=Amenokoyane-no-Mikoto|ja|天児屋根命|1=Ame-no-Koyane|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天児屋命}} * [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]] * {{ill|Takemikazuchi|lt=Takemikazuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|建御雷命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takemikazuchi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タケミカヅチ}} * [[:en:Emperor Nintoku|Emperor Nintoku]] * {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}} * {{ill|Ukanomitama|lt-Ukanomitama-no-Kami|ja|倉稲魂命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ}} * {{ill|Oyamakui no Kami|lt=Ōyamakui-no-Kami|ja|大山祇神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oyamakui no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ|1=Ōyamatsumi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q386563}} * {{ill|Kagu-tsuchi|lt=Kagutsuchi-no-Kami|ja|火之迦具土神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagu-tsuchi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to カグツチ}} * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}} * {{ill|Kagu-tsuchi|lt=Ho-musubi-no-Kami|ja|火産霊神|1=Kagu-tsuchi|lt=Kagutsuchi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagu-tsuchi|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagutsuchi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to カグツチ}} * [[:en:Ukemochi|Ukemochi-no-Kami]] * [[:en:Yamatohime-no-mikoto|Yamatohime-no-Mikoto]] {{Div col end}}
=== Inari Shrine === * {{ill|Ukanomitama|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Kami|ja|倉稲魂命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ}} * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|lt=Ōkuninushi-no-Kami|ja|大己貴命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}} * {{ill|Ōta-no-Kami|ja|大田命|1=Ōta-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=太田命|4=fr|5=大田命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōta-no-Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōta-no-Mikoto|qq=}} * [[:en:Ukemochi|Ukemochi-no-Kami]] * {{ill|Ōmiya-no-Me|lt=Ōmiya-no-Me|lt=Ōmiyahime-no-Mikoto|lt=Ōmiyanome-no-Kami|ja|大宮能売神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmiyahime-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオミヤノメ}}
== History == The shrine is traditionally said to have been founded in [[:en:876|876]] (the 18th year of the [[:en:Zhenguan|Jōgan era]]). During the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]], the area was coastal and formed a sandbar called "Suzaki." Thus, the shrine became known as "Hama-no-Miya" (Shrine of the Beach).
The exact date of the establishment of the [[:en:Inari shrine|Inari Shrine]] is unknown.
== Shrine Buildings and Grounds == The grounds include the {{ill|Miyamado Inari Shrine|ja|海山道稲荷神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyamado Inari Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 洲崎濱宮神明神社}}, which surpasses the Shinmei Shrine in scale.
There are over 20 subsidiary [[:en:Inari shrine|Inari shrines]], a {{ill|Tenjin shrine|ja|天神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenjin shrine|1=Tenjinsha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11443279}} housing Japan’s largest statue of {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}}, and various monuments such as the Pen Mound, Eyeglass Mound, and Fox Mound.
== Festivals and Events == * festival: October 3–5 * Inari Grand Festival: April 8–9 * Fox Wedding Ceremony: Held on [[:en:Setsubun|Setsubun]] (February), this purification ritual involves a mock wedding procession. Participants representing the bride and groom wear fox masks, and a "bean-throwing" event is also held.
== location == 1-62 Miyamado Town, [[:en:Yokkaichi|Yokkaichi]], [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]], Japan
== Access == * A short walk from [[:en:Kintetsu Nagoya Line|Kintetsu Nagoya Line]]'s [[:en:Miyamado Station|Miyamado Station]].

'''Suzaki Hamamiya Shinmei Shrine''' ({{ill|Suzaki Hamamiya Shinmei Shrine|ja|洲崎濱宮神明神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suzaki Hamamiya Shinmei Shrine|1=Suzaki Hamamiya Shinmei Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11556511}}, ''Suzaki Hamamiya Shinmei Jinja'') is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in the [[Shiobama District]], [[:en:Yokkaichi|Yokkaichi]], [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]], Japan. It is often collectively referred to as "Miyamado Shrine" due to the large presence of its [[:en:Inari shrine|Inari Shrine]].
== Overview == [[File:Miyamado-inari(Mie).JPG|thumb|Miyamado Inari Shrine]] The shrine's name "濱" (''Hama'') is also written as "浜" or "濵."
The subordinate shrine, known as '''Miyamado Inari Shrine''' ({{ill|Miyamado Inari Shrine|ja|海山道稲荷神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyamado Inari Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 洲崎濱宮神明神社}}), is larger than the main Shinmei Shrine and is famous for the "Fox Wedding Ceremony" ([[:en:Kitsune no yomeiri|Fox Wedding]]).
The grounds also include multiple Inari shrines, {{ill|Tenjinsha|lt=Tenjin shrines|ja|天神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenjinsha|qq=}}, and unique features such as Japan’s largest statue of {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}}.
== Enshrined Deities == === Shinmei Shrine === {{Div col}} * {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|lt=Amenokoyane-no-Mikoto|ja|天児屋根命|1=Ame-no-Koyane|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天児屋命}} * [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]] * {{ill|Takemikazuchi|lt=Takemikazuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|建御雷命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takemikazuchi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タケミカヅチ}} * [[:en:Emperor Nintoku|Emperor Nintoku]] * {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}} * {{ill|Ukanomitama|lt-Ukanomitama-no-Kami|ja|倉稲魂命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ}} * {{ill|Oyamakui no Kami|lt=Ōyamakui-no-Kami|ja|大山祇神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oyamakui no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ|1=Ōyamatsumi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q386563}} * {{ill|Kagu-tsuchi|lt=Kagutsuchi-no-Kami|ja|火之迦具土神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagu-tsuchi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to カグツチ}} * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}} * {{ill|Kagu-tsuchi|lt=Ho-musubi-no-Kami|ja|火産霊神|1=Kagu-tsuchi|lt=Kagutsuchi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagu-tsuchi|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagutsuchi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to カグツチ}} * [[:en:Ukemochi|Ukemochi-no-Kami]] * [[:en:Yamatohime-no-mikoto|Yamatohime-no-Mikoto]] {{Div col end}}
=== Inari Shrine === * {{ill|Ukanomitama|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Kami|ja|倉稲魂命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ}} * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|lt=Ōkuninushi-no-Kami|ja|大己貴命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}} * {{ill|Ōta-no-Kami|ja|大田命|1=Ōta-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=太田命|4=fr|5=大田命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōta-no-Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōta-no-Mikoto|qq=}} * [[:en:Ukemochi|Ukemochi-no-Kami]] * {{ill|Ōmiya-no-Me|lt=Ōmiya-no-Me|lt=Ōmiyahime-no-Mikoto|lt=Ōmiyanome-no-Kami|ja|大宮能売神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmiyahime-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオミヤノメ}}
== History == The shrine is traditionally said to have been founded in [[:en:876|876]] (the 18th year of the [[:en:Zhenguan|Jōgan era]]). During the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]], the area was coastal and formed a sandbar called "Suzaki." Thus, the shrine became known as "Hama-no-Miya" (Shrine of the Beach).
The exact date of the establishment of the [[:en:Inari shrine|Inari Shrine]] is unknown.
== Shrine Buildings and Grounds == The grounds include the {{ill|Miyamado Inari Shrine|ja|海山道稲荷神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyamado Inari Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 洲崎濱宮神明神社}}, which surpasses the Shinmei Shrine in scale.
There are over 20 subsidiary [[:en:Inari shrine|Inari shrines]], a {{ill|Tenjin shrine|ja|天神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenjin shrine|1=Tenjinsha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11443279}} housing Japan’s largest statue of {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}}, and various monuments such as the Pen Mound, Eyeglass Mound, and Fox Mound.
== Festivals and Events == * festival: October 3–5 * Inari Grand Festival: April 8–9 * Fox Wedding Ceremony: Held on [[:en:Setsubun|Setsubun]] (February), this purification ritual involves a mock wedding procession. Participants representing the bride and groom wear fox masks, and a "bean-throwing" event is also held.
== location == 1-62 Miyamado Town, [[:en:Yokkaichi|Yokkaichi]], [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]], Japan
== Access == * A short walk from [[:en:Kintetsu Nagoya Line|Kintetsu Nagoya Line]]'s [[:en:Miyamado Station|Miyamado Station]].
== See Also == {{Commonscat|Miyamado-jinja|Miyamado Shrine}} * [[:en:Shinmei shrines|Shinmei Shrine]] * [[:en:Inari shrine|Inari Shrine]]

The subordinate shrine, known as '''Miyamado Inari Shrine''' ({{ill|Miyamado Inari Shrine|ja|海山道稲荷神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyamado Inari Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 洲崎濱宮神明神社}}), is larger than the main Shinmei Shrine and is famous for the "Fox Wedding Ceremony" ([[:en:Kitsune no yomeiri|Fox Wedding]]).

== Enshrined Deities == === Shinmei Shrine === {{Div col}} * {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|lt=Amenokoyane-no-Mikoto|ja|天児屋根命|1=Ame-no-Koyane|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天児屋命}} * [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]] * {{ill|Takemikazuchi|lt=Takemikazuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|建御雷命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takemikazuchi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タケミカヅチ}} * [[:en:Emperor Nintoku|Emperor Nintoku]] * {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}} * {{ill|Ukanomitama|lt-Ukanomitama-no-Kami|ja|倉稲魂命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ}} * {{ill|Oyamakui no Kami|lt=Ōyamakui-no-Kami|ja|大山祇神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oyamakui no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ|1=Ōyamatsumi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q386563}} * {{ill|Kagu-tsuchi|lt=Kagutsuchi-no-Kami|ja|火之迦具土神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagu-tsuchi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to カグツチ}} * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}} * {{ill|Kagu-tsuchi|lt=Ho-musubi-no-Kami|ja|火産霊神|1=Kagu-tsuchi|lt=Kagutsuchi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagu-tsuchi|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagutsuchi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to カグツチ}} * [[:en:Ukemochi|Ukemochi-no-Kami]] * [[:en:Yamatohime-no-mikoto|Yamatohime-no-Mikoto]] {{Div col end}}
=== Inari Shrine === * {{ill|Ukanomitama|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Kami|ja|倉稲魂命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ}} * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|lt=Ōkuninushi-no-Kami|ja|大己貴命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}} * {{ill|Ōta-no-Kami|ja|大田命|1=Ōta-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=太田命|4=fr|5=大田命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōta-no-Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōta-no-Mikoto|qq=}} * [[:en:Ukemochi|Ukemochi-no-Kami]] * {{ill|Ōmiya-no-Me|lt=Ōmiya-no-Me|lt=Ōmiyahime-no-Mikoto|lt=Ōmiyanome-no-Kami|ja|大宮能売神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmiyahime-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオミヤノメ}}
== History == The shrine is traditionally said to have been founded in [[:en:876|876]] (the 18th year of the [[:en:Zhenguan|Jōgan era]]). During the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]], the area was coastal and formed a sandbar called "Suzaki." Thus, the shrine became known as "Hama-no-Miya" (Shrine of the Beach).
The exact date of the establishment of the [[:en:Inari shrine|Inari Shrine]] is unknown.
== Shrine Buildings and Grounds == The grounds include the {{ill|Miyamado Inari Shrine|ja|海山道稲荷神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyamado Inari Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 洲崎濱宮神明神社}}, which surpasses the Shinmei Shrine in scale.
There are over 20 subsidiary [[:en:Inari shrine|Inari shrines]], a {{ill|Tenjin shrine|ja|天神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenjin shrine|1=Tenjinsha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11443279}} housing Japan’s largest statue of {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}}, and various monuments such as the Pen Mound, Eyeglass Mound, and Fox Mound.
== Festivals and Events == * festival: October 3–5 * Inari Grand Festival: April 8–9 * Fox Wedding Ceremony: Held on [[:en:Setsubun|Setsubun]] (February), this purification ritual involves a mock wedding procession. Participants representing the bride and groom wear fox masks, and a "bean-throwing" event is also held.
== location == 1-62 Miyamado Town, [[:en:Yokkaichi|Yokkaichi]], [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]], Japan
== Access == * A short walk from [[:en:Kintetsu Nagoya Line|Kintetsu Nagoya Line]]'s [[:en:Miyamado Station|Miyamado Station]].
== See Also == {{Commonscat|Miyamado-jinja|Miyamado Shrine}} * [[:en:Shinmei shrines|Shinmei Shrine]] * [[:en:Inari shrine|Inari Shrine]]
{{Shinto-stub}}

There are over 20 subsidiary [[:en:Inari shrine|Inari shrines]], a {{ill|Tenjin shrine|ja|天神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenjin shrine|1=Tenjinsha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11443279}} housing Japan’s largest statue of {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}}, and various monuments such as the Pen Mound, Eyeglass Mound, and Fox Mound.
== Festivals and Events == * festival: October 3–5 * Inari Grand Festival: April 8–9 * Fox Wedding Ceremony: Held on [[:en:Setsubun|Setsubun]] (February), this purification ritual involves a mock wedding procession. Participants representing the bride and groom wear fox masks, and a "bean-throwing" event is also held.
== location == 1-62 Miyamado Town, [[:en:Yokkaichi|Yokkaichi]], [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]], Japan

'''Suzaki Hamamiya Shinmei Shrine''' ({{ill|Suzaki Hamamiya Shinmei Shrine|ja|洲崎濱宮神明神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suzaki Hamamiya Shinmei Shrine|1=Suzaki Hamamiya Shinmei Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11556511}}, ''Suzaki Hamamiya Shinmei Jinja'') is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in the [[Shiobama District]], [[:en:Yokkaichi|Yokkaichi]], [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]], Japan. It is often collectively referred to as "Miyamado Shrine" due to the large presence of its [[:en:Inari shrine|Inari Shrine]].
== Overview == [[File:Miyamado-inari(Mie).JPG|thumb|Miyamado Inari Shrine]] The shrine's name "濱" (''Hama'') is also written as "浜" or "濵."
The subordinate shrine, known as '''Miyamado Inari Shrine''' ({{ill|Miyamado Inari Shrine|ja|海山道稲荷神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyamado Inari Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 洲崎濱宮神明神社}}), is larger than the main Shinmei Shrine and is famous for the "Fox Wedding Ceremony" ([[:en:Kitsune no yomeiri|Fox Wedding]]).
The grounds also include multiple Inari shrines, {{ill|Tenjinsha|lt=Tenjin shrines|ja|天神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenjinsha|qq=}}, and unique features such as Japan’s largest statue of {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}}.
== Enshrined Deities == === Shinmei Shrine === {{Div col}} * {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|lt=Amenokoyane-no-Mikoto|ja|天児屋根命|1=Ame-no-Koyane|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天児屋命}} * [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]] * {{ill|Takemikazuchi|lt=Takemikazuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|建御雷命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takemikazuchi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タケミカヅチ}} * [[:en:Emperor Nintoku|Emperor Nintoku]] * {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}} * {{ill|Ukanomitama|lt-Ukanomitama-no-Kami|ja|倉稲魂命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ}} * {{ill|Oyamakui no Kami|lt=Ōyamakui-no-Kami|ja|大山祇神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oyamakui no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ|1=Ōyamatsumi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q386563}} * {{ill|Kagu-tsuchi|lt=Kagutsuchi-no-Kami|ja|火之迦具土神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagu-tsuchi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to カグツチ}} * {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}} * {{ill|Kagu-tsuchi|lt=Ho-musubi-no-Kami|ja|火産霊神|1=Kagu-tsuchi|lt=Kagutsuchi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagu-tsuchi|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagutsuchi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to カグツチ}} * [[:en:Ukemochi|Ukemochi-no-Kami]] * [[:en:Yamatohime-no-mikoto|Yamatohime-no-Mikoto]] {{Div col end}}
=== Inari Shrine === * {{ill|Ukanomitama|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Kami|ja|倉稲魂命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ}} * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|lt=Ōkuninushi-no-Kami|ja|大己貴命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}} * {{ill|Ōta-no-Kami|ja|大田命|1=Ōta-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=太田命|4=fr|5=大田命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōta-no-Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōta-no-Mikoto|qq=}} * [[:en:Ukemochi|Ukemochi-no-Kami]] * {{ill|Ōmiya-no-Me|lt=Ōmiya-no-Me|lt=Ōmiyahime-no-Mikoto|lt=Ōmiya-no-Me-no-Kami|ja|大宮能売神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmiyahime-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオミヤノメ}}
== History == The shrine is traditionally said to have been founded in [[:en:876|876]] (the 18th year of the [[:en:Zhenguan|Jōgan era]]). During the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]], the area was coastal and formed a sandbar called "Suzaki." Thus, the shrine became known as "Hama-no-Miya" (Shrine of the Beach).
The exact date of the establishment of the [[:en:Inari shrine|Inari Shrine]] is unknown.
== Shrine Buildings and Grounds == The grounds include the {{ill|Miyamado Inari Shrine|ja|海山道稲荷神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyamado Inari Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 洲崎濱宮神明神社}}, which surpasses the Shinmei Shrine in scale.
There are over 20 subsidiary [[:en:Inari shrine|Inari shrines]], a {{ill|Tenjin shrine|ja|天神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenjin shrine|1=Tenjinsha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11443279}} housing Japan’s largest statue of {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}}, and various monuments such as the Pen Mound, Eyeglass Mound, and Fox Mound.
== Festivals and Events == * festival: October 3–5 * Inari Grand Festival: April 8–9 * Fox Wedding Ceremony: Held on [[:en:Setsubun|Setsubun]] (February), this purification ritual involves a mock wedding procession. Participants representing the bride and groom wear fox masks, and a "bean-throwing" event is also held.
== location == 1-62 Miyamado Town, [[:en:Yokkaichi|Yokkaichi]], [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]], Japan
== Access == * A short walk from [[:en:Kintetsu Nagoya Line|Kintetsu Nagoya Line]]'s [[:en:Miyamado Station|Miyamado Station]].

'''Suzaki Hamamiya Shinmei Shrine''' ({{ill|Suzaki Hamamiya Shinmei Shrine|ja|洲崎濱宮神明神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suzaki Hamamiya Shinmei Shrine|1=Suzaki Hamamiya Shinmei Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11556511}}, ''Suzaki Hamamiya Shinmei Jinja'') is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in the [[Shiobama District]], {{ill|Yokkaichi|en|Yokkaichi|WD=Q26410|lt=Yokkaichi}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|en|Mie Prefecture|WD=Q128196|lt=Mie Prefecture}}, Japan. It is often collectively referred to as "Miyamado Shrine" due to the large presence of its {{ill|Inari shrine|en|Inari shrine|WD=Q514480|lt=Inari Shrine}}.

== Overview ==
[[File:Miyamado-inari(Mie).JPG|thumb|Miyamado Inari Shrine]]
The shrine's name "濱" (''Hama'') is also written as "浜" or "濵."

The subordinate shrine, known as '''Miyamado Inari Shrine''' ({{ill|Miyamado Inari Shrine|ja|海山道稲荷神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyamado Inari Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 洲崎濱宮神明神社}}), is larger than the main Shinmei Shrine and is famous for the "Fox Wedding Ceremony" ({{ill|Kitsune no yomeiri|en|Kitsune no yomeiri|WD=Q11571371|lt=Fox Wedding}}). 

The grounds also include multiple Inari shrines, {{ill|Tenjinsha|lt=Tenjin shrines|ja|天神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenjinsha|qq=}}, and unique features such as Japan’s largest statue of {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}}.

== Enshrined Deities ==
=== Shinmei Shrine ===
{{Div col}}
* {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|lt=Amenokoyane-no-Mikoto|ja|天児屋根命|1=Ame-no-Koyane|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天児屋命}}
* {{ill|Amaterasu|en|Amaterasu|WD=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami}}
* {{ill|Takemikazuchi|lt=Takemikazuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|建御雷命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takemikazuchi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タケミカヅチ}}
* {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|en|Emperor Nintoku|WD=Q313119|lt=Emperor Nintoku}}
* {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}}
* {{ill|Ukanomitama|lt-Ukanomitama-no-Kami|ja|倉稲魂命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ}}
* {{ill|Oyamakui no Kami|lt=Ōyamakui-no-Kami|ja|大山祇神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oyamakui no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ|1=Ōyamatsumi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q386563}}
* {{ill|Kagu-tsuchi|lt=Kagutsuchi-no-Kami|ja|火之迦具土神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagu-tsuchi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to カグツチ}}
* {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}}
* {{ill|Kagu-tsuchi|lt=Ho-musubi-no-Kami|ja|火産霊神|1=Kagu-tsuchi|lt=Kagutsuchi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagu-tsuchi|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagutsuchi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to カグツチ}}
* {{ill|Ukemochi|en|Ukemochi|WD=Q2280323|lt=Ukemochi-no-Kami}}
* {{ill|Yamatohime-no-mikoto|en|Yamatohime-no-mikoto|WD=Q4388732|lt=Yamatohime-no-Mikoto}}
{{Div col end}}

=== Inari Shrine ===
* {{ill|Ukanomitama|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Kami|ja|倉稲魂命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ}}
* {{ill|Ōkuninushi|lt=Ōkuninushi-no-Kami|ja|大己貴命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}
* {{ill|Ōta-no-Kami|ja|大田命|1=Ōta-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=太田命|4=fr|5=大田命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōta-no-Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōta-no-Mikoto|qq=}}
* {{ill|Ukemochi|en|Ukemochi|WD=Q2280323|lt=Ukemochi-no-Kami}}
* {{ill|Ōmiya-no-Me|lt=Ōmiya-no-Me|lt=Ōmiyahime-no-Mikoto|lt=Ōmiya-no-Me-no-Kami|ja|大宮能売神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmiyahime-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオミヤノメ}}

== History ==
The shrine is traditionally said to have been founded in {{ill|876|en|876|WD=Q23815|lt=876}} (the 18th year of the {{ill|Zhenguan|en|Zhenguan|WD=Q3577278|lt=Jōgan era}}). During the {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian period}}, the area was coastal and formed a sandbar called "Suzaki." Thus, the shrine became known as "Hama-no-Miya" (Shrine of the Beach).

The exact date of the establishment of the {{ill|Inari shrine|en|Inari shrine|WD=Q514480|lt=Inari Shrine}} is unknown.

== Shrine Buildings and Grounds ==
The grounds include the {{ill|Miyamado Inari Shrine|ja|海山道稲荷神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyamado Inari Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 洲崎濱宮神明神社}}, which surpasses the Shinmei Shrine in scale.

There are over 20 subsidiary {{ill|Inari shrine|en|Inari shrine|WD=Q514480|lt=Inari shrines}}, a {{ill|Tenjin shrine|ja|天神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenjin shrine|1=Tenjinsha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11443279}} housing Japan’s largest statue of {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}}, and various monuments such as the Pen Mound, Eyeglass Mound, and Fox Mound.

== Festivals and Events ==
* festival: October 3–5
* Inari Grand Festival: April 8–9
* Fox Wedding Ceremony: Held on {{ill|Setsubun|en|Setsubun|WD=Q746798|lt=Setsubun}} (February), this purification ritual involves a mock wedding procession. Participants representing the bride and groom wear fox masks, and a "bean-throwing" event is also held.

== location ==
1-62 Miyamado Town, {{ill|Yokkaichi|en|Yokkaichi|WD=Q26410|lt=Yokkaichi}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|en|Mie Prefecture|WD=Q128196|lt=Mie Prefecture}}, Japan

== Access ==
* A short walk from {{ill|Kintetsu Nagoya Line|en|Kintetsu Nagoya Line|WD=Q1056563|lt=Kintetsu Nagoya Line}}'s {{ill|Miyamado Station|en|Miyamado Station|WD=Q11558874|lt=Miyamado Station}}.

| founded = {{ill|876|en|876|WD=Q23815|lt=876}} (18th year of the {{ill|Zhenguan|en|Zhenguan|WD=Q3577278|lt=Jōgan era}})
| main_building_style = 
| festivals = October 3–5 (festival); February (Fox Wedding Ceremony)
| map_type = Japan Mie
| location = {{ill|Yokkaichi|WD=Q26410}}
| coordinates = {{Coord|34.942778|N|136.621667|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}

'''Suzaki Hamamiya Shinmei Shrine''' ({{ill|Suzaki Hamamiya Shinmei Shrine|ja|洲崎濱宮神明神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suzaki Hamamiya Shinmei Shrine|1=Suzaki Hamamiya Shinmei Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11556511}}, ''Suzaki Hamamiya Shinmei Jinja'') is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in the [[Shiobama District]], {{ill|Yokkaichi|en|Yokkaichi|WD=Q26410|lt=Yokkaichi}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|en|Mie Prefecture|WD=Q128196|lt=Mie Prefecture}}, Japan. It is often collectively referred to as "Miyamado Shrine" due to the large presence of its {{ill|Inari shrine|en|Inari shrine|WD=Q514480|lt=Inari Shrine}}.

== Overview ==
[[File:Miyamado-inari(Mie).JPG|thumb|Miyamado Inari Shrine]]
The shrine's name "濱" (''Hama'') is also written as "浜" or "濵."

The subordinate shrine, known as '''Miyamado Inari Shrine''' ({{ill|Miyamado Inari Shrine|ja|海山道稲荷神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyamado Inari Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 洲崎濱宮神明神社}}), is larger than the main Shinmei Shrine and is famous for the "Fox Wedding Ceremony" ({{ill|Kitsune no yomeiri|en|Kitsune no yomeiri|WD=Q11571371|lt=Fox Wedding}}). 

The grounds also include multiple Inari shrines, {{ill|Tenjinsha|lt=Tenjin shrines|ja|天神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenjinsha|qq=}}, and unique features such as Japan’s largest statue of {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}}.

== Enshrined Deities ==
=== Shinmei Shrine ===
{{Div col}}
* {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|lt=Amenokoyane-no-Mikoto|ja|天児屋根命|1=Ame-no-Koyane|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天児屋命}}
* {{ill|Amaterasu|en|Amaterasu|WD=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami}}
* {{ill|Takemikazuchi|lt=Takemikazuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|建御雷命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takemikazuchi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タケミカヅチ}}
* {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|en|Emperor Nintoku|WD=Q313119|lt=Emperor Nintoku}}
* {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}}
* {{ill|Ukanomitama|lt-Ukanomitama-no-Kami|ja|倉稲魂命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ}}
* {{ill|Oyamakui no Kami|lt=Ōyamakui-no-Kami|ja|大山祇神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oyamakui no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ|1=Ōyamatsumi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q386563}}
* {{ill|Kagu-tsuchi|lt=Kagutsuchi-no-Kami|ja|火之迦具土神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagu-tsuchi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to カグツチ}}
* {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}}
* {{ill|Kagu-tsuchi|lt=Ho-musubi-no-Kami|ja|火産霊神|1=Kagu-tsuchi|lt=Kagutsuchi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagu-tsuchi|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagutsuchi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to カグツチ}}
* {{ill|Ukemochi|en|Ukemochi|WD=Q2280323|lt=Ukemochi-no-Kami}}
* {{ill|Yamatohime-no-mikoto|en|Yamatohime-no-mikoto|WD=Q4388732|lt=Yamatohime-no-Mikoto}}
{{Div col end}}

=== Inari Shrine ===
* {{ill|Ukanomitama|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Kami|ja|倉稲魂命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ}}
* {{ill|Ōkuninushi|lt=Ōkuninushi-no-Kami|ja|大己貴命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}
* {{ill|Ōta-no-Kami|ja|大田命|1=Ōta-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=太田命|4=fr|5=大田命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōta-no-Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōta-no-Mikoto|qq=}}
* {{ill|Ukemochi|en|Ukemochi|WD=Q2280323|lt=Ukemochi-no-Kami}}
* {{ill|Ōmiya-no-Me|lt=Ōmiya-no-Me|lt=Ōmiyahime-no-Mikoto|lt=Ōmiya-no-Me-no-Kami|ja|大宮能売神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmiyahime-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオミヤノメ}}

== History ==
The shrine is traditionally said to have been founded in {{ill|876|en|876|WD=Q23815|lt=876}} (the 18th year of the {{ill|Zhenguan|en|Zhenguan|WD=Q3577278|lt=Jōgan era}}). During the {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian period}}, the area was coastal and formed a sandbar called "Suzaki." Thus, the shrine became known as "Hama-no-Miya" (Shrine of the Beach).

The exact date of the establishment of the {{ill|Inari shrine|en|Inari shrine|WD=Q514480|lt=Inari Shrine}} is unknown.

== Shrine Buildings and Grounds ==
The grounds include the {{ill|Miyamado Inari Shrine|ja|海山道稲荷神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyamado Inari Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 洲崎濱宮神明神社}}, which surpasses the Shinmei Shrine in scale.

There are over 20 subsidiary {{ill|Inari shrine|en|Inari shrine|WD=Q514480|lt=Inari shrines}}, a {{ill|Tenjin shrine|ja|天神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenjin shrine|1=Tenjinsha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11443279}} housing Japan’s largest statue of {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}}, and various monuments such as the Pen Mound, Eyeglass Mound, and Fox Mound.

== Festivals and Events ==
* festival: October 3–5
* Inari Grand Festival: April 8–9
* Fox Wedding Ceremony: Held on {{ill|Setsubun|en|Setsubun|WD=Q746798|lt=Setsubun}} (February), this purification ritual involves a mock wedding procession. Participants representing the bride and groom wear fox masks, and a "bean-throwing" event is also held.

== location ==
1-62 Miyamado Town, {{ill|Yokkaichi|en|Yokkaichi|WD=Q26410|lt=Yokkaichi}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|en|Mie Prefecture|WD=Q128196|lt=Mie Prefecture}}, Japan

== Access ==
* A short walk from {{ill|Kintetsu Nagoya Line|en|Kintetsu Nagoya Line|WD=Q1056563|lt=Kintetsu Nagoya Line}}'s {{ill|Miyamado Station|en|Miyamado Station|WD=Q11558874|lt=Miyamado Station}}.

* {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|lt=Amenokoyane-no-Mikoto|ja|天児屋根命|1=Ame-no-Koyane|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天児屋命}}
* {{ill|Amaterasu|en|Amaterasu|WD=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami}}
* {{ill|Takemikazuchi|lt=Takemikazuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|建御雷命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takemikazuchi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タケミカヅチ}}
* {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|en|Emperor Nintoku|WD=Q313119|lt=Emperor Nintoku}}
* {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}}
* {{ill|Ukanomitama|lt-Ukanomitama-no-Kami|ja|倉稲魂命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ}}
* {{ill|Oyamakui no Kami|lt=Ōyamakui-no-Kami|ja|大山祇神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oyamakui no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ|1=Ōyamatsumi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q386563}}
* {{ill|Kagu-tsuchi|lt=Kagutsuchi-no-Kami|ja|火之迦具土神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagu-tsuchi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to カグツチ}}
* {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}}
* {{ill|Kagu-tsuchi|lt=Ho-musubi-no-Kami|ja|火産霊神|1=Kagu-tsuchi|lt=Kagutsuchi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagu-tsuchi|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagutsuchi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to カグツチ}}

* {{ill|Yamatohime-no-mikoto|en|Yamatohime-no-mikoto|WD=Q4388732|lt=Yamatohime-no-Mikoto}}
{{Div col end}}

=== Inari Shrine ===
* {{ill|Ukanomitama|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Kami|ja|倉稲魂命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ}}
* {{ill|Ōkuninushi|lt=Ōkuninushi-no-Kami|ja|大己貴命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}
* {{ill|Ōta-no-Kami|ja|大田命|1=Ōta-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=太田命|4=fr|5=大田命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōta-no-Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōta-no-Mikoto|qq=}}
* {{ill|Ukemochi|en|Ukemochi|WD=Q2280323|lt=Ukemochi-no-Kami}}
* {{ill|Ōmiya-no-Me|lt=Ōmiya-no-Me|lt=Ōmiyahime-no-Mikoto|lt=Ōmiya-no-Me-no-Kami|ja|大宮能売神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmiyahime-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオミヤノメ}}

== History ==
The shrine is traditionally said to have been founded in {{ill|876|en|876|WD=Q23815|lt=876}} (the 18th year of the {{ill|Zhenguan|en|Zhenguan|WD=Q3577278|lt=Jōgan era}}). During the {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian period}}, the area was coastal and formed a sandbar called "Suzaki." Thus, the shrine became known as "Hama-no-Miya" (Shrine of the Beach).

The exact date of the establishment of the {{ill|Inari shrine|en|Inari shrine|WD=Q514480|lt=Inari Shrine}} is unknown.

== Shrine Buildings and Grounds ==
The grounds include the {{ill|Miyamado Inari Shrine|ja|海山道稲荷神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyamado Inari Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 洲崎濱宮神明神社}}, which surpasses the Shinmei Shrine in scale.

There are over 20 subsidiary {{ill|Inari shrine|en|Inari shrine|WD=Q514480|lt=Inari shrines}}, a {{ill|Tenjin shrine|ja|天神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenjin shrine|1=Tenjinsha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11443279}} housing Japan’s largest statue of {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}}, and various monuments such as the Pen Mound, Eyeglass Mound, and Fox Mound.

== Festivals and Events ==
* festival: October 3–5
* Inari Grand Festival: April 8–9
* Fox Wedding Ceremony: Held on {{ill|Setsubun|en|Setsubun|WD=Q746798|lt=Setsubun}} (February), this purification ritual involves a mock wedding procession. Participants representing the bride and groom wear fox masks, and a "bean-throwing" event is also held.

== location ==
1-62 Miyamado Town, {{ill|Yokkaichi|en|Yokkaichi|WD=Q26410|lt=Yokkaichi}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|en|Mie Prefecture|WD=Q128196|lt=Mie Prefecture}}, Japan

== Access ==
* A short walk from {{ill|Kintetsu Nagoya Line|en|Kintetsu Nagoya Line|WD=Q1056563|lt=Kintetsu Nagoya Line}}'s {{ill|Miyamado Station|en|Miyamado Station|WD=Q11558874|lt=Miyamado Station}}.

'''Suzaki Hamamiya Shinmei Shrine''' ({{ill|Suzaki Hamamiya Shinmei Shrine|ja|洲崎濱宮神明神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suzaki Hamamiya Shinmei Shrine|1=Suzaki Hamamiya Shinmei Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11556511}}, ''Suzaki Hamamiya Shinmei Jinja'') is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in the [[Shiobama District]], {{ill|Yokkaichi|en|Yokkaichi|WD=Q26410|lt=Yokkaichi}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|en|Mie Prefecture|WD=Q128196|lt=Mie Prefecture}}, Japan. It is often collectively referred to as "Miyamado Shrine" due to the large presence of its {{ill|Inari shrine|en|Inari shrine|WD=Q514480|lt=Inari Shrine}}.

== Overview ==
[[File:Miyamado-inari(Mie).JPG|thumb|Miyamado Inari Shrine]]
The shrine's name "濱" (''Hama'') is also written as "浜" or "濵."

The subordinate shrine, known as '''Miyamado Inari Shrine''' ({{ill|Miyamado Inari Shrine|ja|海山道稲荷神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyamado Inari Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 洲崎濱宮神明神社}}), is larger than the main Shinmei Shrine and is famous for the "Fox Wedding Ceremony" ({{ill|Kitsune no yomeiri|en|Kitsune no yomeiri|WD=Q11571371|lt=Fox Wedding}}). 

The grounds also include multiple Inari shrines, {{ill|Tenjinsha|lt=Tenjin shrines|ja|天神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenjinsha|qq=}}, and unique features such as Japan’s largest statue of {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}}.

* {{ill|Yamatohime-no-mikoto|en|Yamatohime-no-mikoto|WD=Q4388732|lt=Yamatohime-no-Mikoto}}

During the {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian period}}, the area was coastal and formed a sandbar called "Suzaki." Thus, the shrine became known as "Hama-no-Miya" (Shrine of the Beach).

The exact date of the establishment of the {{ill|Inari shrine|en|Inari shrine|WD=Q514480|lt=Inari Shrine}} is unknown.

* {{ill|Ukanomitama|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Kami|ja|倉稲魂命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ}}
* {{ill|Ōkuninushi|lt=Ōkuninushi-no-Kami|ja|大己貴命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}

* {{ill|Ukemochi|en|Ukemochi|WD=Q2280323|lt=Ukemochi-no-Kami}}

== History ==
The shrine is traditionally said to have been founded in {{ill|876|en|876|WD=Q23815|lt=876}} (the 18th year of the {{ill|Zhenguan|en|Zhenguan|WD=Q3577278|lt=Jōgan era}}). During the {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian period}}, the area was coastal and formed a sandbar called "Suzaki." Thus, the shrine became known as "Hama-no-Miya" (Shrine of the Beach).

The exact date of the establishment of the {{ill|Inari shrine|en|Inari shrine|WD=Q514480|lt=Inari Shrine}} is unknown.

== Shrine Buildings and Grounds ==
The grounds include the {{ill|Miyamado Inari Shrine|ja|海山道稲荷神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyamado Inari Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 洲崎濱宮神明神社}}, which surpasses the Shinmei Shrine in scale.

There are over 20 subsidiary {{ill|Inari shrine|en|Inari shrine|WD=Q514480|lt=Inari shrines}}, a {{ill|Tenjin shrine|ja|天神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenjin shrine|1=Tenjinsha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11443279}} housing Japan’s largest statue of {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}}, and various monuments such as the Pen Mound, Eyeglass Mound, and Fox Mound.

== Festivals and Events ==
* festival: October 3–5
* Inari Grand Festival: April 8–9
* Fox Wedding Ceremony: Held on {{ill|Setsubun|en|Setsubun|WD=Q746798|lt=Setsubun}} (February), this purification ritual involves a mock wedding procession. Participants representing the bride and groom wear fox masks, and a "bean-throwing" event is also held.

== location ==
1-62 Miyamado Town, {{ill|Yokkaichi|en|Yokkaichi|WD=Q26410|lt=Yokkaichi}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|en|Mie Prefecture|WD=Q128196|lt=Mie Prefecture}}, Japan

== Access ==
* A short walk from {{ill|Kintetsu Nagoya Line|en|Kintetsu Nagoya Line|WD=Q1056563|lt=Kintetsu Nagoya Line}}'s {{ill|Miyamado Station|en|Miyamado Station|WD=Q11558874|lt=Miyamado Station}}.

'''Suzaki Hamamiya Shinmei Shrine''' ({{ill|Suzaki Hamamiya Shinmei Shrine|ja|洲崎濱宮神明神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suzaki Hamamiya Shinmei Shrine|1=Suzaki Hamamiya Shinmei Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11556511}}, ''Suzaki Hamamiya Shinmei Jinja'') is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in the [[Shiobama District]], {{ill|Yokkaichi|en|Yokkaichi|WD=Q26410|lt=Yokkaichi}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|en|Mie Prefecture|WD=Q128196|lt=Mie Prefecture}}, Japan. It is often collectively referred to as "Miyamado Shrine" due to the large presence of its {{ill|Inari shrine|en|Inari shrine|WD=Q514480|lt=Inari Shrine}}.

== Overview ==
[[File:Miyamado-inari(Mie).JPG|thumb|Miyamado Inari Shrine]]
The shrine's name "濱" (''Hama'') is also written as "浜" or "濵."

The subordinate shrine, known as '''Miyamado Inari Shrine''' ({{ill|Miyamado Inari Shrine|ja|海山道稲荷神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyamado Inari Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 洲崎濱宮神明神社}}), is larger than the main Shinmei Shrine and is famous for the "Fox Wedding Ceremony" ({{ill|Kitsune no yomeiri|en|Kitsune no yomeiri|WD=Q11571371|lt=Fox Wedding}}). 

The grounds also include multiple Inari shrines, {{ill|Tenjinsha|lt=Tenjin shrines|ja|天神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenjinsha|qq=}}, and unique features such as Japan’s largest statue of {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}}.

* Fox Wedding Ceremony: Held on {{ill|Setsubun|en|Setsubun|WD=Q746798|lt=Setsubun}} (February), this purification ritual involves a mock wedding procession. Participants representing the bride and groom wear fox masks, and a "bean-throwing" event is also held.

== location ==
1-62 Miyamado Town, {{ill|Yokkaichi|en|Yokkaichi|WD=Q26410|lt=Yokkaichi}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|en|Mie Prefecture|WD=Q128196|lt=Mie Prefecture}}, Japan

== Access ==
* A short walk from {{ill|Kintetsu Nagoya Line|en|Kintetsu Nagoya Line|WD=Q1056563|lt=Kintetsu Nagoya Line}}'s {{ill|Miyamado Station|en|Miyamado Station|WD=Q11558874|lt=Miyamado Station}}.

'''Suzaki Hamamiya Shinmei Shrine''' ({{ill|Suzaki Hamamiya Shinmei Shrine|ja|洲崎濱宮神明神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suzaki Hamamiya Shinmei Shrine|1=Suzaki Hamamiya Shinmei Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11556511}}, ''Suzaki Hamamiya Shinmei Jinja'') is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in the [[Shiobama District]], {{ill|Yokkaichi|en|Yokkaichi|WD=Q26410|lt=Yokkaichi}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|en|Mie Prefecture|WD=Q128196|lt=Mie Prefecture}}, Japan. It is often collectively referred to as "Miyamado Shrine" due to the large presence of its {{ill|Inari shrine|en|Inari shrine|WD=Q514480|lt=Inari Shrine}}.

== Overview ==
[[File:Miyamado-inari(Mie).JPG|thumb|Miyamado Inari Shrine]]
The shrine's name "濱" (''Hama'') is also written as "浜" or "濵."

The subordinate shrine, known as '''Miyamado Inari Shrine''' ({{ill|Miyamado Inari Shrine|ja|海山道稲荷神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyamado Inari Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 洲崎濱宮神明神社}}), is larger than the main Shinmei Shrine and is famous for the "Fox Wedding Ceremony" ({{ill|Kitsune no yomeiri|en|Kitsune no yomeiri|WD=Q11571371|lt=Fox Wedding}}). 

The grounds also include multiple Inari shrines, {{ill|Tenjinsha|lt=Tenjin shrines|ja|天神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenjinsha|qq=}}, and unique features such as Japan’s largest statue of {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}}.

== Enshrined Deities ==
=== Shinmei Shrine ===
{{Div col}}
* {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|WD=Q2564508|lt=Amenokoyane-no-Mikoto|ja|天児屋根命|1=Ame-no-Koyane|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天児屋命}}
* {{ill|Amaterasu|en|Amaterasu|WD=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami}}
* {{ill|Takemikazuchi|WD=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|建御雷命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takemikazuchi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タケミカヅチ}}
* {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|en|Emperor Nintoku|WD=Q313119|lt=Emperor Nintoku}}
* {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}}
* {{ill|Ukanomitama|lt-Ukanomitama-no-Kami|ja|倉稲魂命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ}}
* {{ill|Oyamakui no Kami|lt=Ōyamakui-no-Kami|ja|大山祇神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oyamakui no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ|1=Ōyamatsumi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q386563}}
* {{ill|Kagu-tsuchi|lt=Kagutsuchi-no-Kami|ja|火之迦具土神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagu-tsuchi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to カグツチ}}
* {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}}
* {{ill|Kagu-tsuchi|lt=Ho-musubi-no-Kami|ja|火産霊神|1=Kagu-tsuchi|lt=Kagutsuchi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagu-tsuchi|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagutsuchi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to カグツチ}}
* {{ill|Ukemochi|en|Ukemochi|WD=Q2280323|lt=Ukemochi-no-Kami}}
* {{ill|Yamatohime-no-mikoto|en|Yamatohime-no-mikoto|WD=Q4388732|lt=Yamatohime-no-Mikoto}}
{{Div col end}}

=== Inari Shrine ===
* {{ill|Ukanomitama|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Kami|ja|倉稲魂命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ}}
* {{ill|Ōkuninushi|lt=Ōkuninushi-no-Kami|ja|大己貴命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}
* {{ill|Ōta-no-Kami|ja|大田命|1=Ōta-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=太田命|4=fr|5=大田命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōta-no-Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōta-no-Mikoto|qq=}}
* {{ill|Ukemochi|en|Ukemochi|WD=Q2280323|lt=Ukemochi-no-Kami}}
* {{ill|Ōmiya-no-Me|lt=Ōmiya-no-Me|lt=Ōmiyahime-no-Mikoto|lt=Ōmiya-no-Me-no-Kami|ja|大宮能売神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmiyahime-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオミヤノメ}}

== History ==
The shrine is traditionally said to have been founded in {{ill|876|en|876|WD=Q23815|lt=876}} (the 18th year of the {{ill|Zhenguan|en|Zhenguan|WD=Q3577278|lt=Jōgan era}}). During the {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian period}}, the area was coastal and formed a sandbar called "Suzaki." Thus, the shrine became known as "Hama-no-Miya" (Shrine of the Beach).

The exact date of the establishment of the {{ill|Inari shrine|en|Inari shrine|WD=Q514480|lt=Inari Shrine}} is unknown.

== Shrine Buildings and Grounds ==
The grounds include the {{ill|Miyamado Inari Shrine|ja|海山道稲荷神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyamado Inari Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 洲崎濱宮神明神社}}, which surpasses the Shinmei Shrine in scale.

There are over 20 subsidiary {{ill|Inari shrine|en|Inari shrine|WD=Q514480|lt=Inari shrines}}, a {{ill|Tenjin shrine|ja|天神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenjin shrine|1=Tenjinsha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11443279}} housing Japan’s largest statue of {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}}, and various monuments such as the Pen Mound, Eyeglass Mound, and Fox Mound.

== Festivals and Events ==
* festival: October 3–5
* Inari Grand Festival: April 8–9
* Fox Wedding Ceremony: Held on {{ill|Setsubun|en|Setsubun|WD=Q746798|lt=Setsubun}} (February), this purification ritual involves a mock wedding procession. Participants representing the bride and groom wear fox masks, and a "bean-throwing" event is also held.

== location ==
1-62 Miyamado Town, {{ill|Yokkaichi|en|Yokkaichi|WD=Q26410|lt=Yokkaichi}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|en|Mie Prefecture|WD=Q128196|lt=Mie Prefecture}}, Japan

== Access ==
* A short walk from {{ill|Kintetsu Nagoya Line|en|Kintetsu Nagoya Line|WD=Q1056563|lt=Kintetsu Nagoya Line}}'s {{ill|Miyamado Station|en|Miyamado Station|WD=Q11558874|lt=Miyamado Station}}.

'''Suzaki Hamamiya Shinmei Shrine''' ({{ill|Suzaki Hamamiya Shinmei Shrine|ja|洲崎濱宮神明神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suzaki Hamamiya Shinmei Shrine|1=Miyamado Inari Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11556511}}, ''Suzaki Hamamiya Shinmei Jinja'') is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in the [[Shiobama District]], {{ill|Yokkaichi|en|Yokkaichi|WD=Q26410|lt=Yokkaichi|1=Yokkaichi}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|en|Mie Prefecture|WD=Q128196|lt=Mie Prefecture|1=Mie Prefecture}}, Japan. It is often collectively referred to as "Miyamado Shrine" due to the large presence of its {{ill|Inari shrine|en|Inari shrine|WD=Q514480|lt=Inari Shrine|1=Inari shrine}}.

== Overview ==
[[File:Miyamado-inari(Mie).JPG|thumb|Miyamado Inari Shrine]]
The shrine's name "濱" (''Hama'') is also written as "浜" or "濵."

The subordinate shrine, known as '''Miyamado Inari Shrine''' ({{ill|Miyamado Inari Shrine|ja|海山道稲荷神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyamado Inari Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 洲崎濱宮神明神社}}), is larger than the main Shinmei Shrine and is famous for the "Fox Wedding Ceremony" ({{ill|Kitsune no yomeiri|en|Kitsune no yomeiri|WD=Q11571371|lt=Fox Wedding|1=Kitsune no yomeiri}}). 

The grounds also include multiple Inari shrines, {{ill|Tenjinsha|lt=Tenjin shrines|ja|天神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenjinsha|qq=}}, and unique features such as Japan’s largest statue of {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}}.

== Enshrined Deities ==
=== Shinmei Shrine ===
{{Div col}}
* {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|WD=Q2564508|lt=Amenokoyane-no-Mikoto|ja|天児屋根命|1=Ame-no-Koyane|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天児屋命}}
* {{ill|Amaterasu|en|Amaterasu|WD=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|1=Amaterasu}}
* {{ill|Takemikazuchi|WD=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|建御雷命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takemikazuchi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タケミカヅチ|1=Takemikazuchi}}
* {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|en|Emperor Nintoku|WD=Q313119|lt=Emperor Nintoku|1=Emperor Nintoku}}
* {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}}
* {{ill|Ukanomitama|lt-Ukanomitama-no-Kami|ja|倉稲魂命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ}}
* {{ill|Oyamakui no Kami|WD=Q10935105|lt=Ōyamakui-no-Kami|ja|大山祇神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oyamakui no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ|1=Oyamakui no Kami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q386563}}
* {{ill|Kagu-tsuchi|lt=Kagutsuchi-no-Kami|ja|火之迦具土神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagu-tsuchi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to カグツチ}}
* {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}}
* {{ill|Kagu-tsuchi|lt=Ho-musubi-no-Kami|ja|火産霊神|1=Kagu-tsuchi|lt=Kagutsuchi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagu-tsuchi|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagutsuchi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to カグツチ}}
* {{ill|Ukemochi|en|Ukemochi|WD=Q2280323|lt=Ukemochi-no-Kami|1=Ukemochi}}
* {{ill|Yamatohime-no-mikoto|en|Yamatohime-no-mikoto|WD=Q4388732|lt=Yamatohime-no-Mikoto|1=Yamatohime-no-mikoto}}
{{Div col end}}

=== Inari Shrine ===
* {{ill|Ukanomitama|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Kami|ja|倉稲魂命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ|1=Ukanomitama}}
* {{ill|Ōkuninushi|lt=Ōkuninushi-no-Kami|ja|大己貴命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}
* {{ill|Ōta-no-Kami|ja|大田命|1=Ōta-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=太田命|4=fr|5=大田命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōta-no-Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōta-no-Mikoto|qq=}}
* {{ill|Ukemochi|en|Ukemochi|WD=Q2280323|lt=Ukemochi-no-Kami|1=Ukemochi}}
* {{ill|Ōmiya-no-Me|lt=Ōmiya-no-Me|lt=Ōmiyahime-no-Mikoto|lt=Ōmiya-no-Me-no-Kami|ja|大宮能売神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmiyahime-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオミヤノメ}}

== History ==
The shrine is traditionally said to have been founded in {{ill|876|en|876|WD=Q23815|lt=876|1=876}} (the 18th year of the {{ill|Zhenguan|en|Zhenguan|WD=Q3577278|lt=Jōgan era|1=Zhenguan}}). During the {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian period|1=Heian period}}, the area was coastal and formed a sandbar called "Suzaki." Thus, the shrine became known as "Hama-no-Miya" (Shrine of the Beach).

The exact date of the establishment of the {{ill|Inari shrine|en|Inari shrine|WD=Q514480|lt=Inari Shrine|1=Inari shrine}} is unknown.

== Shrine Buildings and Grounds ==
The grounds include the {{ill|Miyamado Inari Shrine|ja|海山道稲荷神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyamado Inari Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 洲崎濱宮神明神社}}, which surpasses the Shinmei Shrine in scale.

There are over 20 subsidiary {{ill|Inari shrine|en|Inari shrine|WD=Q514480|lt=Inari shrines|1=Inari shrine}}, a {{ill|Tenjin shrine|ja|天神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenjin shrine|1=Tenjinsha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11443279}} housing Japan’s largest statue of {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}}, and various monuments such as the Pen Mound, Eyeglass Mound, and Fox Mound.

== Festivals and Events ==
* festival: October 3–5
* Inari Grand Festival: April 8–9
* Fox Wedding Ceremony: Held on {{ill|Setsubun|en|Setsubun|WD=Q746798|lt=Setsubun|1=Setsubun}} (February), this purification ritual involves a mock wedding procession. Participants representing the bride and groom wear fox masks, and a "bean-throwing" event is also held.

== location ==
1-62 Miyamado Town, {{ill|Yokkaichi|en|Yokkaichi|WD=Q26410|lt=Yokkaichi|1=Yokkaichi}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|en|Mie Prefecture|WD=Q128196|lt=Mie Prefecture|1=Mie Prefecture}}, Japan

== Access ==
* A short walk from {{ill|Kintetsu Nagoya Line|en|Kintetsu Nagoya Line|WD=Q1056563|lt=Kintetsu Nagoya Line|1=Kintetsu Nagoya Line}}'s {{ill|Miyamado Station|en|Miyamado Station|WD=Q11558874|lt=Miyamado Station|1=Miyamado Station}}.

[[File:Miyamado-inari(Mie).JPG|thumb|Miyamado Inari Shrine]]
The shrine's name "濱" (''Hama'') is also written as "浜" or "濵."

The subordinate shrine, known as '''Miyamado Inari Shrine''' ({{ill|Miyamado Inari Shrine|ja|海山道稲荷神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyamado Inari Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 洲崎濱宮神明神社}}), is larger than the main Shinmei Shrine and is famous for the "Fox Wedding Ceremony" ({{ill|Kitsune no yomeiri|en|Kitsune no yomeiri|WD=Q11571371|lt=Fox Wedding|1=Kitsune no yomeiri}}). 

The grounds also include multiple Inari shrines, {{ill|Tenjinsha|lt=Tenjin shrines|ja|天神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenjinsha|qq=}}, and unique features such as Japan’s largest statue of {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}}.

== Enshrined Deities ==
=== Shinmei Shrine ===
{{Div col}}
* {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|WD=Q2564508|lt=Amenokoyane-no-Mikoto|ja|天児屋根命|1=Ame-no-Koyane|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天児屋命}}
* {{ill|Amaterasu|en|Amaterasu|WD=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|1=Amaterasu}}
* {{ill|Takemikazuchi|WD=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|建御雷命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takemikazuchi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タケミカヅチ|1=Takemikazuchi}}
* {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|en|Emperor Nintoku|WD=Q313119|lt=Emperor Nintoku|1=Emperor Nintoku}}
* {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}}
* {{ill|Ukanomitama|lt-Ukanomitama-no-Kami|ja|倉稲魂命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ}}
* {{ill|Oyamakui no Kami|WD=Q10935105|lt=Ōyamakui-no-Kami|ja|大山祇神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oyamakui no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ|1=Oyamakui no Kami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q386563}}
* {{ill|Kagu-tsuchi|lt=Kagutsuchi-no-Kami|ja|火之迦具土神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagu-tsuchi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to カグツチ}}
* {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}}
* {{ill|Kagu-tsuchi|lt=Ho-musubi-no-Kami|ja|火産霊神|1=Kagu-tsuchi|lt=Kagutsuchi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagu-tsuchi|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagutsuchi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to カグツチ}}
* {{ill|Ukemochi|en|Ukemochi|WD=Q2280323|lt=Ukemochi-no-Kami|1=Ukemochi}}
* {{ill|Yamatohime-no-mikoto|en|Yamatohime-no-mikoto|WD=Q4388732|lt=Yamatohime-no-Mikoto|1=Yamatohime-no-mikoto}}
{{Div col end}}

=== Inari Shrine ===
* {{ill|Ukanomitama|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Kami|ja|倉稲魂命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ|1=Ukanomitama}}
* {{ill|Ōkuninushi|lt=Ōkuninushi-no-Kami|ja|大己貴命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}
* {{ill|Ōta-no-Kami|ja|大田命|1=Ōta-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=太田命|4=fr|5=大田命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōta-no-Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōta-no-Mikoto|qq=}}
* {{ill|Ukemochi|en|Ukemochi|WD=Q2280323|lt=Ukemochi-no-Kami|1=Ukemochi}}
* {{ill|Ōmiya-no-Me|lt=Ōmiya-no-Me|lt=Ōmiyahime-no-Mikoto|lt=Ōmiya-no-Me-no-Kami|ja|大宮能売神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmiyahime-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオミヤノメ}}

== History ==
The shrine is traditionally said to have been founded in {{ill|876|en|876|WD=Q23815|lt=876|1=876}} (the 18th year of the {{ill|Zhenguan|en|Zhenguan|WD=Q3577278|lt=Jōgan era|1=Zhenguan}}). During the {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian period|1=Heian period}}, the area was coastal and formed a sandbar called "Suzaki." Thus, the shrine became known as "Hama-no-Miya" (Shrine of the Beach).

The exact date of the establishment of the {{ill|Inari shrine|en|Inari shrine|WD=Q514480|lt=Inari Shrine|1=Inari shrine}} is unknown.

== Shrine Buildings and Grounds ==
The grounds include the {{ill|Miyamado Inari Shrine|ja|海山道稲荷神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyamado Inari Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 洲崎濱宮神明神社}}, which surpasses the Shinmei Shrine in scale.

There are over 20 subsidiary {{ill|Inari shrine|en|Inari shrine|WD=Q514480|lt=Inari shrines|1=Inari shrine}}, a {{ill|Tenjin shrine|ja|天神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenjin shrine|1=Tenjinsha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11443279}} housing Japan’s largest statue of {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}}, and various monuments such as the Pen Mound, Eyeglass Mound, and Fox Mound.

== Festivals and Events ==
* festival: October 3–5
* Inari Grand Festival: April 8–9
* Fox Wedding Ceremony: Held on {{ill|Setsubun|en|Setsubun|WD=Q746798|lt=Setsubun|1=Setsubun}} (February), this purification ritual involves a mock wedding procession. Participants representing the bride and groom wear fox masks, and a "bean-throwing" event is also held.

== location ==
1-62 Miyamado Town, {{ill|Yokkaichi|en|Yokkaichi|WD=Q26410|lt=Yokkaichi|1=Yokkaichi}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|en|Mie Prefecture|WD=Q128196|lt=Mie Prefecture|1=Mie Prefecture}}, Japan

== Access ==
* A short walk from {{ill|Kintetsu Nagoya Line|en|Kintetsu Nagoya Line|WD=Q1056563|lt=Kintetsu Nagoya Line|1=Kintetsu Nagoya Line}}'s {{ill|Miyamado Station|en|Miyamado Station|WD=Q11558874|lt=Miyamado Station|1=Miyamado Station}}.

=== Inari Shrine ===
* {{ill|Ukanomitama|qid=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Kami|ja|倉稲魂命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ|1=Ukanomitama}}

== History ==
The shrine is traditionally said to have been founded in {{ill|876|en|876|qid=Q23815|lt=876|1=876}} (the 18th year of the {{ill|Zhenguan|en|Zhenguan|qid=Q3577278|lt=Jōgan era|1=Zhenguan}}). During the {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|qid=Q193292|lt=Heian period|1=Heian period}}, the area was coastal and formed a sandbar called "Suzaki." Thus, the shrine became known as "Hama-no-Miya" (Shrine of the Beach).

The exact date of the establishment of the {{ill|Inari shrine|en|Inari shrine|qid=Q514480|lt=Inari Shrine|1=Inari shrine}} is unknown.

== Shrine Buildings and Grounds ==
The grounds include the {{ill|Miyamado Inari Shrine|ja|海山道稲荷神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyamado Inari Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 洲崎濱宮神明神社}}, which surpasses the Shinmei Shrine in scale.

There are over 20 subsidiary {{ill|Inari shrine|en|Inari shrine|qid=Q514480|lt=Inari shrines|1=Inari shrine}}, a {{ill|Tenjin shrine|ja|天神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenjin shrine|1=Tenjinsha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11443279}} housing Japan’s largest statue of {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q382005}}, and various monuments such as the Pen Mound, Eyeglass Mound, and Fox Mound.

== Festivals and Events ==
* festival: October 3–5
* Inari Grand Festival: April 8–9
* Fox Wedding Ceremony: Held on {{ill|Setsubun|en|Setsubun|qid=Q746798|lt=Setsubun|1=Setsubun}} (February), this purification ritual involves a mock wedding procession. Participants representing the bride and groom wear fox masks, and a "bean-throwing" event is also held.

== location ==
1-62 Miyamado Town, {{ill|Yokkaichi|en|Yokkaichi|qid=Q26410|lt=Yokkaichi|1=Yokkaichi}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|en|Mie Prefecture|qid=Q128196|lt=Mie Prefecture|1=Mie Prefecture}}, Japan

== Access ==
* A short walk from {{ill|Kintetsu Nagoya Line|en|Kintetsu Nagoya Line|qid=Q1056563|lt=Kintetsu Nagoya Line|1=Kintetsu Nagoya Line}}'s {{ill|Miyamado Station|en|Miyamado Station|qid=Q11558874|lt=Miyamado Station|1=Miyamado Station}}.
